name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
description:
AED is a premedical honor society dedicated to preparing and exposing interested students to various health-care disciplines including Medical, Dental, PA, PT and OT. The mission of the Society is to encourage and recognize excellence in pre-healthcare scholarship; to stimulate an appreciation of the importance of pre-healthcare education; to promote communication between healthcare students and educators; to provide a forum for students with common interests; and to use its resources to benefit health organizations, charities and the community. The Clemson Chapter of AED hosts meetings throughout the year with healthcare professionals, Kaplan and the Princeton Review representatives, clinical workshops, prep for entrance exams and applications and is committed to serving the community through various service projects.

To join AED, simply request to join our TigerQuest! From there, you will be added to our email list and receive our weekly newsletter, the Stethoscope, where you will find information about paying your one-time member dues and about all of our meetings and events. To be considered a member in good standing, earn 6 points by attending any of our events or completing any other point opportunity we send out. 

Email clemsonaed@gmail.com with any questions!
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta (AED) is designed for distinguished students who want to pursue a career in one of the health fields. AED hosts meetings in which physicians, dentists, and other health professionals come to speak to students on various topics.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description: Alpha Kappa Psi is a co-educational professional business fraternity aimed to foster personal and professional growth within its members, open to ALL majors at Clemson University.
summary: Co-Educational Professional Business Fraternity

name: Alpha Lambda Delta
description: Alpha Lambda Delta is a National Honor Society for first-year college students. Invitations for membership are extended to freshmen who excel academically, earning a position in the top 20% of their class. Clemson's chapter works to serve the local community while promoting academic success and personal development.
summary: The National Honor Society that recognizes first-year academic success at Clemson University.

name: Alpha Omega Epsilon
description: The sisters of Alpha Omega Epsilon promote ideals and objectives that hope to further the advancement of female engineers and technical scientists, while simultaneously developing lifelong friendships. Alpha Omega Epsilon strives for scholarship and academic achievement. The sisters create a friendly and warm atmosphere where integrity, character, and self-confidence can flourish. The sorority hopes to enlighten female engineers and technical scientists to the career opportunities available to them. Alpha Omega Epsilon is open to women also interested in STEM. Alpha Omega Epsilon encourages ties with other Greek and engineering organizations, fosters leadership at the chapter and international levels, and provides networking opportunities for its members.
summary: Alpha Omega Epsilon is a professional and social sorority for women in engineering and technical science that promotes friendship, leadership, and professionalism to all members of the organization, the community, and our professions.

name: Alpha Phi Sorority
description: Alpha Phi is a sisterhood of outstanding women supporting one another in lifelong achievement. The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 160 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members. Sisters share a commitment to excellence and a strong desire to help one another and their communities. Alpha Phis are leaders, scholars, contributors and lifetime members of a sisterhood that values these traits. Alpha Phi assists college women in making informed choices to reach their highest potential. Alpha Phi enhances and promotes each member's development and learning by focusing on sisterhood, service, scholarship, leadership, loyalty and character development. The innovative leadership and organizational practices of Alpha Phi's Founders in 1872 set the spirit for the Alpha Phi of today...a sisterhood that values the past but looks forward to the progress offered by the future.
summary: Alpha Phi is a chapter of collegiate women bettering the community through hosting and participating in philanthropy and community service events.

name: Alpha Sigma Phi
description: to Better the Man, through the creation and perpetuation of brotherhood founded upon the values of character: Silence, Charity, Purity, Honor, Patriotism.
summary: to Better the Man, through the creation and perpetuation of brotherhood founded upon the values of character: Silence, Charity, Purity, Honor, Patriotism.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description: AIChE is an organization dedicated to promoting the chemical engineering field through social, community, and professional activities. The Clemson chapter seeks to connect students to professionals to bridge the gap between college and industry.
summary: AIChE is an organization dedicated to promoting the chemical engineering field through social, community, and professional activities. AIChE establishes business contacts to help bridge the gap between college and industry.

name: American Medical Student Association
description: The American Medical Student Association (AMSA), with a half-century history of medical student activism, is the oldest and largest independent association of physicians-in-training in the United States. Today, AMSA is a student-governed, national organization committed to representing the concerns of physicians-in-training. AMSA members are medical students, premedical students, interns, residents, and practicing physicians. Founded in 1950, AMSA continues its commitment to improving medical training and the nation’s health. As a part of Clemson's premedical AMSA chapter, you will join a community of students who share your interests, and obtain relevant experience and opportunities to jumpstart your future career.
summary: AMSA

name: American Sign Language Club
description: The Clemson American Sign Language (ASL) Club is an independent student organization that brings students together to learn American Sign Language and celebrate Deaf culture with local Deaf community members. The club offers a variety of social, cultural, and educational activities for its members. Beginner and experience signers alike actively improve their ASL skills, learn more about the ASL community's culture, have fun, and make new friends.
summary: Bringing together students interested in both ASL and interacting with the Deaf community.  We have weekly events to socialize, learn, and have fun together.  Come join us!  No ASL experience necessary.

name: Animal and Veterinary Science Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to create, develop, and foster interaction between graduate students, undergraduate students, staff, and faculty in the Department of Animal and Veterinary Science, to disseminate information to graduates students in Animal and Veterinary Science, and to represent the interests of graduate students in Animal and Veterinary Science to the Administration, and the University at-large, and to represent Clemson University, the Department of Animal and Veterinary Science the graduates students in Animal and Veterinary Science at national meetings of Animal and Veterinary Science and related fields.
summary: Animal and Veterinary Science graduate students with a passion for improving animal welfare, and make our food supply as safe and efficient as possible!

name: Anime and Manga Club
description:
We meet once a week on Thursday nights to watch anime or enjoy social events. Some of the shows we watch are voted for by members. We're excited to host multiple fun events to get people together and enjoy activities as a community on and off campus. If you would like to be added to our discord server, please join the club and access the link in our documents section!

Spring 2025 semester: We are meeting every Thursday night from 7:00-9:00PM in Hardin 233.
summary: We create a place in which Clemson students can come together and meet others with similar interests in anime and manga.

name: Arnold Air Society
description:
Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary, service military organization with three main objectives:

1) develop interpersonal relationships in Air Force ROTC

2) provide opportunities to develop leadership, professionalism, organizational skills, and more

3) participate in community service.
summary: Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary, service organizations advocating the support of aerospace power.

name: Asian Student Association
description:
The Asian Student Association (ASA) is a multicultural organization established to serve the Clemson community in a spirit of unity and diversity. In addition to exploring different cultures, we aim to bring people together, foster friendships, create a sense of family/a home-away-from-home for our members.

Follow our Instagram page at https://www.instagram.com/clemson_asa/ !

Follow our Facebook page at https://www.facebook.com/ClemsonASA !
summary: The Clemson Asian Student Association is a student led organization dedicated to the advancement of Asian/Asian American culture in and around Clemson University and Upstate South Carolina.

name: Clemson University ACM Student Chapter
description: • ACM is a global scientific and educational organization dedicated to advancing the art, science, engineering, and application of computing, serving both professional and public interests by fostering the open exchange of information and by promoting the highest professional and ethical standards. The Clemson Chapter of the Association for Computing Machinery aims to promote community, connection, and collaboration for those interested in computing and support the professional growth of its members by providing opportunities for learning, career development, and professional networking.
summary: The professional organization for Computer Science.

name: Clemson Swing Club
description: Clemson Swing Club offers weekly dances and beginner lessons. Meetings are held Monday nights in front of Cooper library or in Sirrine Hall.
summary: Clemson Swing focuses on bringing swing dancing and history to the Clemson community.

name: Baptist Collegiate Ministry
description:
Tuesdays at 7pm at the BCM Building

LOVE GOD. LOVE PEOPLE.

It's that simple. 

We are all part of God's story as we develop students into LEADERS in the KINGDOM OF GOD. Those willing to be influencers in the way of JESUS. We believe that we are all created by God with immeasurable value, are desperately in need of a savior, and that we are all responsible to make a difference in the world around us. 

We'd love for you to be part of the story.
summary: A community of college students at Clemson University sharing life and following Jesus together. Love God. Love people. Tuesdays 7pm! clemsonbcm.org

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description:
• Eligibility Requirements:

    • Declared major of Accounting, Financial Management, or Information Systems
    • 6 hours of accounting courses completed at Clemson (including ACCT 3110).  Transfer credits do not qualify.
    • 3.0 Overall GPA
    • 3.2 Accounting GPA
    • Minimum of 3 remaining semesters at Clemson

    BAP is an honorary organization for Accounting and Finance students and professionals. BAP promotes the study and practice of accounting, finance and information systems; providing opportunities for self-development and service

    The primary objective of Beta Alpha Psi is to encourage and give recognition to scholastic and professional excellence in the business information field. This includes: promoting the study and practice of accounting, finance, and information systems, providing opportunities for self-development, service, and association among members and practicing professionals, and encouraging a sense of ethical, social, and public responsibility.
summary: BAP is an honorary organization for Accounting and Finance students and professionals. BAP promotes the study and practice of accounting, finance and information systems; providing opportunities for self-development and service

name: Beta Theta Pi
description:
Founded in 1839, Beta Theta Pi members uphold the fraternity's ideals of lifelong friendship, dedication to leadership, devotion to academic excellence, commitment to community service and high standards of conduct as declared by our Men of Principle Initiative.

 

Delta Nu is a nationally recognized Beta Theta Pi chapter. Our chapter continues to set the bar of success for all Clemson University fraternities & students, from the success of our annual philanthropy "Beta Lei" & winning the Homecoming Float Competition to having the best grades of any fraternity on campus in both 2012 & 2013. Our members have been involved in many aspects of campus leadership & organizations including: President’s Cabinet, Blue Key, Omicron Delta Kappa, Order of Omega, Tiger Brotherhood, Student Senate, Student Alumni Council, IPTAY Student Advisory Board, Phi Kappa Phi Honor Society, and Student Judicial Board. Leadership positions include Norris Medal award winners, Student Body Presidents & Vice Presidents, Student Body Treasurers, Greek Men of the Year, Tigerama Directors, and IFC Presidents.
summary: Founded in 1839, Beta Theta Pi members uphold the fraternity's ideals of lifelong friendship, dedication to leadership, devotion to academic excellence, commitment to community service and high standards of conduct.

name: Genetics and Biochemistry Club
description: Organization for undergraduate students interested in the fields of Biochemistry and/or Genetics. Undergraduates are provided with unparalleled experiences through faculty guest speakers, field trips, and networking with other students pursuing research. The club hosts an annual field trip over fall break to our nation's capital to visit research laboratories at the National Institute of Health's campus and other private biotechnology/pharmaceutical laboratories. The G&B Club also hosts spring trips to private research facilities in Greenwood, Greenville, and Pendleton.
summary: Organization for undergraduate students interested in the fields of Biochemistry and/or Genetics. Undergraduates are introduced to these professional fields by faculty guest speakers, field trips, and networking with other students pursuing research.

name: Biological Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: The Clemson University Department of Biological Sciences Graduate Student Association (BioSci GSA) is a free organization composed of all students actively pursuing M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in biological science, environmental toxicology, and microbiology. BioSci GSA is led by graduate students in the Department of Biological Sciences. Our mission is to maintain a strong community by engaging in student advocacy, facilitating social activities, promoting professional development and networking opportunities, performing community service, and fundraising for student resources, events, and travel grants.
summary: The Clemson University Department of Biological Sciences Graduate Student Association (BioSci GSA) is a free organization composed of all students actively pursuing M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in biological science, environmental toxicology, and microbiology.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
description: Clemson University's Black Graduate Student Association exists to foster a supportive environment for graduate students that promotes the holistic development and creates opportunities for upward mobility that will empower students to achieve academically and professionally.
summary: The premier organization advancing cultural, academic, and professional interests of graduate students of African descent. Our goal is to provide a network of critical resources to ensure the academic success of emerging Black Scholars and professionals.

name: Clemson Black Student Union
description: The mission of the Black Student Union is to PROMOTE, CELEBRATE, and EMBRACE Black Culture on Clemson University's campus while instilling a sense of pride in the community. For more information about our organization, visit @ClemsonBSU on Instagram.
summary: The mission of the Black Student Union is to PROMOTE, CELEBRATE, and EMBRACE Black Culture on Clemson University's campus while instilling a sense of pride in the community. For more information about our organization, visit @ClemsonBSU on Instagram!

name: Clemson University Block and Bridle
description: Founded in 1937, the Clemson University Block and Bridle Club holds a rich tradition of livestock appreciation and fellowship. Our members come from an array of backgrounds, but all are brought together with common interests in agriculture and animal science. Major activities include both social and educational events. Outstanding speakers provide students with information about present and future activities in Animal Agriculture.The highlights for the year are the Little North American Showmanship Contest and the annual banquet with the election of outstanding leaders to honorary membership and the distribution of awards earned and won for outstanding activities by members. The B&B Club has sponsored the livestock, meats and horse judging teams. Projects to earn money for all club functions, include BBQ sales, Bulls and BBQ, assisting with livestock sales and other industry events.
summary: Block and Bridle is the largest agricultural club on campus that hosts industry speakers, participates in community service, and organizes events such as CAFLS Formal, Bulls and BBQ, and many more. We welcome students from all majors!

name: Blue Key Honor Society
description: Blue Key is an honor society consisting of individuals that exhibit exemplary scholarship, leadership, service, and character. The organization seeks to serve Clemson University, its students, faculty, staff, and community through events such as Tigerama, The Pursuit, and Phone-a-thon.
summary: Blue Key is an honor society consisting of individuals that exhibit exemplary scholarship, character, leadership, and service. The organization seeks to serve Clemson University, its students, faculty, staff, and community.

name: Clemson Debate Society
description:
The Clemson Debate society competes in debate tournaments across the country and across the world. Members may also attend debate camps (both domestic and international) or perform in public debates for the student body. Joining this team is a fantastic opportunity to broaden your perspectives and hone your critical thinking, persuasion, and communication skills.

Absolutely no experience is required, there are no dues / cost to joining, and you may qualify to receive scholarships covering the cost of travel.

For more information, send our coach Lindsey Dixon and email ldixon2@clemson.edu
summary: The Clemson Debate Society is an incredible opportunity to develop communication and argumentation skills. We travel and compete on an international scale. There are no dues and anyone is welcome to join or come see what we are all about.

name: Campus Outreach
description: The vision of Campus Outreach is "Glorifying God by Building Laborers on the Campus for the Lost World." We seek to build laborers with a commitment to Jesus and His Great Commission through evangelism and discipleship. We are passionately reaching students with the gospel and training them to become mature followers of Christ. Our desire is to see students leave college equipped to labor in the harvest field for the glory of God. We meet every Wednesday night and also have many events throughout the semester.
summary: An on-campus ministry with the desire to meet students, build relationships, and have fun! We meet every Wednesday night and also have many events throughout the semester.

name: Central Spirit
description:
Central Spirit is the premier athletics-oriented organization on campus with many excellent benefits. We are more than just football fans. We support baseball, basketball, football, golf, gymnastics, lacrosse, rowing, soccer, softball, tennis, track and field, volleyball, and ALL Clemson sports. We paint up, wave and run flags on the field, cheer in student sections, and travel to away games. We also put on the First Friday parade, organize the Homecoming float build, sell t-shirts, pass out stickers, exchange $2 bills, and participate in many social and service events! Does that sound like you? Follow us on social media and join us!

Instagram and Twitter: @cucentralspirit

Facebook and YouTube: Central Spirit
summary: Central Spirit backs the Tigers at every sporting event and maintains some of Clemson's greatest traditions, such as the First Friday parade and the Homecoming Build

name: Chemical Engineering Graduate Student Organization
description: CEGSO promotes social interaction between Chemical Engineering graduate students and provides a forum for communication between the Chemical Engineering graduate student body, the Chemical Engineering departmental faculty and staff, and Clemson's Graduate Student Government.
summary: CEGSO promotes social interaction between Chemical Engineering (chemE) graduate students and provides a forum for communication between the chemE graduate student body, departmental faculty and staff, and Clemson's Graduate Student Government.

name: Chi Alpha Christian Fellowship
description: We are a student organization and a campus ministry. Our main gathering is Sundays at 7pm in the David Peebles Room in the Hendrix Student Center (At the XA House for Summer). In addition we hold regular small groups throughout the week. Join us at the XA House, 177 Old Greenville Hwy. Clemson, SC 29630, across from the lower intramural fields to hang out and for other fun events. We look forward to meeting you!
summary:
Chi Alpha (XA) is a campus ministry & discipleship movement that is bringing the Kingdom of God to local University. 

The name Chi Alpha comes from the Greek which reminds us to be Christou Apostoloi – "Christ's Ambassadors".

name: Chi Omega
description: In 1895, Chi Omega was founded upon the principles of friendship, high standards of personnel, sincere learning and credible scholarship, participation in campus activities, career development, and community service. Chi Omega continues to strive to be the premier women's organization in the 21st Century. Chi Omega not only offers the opportunity for our members to share a bond of sisterhood and friendship that will last a lifetime, we also seek to serve the community and the country as a united group of women, fostering the development of future leaders and empowering women everywhere to make a difference.
summary: An organization of young women dedicated to high purpose and helpfulness.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
description: Our organization mainly focuses to provide support for Chinese students and professionals at Clemson University. We try our best to provide resources to help make their transition to Clemson smoother. We also host events to celebrate mid-Autumn festival and Chinese New Year to make people feel at home, as well as sharing our culture and experiences with other students here at Clemson. We want to be able to share our diverse background with others and support and encourage each other along the way.
summary: Our organization mainly focuses to provide support for Chinese students and professionals at Clemson University. We try our best to provide resources to help make their transition to Clemson smoother.

name: The Chronicle Magazine
description:
Established in 1897, the Chronicle is Clemson University's first and only student art and literary magazine. Twice a year, our staff promotes, produces, and distributes a new issue representing the best artistic and literary work that Clemson students have to offer. We have many committees that work together to create the magazine including creative direction, layout, promotions/events, copy-editing, art submissions, literary submissions, merchandise, web, and business. From art and photography, to poems, short stories, essays, audio and visual submissions, we accept a wide variety of submissions every semester, many of which are selected to appear in the magazine.

 

We meet weekly on Sundays at 7 p.m. in Room 406 of Sirrine Hall!
summary: We are Clemson's student-run arts and literary magazine! We welcome students from all majors to come join us as we work to design, distribute, and promote our magazine and connect with the artistic community here at Clemson!

name: Clemson All-In Recovery
description:
Welcome to Clemson All-In Recovery (formerly Clemson & Sober)
 
CAIR is a student organization aiming to support students interested in recovery, allies of recovery, and/or anyone affected by addiction. The goal is to create a safe and healthy peer support environment to discuss problems, and find solutions. 
 
Right now our primary purpose is to make space for recovery based meetings.  Fellowship, friendship, and support extend beyond the limits of our meeting times, though, because we also organize social activities for and among ourselves.  
 
 
Don't be shy if you have questions,  we are always happy to help!
summary: Clemson All-In Recovery is a student organization, founded and run by students, to provide a community and support for all students seeking recovery and sobriety.

name: Clemson Academic Team
description: This team was created to connect a diverse group of academically motivated students to promote trivia, friendly intercollegiate competition, and curiosity. The main goal of Clemson's Academic Team is to cultivate this group into a regionally and nationally competitive team that exemplifies Clemson’s academic prowess. We host a tournament to encourage high schoolers to get involved in college quizbowl, and go to college tournaments throughout the year to compete. We will have optional practices multiple times a week, and please feel free to stop by at any of our practices, which are 7:00 to 9:00 pm in Poole Hall room F150, Mondays and Wednesdays. Please join our Discord if you would like to become a member (link below). If you have any questions, feel free to email our President, Asheton Wilbanks, at the email below.
summary: The Clemson Academic Team is a student organization that is dedicated to fielding a diverse competitive quiz bowl team, engaging in academic/trivia-related activities, and traveling throughout the region to participate in competitions.

name: Clemson Baja SAE
description: Baja SAE is a collegiate design competition sponsored by the Society of Automotive Engineers in which students compete in an off-road challenge. Students must design, build, test, promote, and race a rugged off-road vehicle. Team members learn all the aspects of a professional race team including management of workforce, business promoting and fund raising, hands-on fabrication and machining, testing, and racing. Students of all classes, majors, and skill levels can join Baja SAE. We pride ourselves on teaching new members and improving each team members' abilities. To join one must simply come out to our shop during our weekly meetings.
summary: Design, Build, Test, Promote, and Race a rugged off-road vehicle in long endurance races against schools from around the world.

name: Clemson Boxing
description:
Clemson Boxing Club

Interested in learning some boxing technique, stay in shape, and meet some great people? In the Clemson Boxing Club, we take in members of ALL skill level and history. Training consists of jump roping, shadow boxing, working on combos and defense on different kinds of punching bags, conditioning, and sparring. 

INTERESTED?

1. Follow our Instagram @clemsonclub.boxing and check out the highlights for information.

Club Summary: Info Sheet

INFO PAGE / CLUB SUMMARY ^^

           a) Specific questions, email president Jennifer Miller @clemsonboxing@clemson.edu or DM the instagram account.

2. TO JOIN, attend your first session free --> THEN we'll get you signed up. Bring athletic wear and shoes and a water bottle.
summary: Clemson Boxing is an ISO offering boxing instruction and experience, along with a respectful community of disciplined students looking to stay active.

name: Clemson Ceramics Association
description: Welcome to the Clemson Ceramics Association! We're here to provide a welcoming environment with the intention of bringing students together to learn a little bit about clay, and how to create some beautiful pottery and ceramics pieces from some of our own ceramic students. The projects we do vary from theme to technique, and are typically based off of group suggestions and what people want to learn.
summary: A welcoming environment to gather and learn a little bit about clay and making from some of our ceramic students! Projects vary from theme to technique and can be based off of group suggestions and what people want to learn!

name: Clemson College Democrats
description:
Clemson College Democrats is a coalition of students who share similar liberal views on social justice and economic opportunity. CCD is founded upon the three core practices of community organizing: respect, empower, include. Through commitment to these values and a common purpose, CCD aims to mobilize student volunteers to take part in securing positive change for America.

We hold meetings the 1st and 3rd Tuesday of every month at 7PM, in Brackett 120. Follow our Instagram and join our group me to stay up to date!
summary: Clemson College Democrats is a coalition of students who share similar liberal views on social justice and economic opportunity.

name: Tiger Christian Life
description: A campus ministry devoted to training students in the gospel, providing Christian fellowship and loving every person because they are created by God.
summary: A campus ministry devoted to training students in the gospel, providing Christian fellowship and loving every person because they are created by God.

name: Clemson Dancers
description:
Welcome to Clemson Dancers!

Follow us on Instagram @clemsondancers

WELCOME NEW AND RETURNING DANCERS!

Clemson Dancers is a student-run recreational dance organization that offers student-taught dance classes in on-campus spaces during the evenings on Mondays, Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. We teach a variety of dance styles for all levels of dancers and host an annual Showcase recital in the spring.

Your level of involvement in Clemson Dancers is at your discretion, as dues are paid on a semester basis and cover as many classes as you want to take. In the fall, Clemson Dancers operates on a rotating schedule, with class schedules changing weekly. Teachers teach a new dance combination every week. In the spring, the schedule remains the same for the entire semester, and teachers teach one dance that is performed in our Showcase at the end of the semester.
summary: Clemson Dancers is a student-run dance organization that offers student-taught dance classes Mondays through Thursdays in on-campus spaces. We teach a variety of dance styles for all levels and host an annual Showcase recital in the spring.

name: Clemson Dholna
description: We are Clemson's Undergraduate Bollywood fusion dance team! We perform at various venues and events on and off campus, including Diwali programs, International Festival, Dance Marathons, basketball games, and more. Please reach out to one of our captains or on Instagram @clemsondholna if you would like to join our team or have us perform at your event!
summary: Clemson Dholna is Clemson's one and only undergraduate Bollywood fusion dance team! Combining various Indian and American genres and styles of dance, we look to entertain and share the Indian culture.

name: Clemson Engineers for Developing Communities
description:
At Clemson Engineers for Developing Communities, our mission is to work with communities to develop sustainable solutions that improve the quality of life through interdisciplinary student-led initiatives that embody our core values in partnership with organizations whose mission and values align with ours.

Core Values:

Accountability – We recognize and accept responsibility for our actions and their outcomes while honoring obligations, expectations, and standards of ethical conduct.

Commitment – We acknowledge our generation’s responsibility as global leaders to holistically apply interdisciplinary solutions towards social, environmental, and economic issues to enhance future lives.

Service – We carry out our moral obligations to improve the safety, health, and well-being of the human race, effectively advancing the standard of living in developing communities.

We only take new students at the beginning of each semester. To join, simply enroll in ECAS 1900. If you would like to learn more about us and our work, please email us at cedc@clemson.edu, and we'll provide you with the necessary instructions.
summary: Clemson Engineers for Developing Communities (CEDC) is a student-led service-learning organization, experiential-learning organization, and research opportunity. We are "serving the developing world, developing those who serve."

name: Clemson Esports
description:
Our Story 

Clemson Esports started out as a small League of Legends club on campus. We have since grown from a hand full of members to several hundred over the course of a couple of years. We have gone on to field many new games and help expand our roster. Our players play in front of thousands of viewers on stages around the country. Our club has become a home for all gamers on campus, competitive and casual alike. If you love Esports and gaming and want to make friends with fellow students with the same interests, then join the club today.    

Events 

Each year we hold several gaming events for the Clemson community. These events consist of tournaments, prizes, giveaways, and also offer a great way to make new friends on campus. We also participate in smaller events such as viewing parties and gaming nights.

Contact Us!
Discord:https://discord.gg/2Vyp6stsnA
Twitter: https://twitter.com/ClemsonEsports
Twitch: http://twitch.tv/Clemson_Esports 
Instagram: http://instagram.com/ClemsonEsports
Youtube: https://goo.gl/XVW9MQ
Email: esports@clemson.edu
summary: Clemson Esports is home to all gamers, competitive and casual alike. Our competitive players compete around the country for prizes and scholarships.

name: Clemson Photography
description:
Clemson Photography is a collaborative group of photographers of all skill levels and interests! We offer several opportunities for you to expand your horizons in photography, including sports photography, portraits, landscapes, architecture, and many events here on campus. We want you to reach your full potential with whatever camera you have, whether that’s a DSLR, your friend’s DSLR, a point-and-shoot, or a smartphone. It’s never too late in the semester to come to a meeting for the first time, and you can come to as many or as few events as you like. Find us on Instagram at @clemson_photography

We look forward to meeting you!
summary: Clemson Photography provides an opportunity for students to expand their knowledge and love of photography. Join today!

name: Clemson Society for Industrial-Organizational Psychology
description: The Clemson Society for Industrial-Organizational Psychology is a professional organization for students in Clemson's I-O Psychology graduate program as well as for interested graduate and undergraduate students in affiliated departments, and members of the general community.
summary: The Clemson Society for Industrial-Organizational Psychology is a professional student organization for Clemson's I-O Psychology graduate program as well as for interested graduate and undergraduate students.

name: Clemson American Nuclear Society Student Chapter
description: Club promoting nuclear outreach and awareness.
summary: Club promoting nuclear outreach and awareness.

name: Clemson University College of Arts and Humanities Ambassadors
description: This organization is for the Student Ambassadors representing the various majors in the College of Arts and Humanities. Through this organization, the Ambassadors can communicate more efficiently, organize various events for the college, and request funding for these events. CAH majors include English, History, Global Black Studies, Language and International Business, Language and International Health, Modern Languages, Performing Arts, Philosophy, Religious Studies, Women's Leadership, and World Cinema.
summary: This organization is for the Ambassadors that represent the various majors in the College of Arts and Humanities.

name: Nuclear Discussion Collective
description:
Formerly the Clemson University Health Physics Society (CUPHS), we are now called Nuclear Discussion Collective (Nuclear DisCo)! We meet on a bi-weekly basis to discuss current environmental health physics, radioecology and radiochemistry topics. Although this is primarily a graduate student group, all are welcome. Please email the current President if you are interested in joining (as of January 2026: Matthew Riss, mriss@clemson.edu).

Although this organization has members who are Clemson University students and may have University
employees associated or engaged in its activities and affairs, the organization is not a part of or an
agency of the University. It is a separate and independent organization which is responsible for and
manages its own activities and affairs. The University does not direct, supervise, or control the
organization and is not responsible for the organization’s contracts, acts, or omissions.
summary: Student group featuring talks on environmental health physics, radioecology, and environmental radiochemistry with a focus on the safe handling and use of radioactive materials. This group is primarily graduate students, but is open to all!

name: Clemson IEEE Student Branch
description:
We are Clemson’s student organization for current and prospective members of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, the world’s largest technical professional organization for the advancement of technology. We provide an on-campus connection between Clemson students, IEEE, and industry professionals through tech talks, competitions, and networking opportunities. We also host fun events like the ECE Cookout to strengthen our Clemson ECE community.

Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/ZYEjp8H2jD
summary: We are Clemson’s student organization for current and prospective members of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, the world’s largest technical professional organization for the advancement of technology.

name: Clemson Iranian Students Organization
description:
CISO is a non-profit, non-political, and non-religious organization aiming to promote Persian culture/customs, diversity, and inclusivity. CISO also seeks to bring Iranian students, faculties, and families closer together and create stronger bonds within the Iranian community. To preserve our ancient Persian culture and heritage, different events, celebrations, and activities will be held throughout the year. Please follow CISO and get involved using the following contact info.

Email: CISO@clemson.edu

Telegram Channel: t.me/WikiClemson
Exec. board Teleg. ID: t.me/HeyCISO
Instagram ID: CISOboard
summary: CISO is a non-profit organization aiming to promote Persian culture/customs, diversity, and inclusivity. CISO also seeks to bring Iranian students, faculties, and families closer together and create stronger bonds within the Iranian community.

name: Clemson Magic: The Gathering
description: Network for people to find games of Magic: The Gathering on and around campus. The club will hold regular game nights and tournaments on occasion.
summary: Network for people to find games of Magic: The Gathering on and around campus. The club will hold regular game nights and tournaments on occasion.

name: Clemson University Microbiological Society
description: The Clemson University Microbiological Society is an organization designed for undergraduate students with an interest in microbiology. You don't have to be a microbiology major to join! The organization offers opportunities to discover new areas of Microbiology and make connections in the Microbiology department at Clemson. Meetings consist of presentations from professors, graduate students, and other microbiology professionals.
summary: The Clemson University Microbiological Society is an organization designed for undergraduate students with an interest in microbiology. You don't have to be a microbiology major to join!

name: Clemson Pre-Veterinary Club
description: The Pre-Vet Club is a professional undergraduate club for Clemson University and Bridge students of any major who aspire to attend vet school after Clemson! It is a great way to become involved in the AVS community at Clemson and to meet peers with similar interests and goals. Our club usually holds meetings every other week, on Tuesday evenings at 7 PM. We host a variety of speakers including admissions officers from a range of veterinary schools, current vet students, local veterinarians and veterinarians from a variety of concentrations including zoological and exotic medicine and equine medicine. We also hold suture and radiology clinics, fundraisers like Rent-A-Puppy, social events like Goat Yoga, and we plan a spring trip! Follow our Instagram for meeting reminders and updates @clemson_prevetclub.
summary: The Pre-Vet Club is a professional undergraduate club for all Clemson University and Bridge students of any major who aspire to attend Veterinary School after Clemson! Joining this club is a great way to become involved with the AVS community.

name: Clemson University Rocket Engineering
description:
Clemson Rocket Engineering is the best way to get involved with Aerospace Engineering on campus. The fully student-led team launches rockets up to 10,000 feet in New Mexico, at the Spaceport America Cup competition (the largest rocket competition in the world).  This competition is an amazing networking experience with engineering companies, and our alumni network is extensive, helping our members get in touch with people at NASA, SpaceX, Blue Origin, Northrop Grumman, Anduril, and more.  There is no prior experience needed to be a part of our family, just a passion for rockets.  We will teach new members everything there is to know about the parts of a rocket, the engineering process, how to use machines and various design software.  Please use the links below to find out more, as well as the attached photos (our main way of communicating is through Slack, so make sure to sign up and turn on notifications if you are 100% in).
 
Email: rockets@clemson.edu
Instagram: Clemson Rockets
Website: cecas.clemson.edu/rocket
Slack: Join Link
summary: Our mission is to introduce the aerospace industry to Clemson University for all demographics, develop future leaders around the world, and research cutting-edge technology.

name: Clemson Scuba Club
description:
The Clemson Scuba Club is an organization created to connect new divers with the means to get certified, facilitate an active diving community at Clemson University, and inspire divers to hone and practice their skills through diving education. We aim to instill a culture among our members that emphasizes the importance of exercising safe diving habits and good judgement.

 

What we do:

The club takes weekend trips to Lake Jocassee and Lake Keowee, and travels throughout the year to various locations in the Carolinas and Florida! We also host non-diving events like club hikes, movies, cookouts, and tailgates.

 

How to contact us:

Email us, DM us on Instagram, or check out our website via our linktree on Instagram! 

Information is sent via email to all Tiger Quest members.
summary: The Clemson Scuba Club is a social organization that is dedicated to the world of diving. We aim to facilitate certifications for new divers, make diving more accessible to already certified divers and bring members together.

name: TigerLive Entertainment
description:
TigerLive Entertainment is Clemson's student-run entertainment board, which plans a variety of on-campus events throughout the year. TigerLive provides a variety of events to Clemson students through committees and an executive board of directors. TigerLive Entertainment also stresses the importance of a strong member base through bi-weekly meetings to support the organization's plans for events.

---

Mission Statement

TigerLive Entertainment provides entertainment through student-programmed events that enrich the experience of the diverse and ever-changing Clemson community. TigerLive Entertainment creates a sense of belonging for Clemson students and builds campus pride in a welcoming and accessible environment. Members attain a level of professional excellence and community.
summary: Clemson's premier student programming board delivering the biggest and best events to all Clemson students. Entertainment FOR students, BY students.

name: Clemson Climbing Club
description:
The Clemson Climbing Club is the perfect place to share your passion for climbing, improve your skills, and make lifelong friends. We're dedicated to promoting the enjoyment and safety of rock climbing in all its forms. Our mission is to support climbers of all kinds, helping our members achieve their unique goals while promoting climbing ethics, addressing access issues, and encouraging participation in regional competitions.

As a club, we unite passionate climbers to build skills, share knowledge, and explore the Southeast's most exciting climbing spots. Whether you're here to hone your skills, compete, or simply enjoy the climb, the Clemson Climbing Club is your place to grow and connect with the climbing community!

We'd love to climb with you and share what makes our club so special! See you Tuesdays and Wednesdays at the Fike Climbing Wall from 8–10 pm :)
summary: Interested in rock climbing? Join Clemson Climbing Club! All skill levels are welcome! Join us for thrilling indoor and outdoor climbing adventures across the Southeast and club nights at the Fike Climbing Wall every Tuesday and Wednesday from 8–10 pm.

name: Cooperative Student Fellowship
description: CSF is a campus ministry organization that is open to students of all traditions and backgrounds. We focus on faith, hope, and love - and the greatest of these is love (I Corinthians 13:13). CSF was started by First Baptist Clemson in 2004 and is a recognized Clemson University religious organization. CSF is a gathering for students who are seeking a Christian community. As a community, we hope to encourage thoughtful exploration of what it means to be a follower of Christ in a complex and divisive world. We embrace conversations that are open and respectful, ideas that are true to who God is and who we are in God, and lifestyles that show following Christ is a way of life, not just a system of belief. We know the journey is enhanced by companions and friends and therefore seek to build genuine relationships among all who come.
summary: CSF is a campus ministry organization that is open to students of all traditions and backgrounds. We focus on faith, hope, and love - and the greatest of these is love (I Corinthians 13:13).

name: Cross Impact Campus Ministries
description: Cross Impact is a Christian ministry. Our desire is to point students on campus to Christ and encourage believers in their faith.
summary: Cross Impact is a Christian ministry. Our desire is to point students on campus to Christ and encourage believers in their faith.

name: Cuong Nhu Martial Arts Club
description:
The Clemson Cuong Nhu Club is a student run martial arts and fitness organization at Clemson University. All Clemson students are eligible to join, regardless of previous experience or physical ability. The club is an affiliate of the Cuong Nhu Martial Arts Association. Cuong Nhu is a unique style of martial arts which blends elements of Shotokan (Karate), Tai Chi Chuan (Kung Fu), Wing Chun (Kung Fu), Viet Vo Dao, Judo, Aikido, and boxing. Club meetings are structured as “classes” where all members receive instruction and coaching for their development, therefore no previous martial arts experience is required. All visitors are welcome to “try it out” before making a commitment. There is no obligation to join. Membership fees are $20 per semester and $40 for a uniform. Open enrollment occurs at the beginning of each fall and spring semester.

 

Spring 2026 SCHEDULE:

Mondays and Fridays | 7:00pm-9:00pm | Hendrix Student Center
summary: The Clemson Cuong Nhu Club is a student run martial arts and fitness organization at Clemson University. All Clemson students are eligible to join, regardless of previous experience or physical ability.

name: Clemson Cycling Club
description:
We're a group of avid bicycle riders, both on and off road. Join us for a group ride! If you want to race we have you covered; we're a part of the Southeastern Collegiate Cycling Conference and participate in mountain, road, cyclocross, and track racing.

Feel free to join the team's GroupMe and get plugged in to rides and events.
summary: The Clemson Cycling Club aims to provide university students with a safe, inclusive environment in which to promote cycling as a sport and lifestyle. This includes operating and racing as an official D1 USA Cycling collegiate club.

name: Delta Delta Delta
description: The purpose of Tri-Delta shall be to establish bonds of everlasting friendship, to develop stronger character, to broaden moral and intellectual lives.
summary: Tridelta is a Panhellenic Sorority whose values are Service, Loyalty, and Friendship.

name: Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc.
description:
The Sigma Chapter of Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. at Clemson University was established in the Spring of 2016. We are the first Asian-Interest sorority in the state of South Carolina. Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. was established by our seven lovely founding mothers on December 5th, 1998 at the University of Georgia. 


We are dedicated to our mission of spreading Asian awareness through our Everlasting Sisterhood, contributing to the community, and fostering dedicated female leaders. We look for undergraduate women who display our seven virtues of Loyalty, Honesty, Respect, Dedication, Integrity, Discipline, and Academic Excellence.

The Sigma Chapter of Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is a co-founding member of Clemson University's Multicultural Greek Council (MGC).

Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is an organization that advocates Asian awareness and empowers women to lead through values-based programs and everlasting sisterhood. Delta Phi Lambda’s national philanthropy is osteoporosis education and prevention, emphasizing the importance of promoting healthy lifestyles and behavior in young women.
summary: A sorority, part of the Multicultural Greek Council, that advocates Asian awareness and empowers women to lead through values-based programs and everlasting sisterhood. Delta Phi Lambda’s national philanthropy is osteoporosis education and prevention.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional business fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities; to encourage scholarship, social activity and the association of students for their mutual advancement by research and practice; to promote closer affiliation between the commercial world and students of commerce, and to further a higher standard of commercial ethics and culture and the civic and commercial welfare of the community.
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is an international professional business fraternity.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description: Delta Sigma Theta is a sisterhood of college educated women committed to public service. Our sorority focuses on sisterhood, scholarship, service, and social action. This sorority was founded on January 13, 1913 on the campus of Howard University by 22 illustrious founders. The Omicron Phi Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta was chartered on May 5, 1983 here at Clemson University by 7 trailblazing women known as Genesis.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta is a sisterhood of college educated women committed to public service. Our sorority focuses on sisterhood, scholarship, service, and social action.

name: Delta Tau Delta
description: Delta Tau Delta is the place for leaders to hone their skills, youths to grow, students to find support and guidance and men to find their paths to a life of excellence. With more than 150 years of guiding men via Truth, Courage, Faith and Power, the Delta Tau Delta experience has fostered innovators, advocates, change agents and community leaders by affecting the world through self-discovery.
summary: Delta Tau Delta is the place for leaders to hone their skills, youths to grow, students to find support and guidance and men to find their paths to a life of excellence.

name: Clemson Disc Golf Association
description: The Disc Golf Club is a club sports team open to all students interested in the sport of disc golf. Club members are encouraged to participate in weekly rounds of disc golf and learning clinics. Several times each year, a select group of club players will complete in Collegiate disc golf tournaments across the Southeast and country.
summary: The Clemson Disc Golf Association is a club sport open to any students interested in disc golf.  Everyone is welcome from beginners to those interested in representing Clemson in tournaments across the Southeast.

name: EcoReps
description: We are a student leadership program dedicated to making Clemson Housing and Dining sustainable. Clemson Ecological Representatives began in the Fall of 2011 as a means to engage Clemson’s on-campus student population in sustainability education. Since EcoReps' inception, hundreds of students have been directly involved in sustainable activities and initiatives each year. During the Summer of 2016, EcoReps was integrated into the Residential Hall Council within University Housing, limiting membership to students living on campus. EcoReps have opportunities to coordinate campus sustainability initiatives such as cleanups and education events, assist with institutional initiatives such as Solid Green Day, partner with third-party organizations such as Green Campus Alliance, and participate in conferences. EcoReps are currently piloting a campus Community Garden.  general body meetings and events are open to the public.
summary: EcoReps is Clemson Home’s sustainability-focused, student leadership program. EcoReps includes 5 student executive board positions. Members must be passionate about the planet.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description:
Engineers Without Borders USA Clemson University Chapter is a service organization under Engineers Without Borders USA. We partner with communities around the world to solve their most pressing infrastructure issues. After our assessment and design phases, our students travel to these countries to implement solutions. Currently, our chapter is working on a project to protect and store water in a village in Rwanda.

This organization can be taken as a Creative Inquiry class for one credit hour, or you can be a club member. Join us here on Tiger Quest to get started! The course number for the CI is EES 4900 Sections 40 & 42.
summary: We are an organization at Clemson University that seeks to solve problems in developing countries through engineering.

name: Environmental Engineering & Earth Sciences Graduate Student Organization
description: This organization is for all graduate students who are interested in environmental engineering and earth sciences at Clemson. This was formerly known as Environmental Engineering & Earth Sciences Student Unification Committee Society. Please email the President to get info on our organization Discord if interested! If you have any feedback for us, please let us know via: https://forms.gle/QUHrgAg36Wwqd892A
summary: This organization is for all graduate students who are interested in environmental engineering and earth sciences at Clemson. This was formerly known as Environmental Engineering & Earth Sciences Student Unification Committee Society.

name: Clemson Equestrian Team
description: The Clemson Equestrian Team rides and shows horses throughout the academic year in the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association. We ride, we show, we have fun, and we make great friends along the way. Clemson Equestrian is open to any full time student at the university and welcomes members of any level of experience. We provide the horses, all you have to do is enjoy the ride!
summary: The Clemson Equestrian Team is open to riders of all levels!

name: Fellowship of Christian Athletes
description: An on-campus organization seeking to proclaim the name of Jesus through worship gatherings, events, outreach, and small groups.
summary: Loving God. Loving Campus

name: Clemson Fencing Club
description:
Clemson Fencing Club is one of the largest student-run and student-coached fencing clubs in the Southeast! Our members fence one (or more!) of the three fencing weapons: epee, foil and saber.

 

We welcome members of all backgrounds. Absolutely no experience or equipment is required, and new members are welcome to come to a practice and try it out at anytime! 

We practice in the Fike Club Gym at these times:

Monday: 5-7pm

Wednesday: 5-7pm

Friday: 5-7pm
summary: We are a student-run and student-coached club dedicated to teaching and competing in the sport of fencing!

name: Field Hockey
summary: Club Field Hockey provides a competitive and fun environment for women and men to play field hockey at the collegiate level. Club Field Hockey is a student-run club that competes against other field hockey teams in the National Field Hockey league.

name: Fine Arts Student Association
description: The Fine Arts Student Association (FASA) aims to strengthen Clemson's art community for artists and art-lovers alike by creating a memorable experience for members through various art-based events, sales, trips, and general discussions. It is a place to escape from all the assigned projects they have to work on and instead participate in the club by working and discussing their own personal projects or on projects the club is putting together for future events. We hold meetings on Fridays at 5:30 pm at least twice a month; however, more details can be found on our Instagram page.
summary: The Fine Arts Student Association (FASA) aims to strengthen Clemson's art community for artists and art-lovers alike by creating a memorable experience for members through various art-based events, sales, trips, and general discussions.

name: FIRST
description:
FIRST is an organization comprised of students who are the first in their family to go to a four year university. These first generation students face unique challenges and this organization strives to help them succeed in college. Our focus on philanthropy, academic success, and social events develops young men and women while having fun at the same time.

If you are a First Generation college student and want to chat with other first gen. students much like yourself, join our GroupMe using the link below:

https://groupme.com/join_group/47280938/TrmPR7
summary: FIRST is an organization comprised of and dedicated to students who are the first in their family to go to a four year university.

name: Flying Tigers Booster Club
description: The Flyin' Tigers Booster Club empowers the cadets of Detachment 770 to excel in their pursuit of leadership, community outreach, professional development, and morale.
summary: To further the opportunities for cadets of Detachment 770

name: Clemson Flying Club
description: The Flying Club is a great opportunity for students who are passionate or interested in aviation. Club dues are $30/semester. This includes all the events for the semester and a t-shirt.  More information can be found by contacting the club Vice President, Jake Camp, at jacamp@clemson.edu. Join our GroupMe, and follow us on Instagram @clemsonuniversityflyingclub
summary: Founded in 1927, the Clemson Flying Club is the oldest collegiate flying club in the country. Today, the CFC offers Clemson students, staff, and alumni the opportunity to experience the world of general aviation.

name: Formula SAE
description: Clemson Formula SAE is a student-run organization dedicated to the enrichment of the Clemson students’ education by offering one of the best ways to apply what they learn in class. From engineering to marketing, computer programming to graphic design, CUFSAE has something to offer for every student.  We aim to provide students with the tools to learn as much as they can while exploring the possibilities of employment in the automotive industry. Our team strives to emulate the practices of the best race teams from around the world in order to be a top Formula SAE competitor. Located in the Ravenel Center of Clemson University, our shop is only a couple miles from the main campus. The team works closely with several departments of the university, including the Mechanical Engineering Department, College of Engineering and Science, Facilities Department, and Graphic Communications Department in order to complete our goals.
summary: Clemson University's Formula SAE team is a non-profit student-run organization dedicated to designing and building an open-wheeled formula-style racecar in the Society of Automotive Engineers Student Design Competition: Formula SAE.

name: Foundation for International Medical Relief of Children
description: The Foundation for International Medical Relief of Children (FIMRC) is a non-profit organization aiming to serve and improve healthcare in rural communities throughout the world by creating self-sustainable health care networks. Running outpatient clinics, working with representatives and health professionals in the community, and providing health education, FIMRC aims to improve healthcare across many dimensions in places that have previously had very limited resources. Our chapter here at Clemson sends members on medical mission trips to FIMRC clinics every winter and spring break, raises funds to provide medical supplies to the clinics, and works to improve our local community as well through local volunteer work and service. We regularly attend Helping Hands of Clemson and Mauldin Miracle League throughout the year, and we participate in Operation Christmas Child and the Clemson Miracle Dance Marathon every year, to name a few examples! Come join us on international trips anywhere from Costa Rica to the Philippines, contribute to our local projects, and become a part of the FIMRC family! We also work with our local organizations to find volunteering opportunities for our members and foster growth and inclusivity within the Clemson community.
summary: Our goal is to provide adequate healthcare to impoverished communities through education, volunteerism, and fundraising. Members have the opportunity to participate in medical mission trips abroad and give back to our local community!

name: Clemson French Club
description: Clemson French Club is a place where students can indulge in and learn about the French language and culture such as watching French cinema, eating French food, discovering French art, learning French, and exploring the work and daily culture. Everyone is encouraged to share their love and experiences! GroupMe: https://tinyurl.com/clemsonfrenchgroupme
summary: Clemson French Club is a place where students can indulge in and learn about the French language and culture such as watching French cinema, eating French food, discovering French art, learning French, and exploring the work and daily culture.

name: Clemson's Sexuality & Gender Alliance
description:
Clemson's Sexuality & Gender Alliance (C-SAGA) is Clemson's founding LGBTQ+ student organization. SAGA is committed to community, inclusivity, and advocacy. Everyone is welcome in SAGA, regardless of identity. We respect privacy and, therefore, will never ask for dues, nor do we enforce membership requirements. Anyone is welcome to attend a SAGA meeting at any time. 

We hold general meetings once a month but also have casual social events intermittently. We are committed to serving Clemson's queer and allied community and welcome feedback from members. Elections are held annually for our executive positions, but please contact us anytime to inquire about openings!
summary: Clemson's Sexuality & Gender Alliance (C-SAGA) is Clemson's founding LGBTQ+ student organization. We are committed to community, inclusivity, and advocacy. We hold meetings every other Wednesday, and host events throughout the semester. See ya soon!

name: Her Campus at Clemson
description: Her Campus at Clemson is a chapter of the #1 online magazine for college women, written entirely by and for students. Our organization creates original content about campus life, style, culture, career, relationships, and more — all with the goal of building a creative, connected community at Clemson. Through writing, editing, social media, photography, and event planning, members gain valuable skills while collaborating on projects that make an impact both on campus and beyond. Whether you’re passionate about journalism, digital media, marketing, or just want to make new friends and get involved, Her Campus at Clemson offers a place for you to share your voice and ideas.
summary: Her Campus at Clemson is a chapter of the #1 online magazine for college women, written entirely by and for students!

name: Clemson Ice Hockey
description: The Clemson Ice Hockey team competes as a Division III team in the ACHA. The club is a top-level program in the North Region and competes against neighboring universities such as UGA, Georgia Tech, UT Knoxville, and UoSC. The purpose of the organization is to give skilled hockey players a chance to extend their playing careers into college. Typically, the season begins in September and extends through February, with the team taking winter break off.
summary: The Clemson Ice Hockey team competes in the Amateur Athletic Union (AAU) in the Division 2 North Region. The team's home rink is in Greenville, SC, and the team travels all over the region to play top-flight programs.

name: Interfraternity Council
description:
Leadership

Clemson University’s IFC community is dedicated to building future leaders by providing numerous leadership opportunities. Campus leaders include men involved in Student Government, IPTAY, CLEMSONLiVE, Student Alumni Council, Clemson Guide Association, FCA, and more. IFC offers many resources to become a leader within the community, and provides leadership training to those who seek it.

Brotherhood

IFC fraternities were founded as a network of support for men who were going through a similar experience. An IFC fraternity is a great way to enhance the general college experience through brotherhood and friendship. One of the founding principles of each IFC fraternity is service to the community. Over $300,000 is raised and over 14,000 hours of service are performed by fraternity men each year in various activities throughout the community.

Scholarship

We strengthen our fraternal bond and maximize personal achievement by striving to excel in our professional and educational endeavors. We strive to meet and surpass the standards set by our national organizations and the University. The Greek community consistently finishes at or above the University grade point average. IFC fraternity life offers a wide variety of academic advantages including tutoring programs, peer advisement and study hours.

Athletics

Intramurals are another exciting aspect of IFC fraternity life. Although open to all students, the strongest competition is among Greek organizations. Athletics prove to be important physical and mental outlets for students, helping improve self-confidence and develop teamwork. Through intense competition, intramurals continue to help build brotherhood and school spirit.
summary: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) governs all of Clemson's male social fraternities and is committed to building a strong Greek community. IFC provides opportunities for its members to become leaders and effect positive change at Clemson.

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description: Kappa Alpha Order is a social fraternity and a member of the Interfraternity Council (IFC).
summary: Kappa Alpha Order is a social fraternity which has been active at Clemson University since 1970.

name: Kappa Delta
description:
A national organization for women, Kappa Delta Sorority provides experiences that build confidence in women and inspire them to action through the power of lifelong friendship. With a focus on values, Kappa Delta offers women the confidence to excel academically, develop leadership skills, become responsible citizens and take action in the world.

The mission of Kappa Delta is: "encouraging members to live with integrity and honor in the bonds of lifelong friendship; building confidence in members to excel in college and throughout their lives; inspiring members to be active leaders, responsible citizens and engaged members of the community; and providing opportunities and experiences that foster personal and professional growth for a lifetime."

Founded Oct. 23, 1897, at State Female Normal School (now Longwood University) in Farmville, Virginia, Kappa Delta now has 168 collegiate chapters and more than 200 active alumnae chapters across the country that allow members to stay involved throughout their lifetimes.

The Epsilon Tau chapter of Kappa Delta Sorority at Clemson University shares in these national values as a sisterhood, always striving to further our mission and impact together.
summary: Kappa Delta Sorority builds confidence and inspires action amongst its members through a focus on the values to excel academically, develop leadership skills, become responsible citizens, and take positive action in the world.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma
description: Kappa promotes, through its standards, a broad college experience. Scholarship and intellectual development is our first priority, and the impressive achievements of our members attest to this. In Kappa, scholarship programs provide an atmosphere for academic accomplishment emphasizing that each member reach her personal academic best. Cultural growth is important as well. Kappa Kappa Gamma encourages members to seek opportunities for self-growth in a variety of interests in the chapter, campus, and in the community. Kappa Kappa Gamma also provides leadership opportunities in a mutually supportive environment. Kappa members believe that philanthropy or mutual helpfulness is of significant importance. Awareness of the needs of others is raised by participating in campus philanthropy events, by quiet moments of companionship with nursing home residents and raising money for our Philanthropy, Mental Health Awareness, by supporting The Jed Foundation.
summary: We are a women's organization that thrives on tradition, leadership, academics, and friendship. From participating in philanthropies to fun sisterhood activities, we believe college is an opportunity to flourish and grow with each other.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi
description: Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of his/her/their band. Our goals are to provide the band not only with organized and concentrated service activities, but to give our membership valid and wholesome experiences in organization, leadership and social contacts. The honorary nature of membership is based on our premise that “it is an honor to be selected to serve” this band, its department of music, its sponsoring institution, and the cause of band music in the nation's colleges and universities.
summary: Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of his/her band.

name: Kappa Sigma
description: Kappa Sigma is the largest college social fraternity in the world with more than 200,000 members, including over 20,000 undergraduates and 320 chapters and colonies located throughout the United States. At Clemson University, our chapter emphasizes academic excellence, outstanding community impact, and a lasting brotherhood.
summary: Kappa-Upsilon Chapter of the Kappa Sigma Fraternity

name: Latinos Unidos at Clemson University
description:
Latinos Unidos at Clemson University (LUCU) is an organization that seeks to unite all Hispanic/Latinx students as well as those interested in learning and embracing the culture. LUCU encourages the learning and sharing of different countries' language and culture. LUCU strives to bring awareness to the issues faced by the Hispanic/Latinx community. 

LUCU's Mission Statement: Unite, Educate, and Empower the Hispanic/Latinx community and anyone willing to learn and embrace our culture.

Click to Access our Google Calendar
summary: Latinos Unidos at Clemson University has a mission is to unite, educate, and empower the Hispanic/Latinx community and anyone willing to learn and embrace our culture.

name: Lutheran Campus Ministry
description: Lutheran Campus Ministry is a faith-based organization that is open to people from any religious or non-religious background. LCM strives to be a welcoming, open community where all can experience God's love through a circle of peers and congregation members and also through the campus pastor. Students are invited to gather together for weekly meals, meaningful conversations, weekend retreats, and other fun events. The student group is affiliated with University Lutheran Church downtown and joins the congregation for worship and service opportunities. Above all, LCM is a place for students to feel loved and cared for while they are away from home during their college experience.
summary: Lutheran Campus Ministry is a faith-based organization that is open to people from any religious or non-religious background. We gather for weekly meals, meaningful conversations, weekend retreats, and other fun events.

name: Material Advantage
description: Material Advantage is an undergraduate and graduate student Materials Science and Engineering organization that allows its members to collaborate with one another, further engage with professors, and network with professionals through various events such as in-person meetings or conferences. The organization also provides its members with membership to four major national societies: ASM, TMS, AIST, and ACerS.
summary: Material Advantage is an undergraduate MSE organization that allows its members to further engage with professors and network with professionals through various events. MA provides membership to four major national societies: ASM, TMS, AIST, and ACerS.

name: Clemson Lacrosse
description: The Men's Lacrosse team is a student run organization and a member of the ALC (Atlantic Lacrosse Conference) in the MCLA. The team is led by Head Coach Drew Tobias along with 3 other assistant coaches and student captains. The team competes against other college club teams and men’s club teams from all over the country, specifically in the South East. Club Lacrosse provides a fun, competitive, and safe environment for students to play lacrosse at a collegiate level. The Clemson Men's Lacrosse team is consistently a nationally ranked team that provides a high level of competition for the club sports level.
summary: The Men's Lacrosse team is a student run organization that competes against other college club lacrosse teams from all over the country in the MCLA. Compete in both the fall and spring creating for a competitive environment.

name: Men's Rugby Club
description: Founded in 1967, the Clemson University rugby team has a rich history of success in intercollegiate competition. With strong performances throughout the 1990's and consistent top 25 national finishes since the early 2000's, Clemson Rugby has been symbol of excellence in American collegiate rugby. Through tremendous support from the University and the Clemson Rugby Foundation, the rugby team is making great strides to re-establish itself as an elite program in the United States. Clemson competes in the SCRC rugby league, a top league in the country.
summary: We are a high-performance program that is dedicated to learning more about the sport, as well as perfecting our skills so that we may compete with any team that comes our way. We are a very welcoming team and would love to have anyone that wants to play!

name: Men's Club Soccer
description: Clemson Men's Club Soccer gives students the opportunity to play competitive soccer against other University club teams around the country. We travel to other universities as well as host one of the largest club soccer tournaments in the area at Clemson every fall semester.
summary: Men's Club Soccer gives students the opportunity to play competitive soccer against other University club teams around the country. We travel to other universities as well as host one of the largest club soccer tournaments at Clemson each semester.

name: Men's Club Volleyball
description:
National Championship DIAAA 2022 Winners (Phoenix, AZ)


Clemson Men's Volleyball offers competitive volleyball practice and play to those that enjoy the sport. We offer the competitiveness and skill of collegiate programs without the rigors of training and travel schedule.

Our team practices every Tuesday and Thursday from 7-9 pm during the Fall/Spring semesters. Players are expected to attend all practices each semester, so please consider if you can comply with these requirements before trying out. Common exam times, funeral, illness, etc. are considered excused absences. If you are looking to play recreationally and improve your skills come out to open gym on Sundays (information below)!

Every Sunday from 7-9 PM, we host open gym in the Club gym at Fike. Feel free to come out and invite your friends!  With any questions please email clemsonmensvolleyball@gmail.com
summary: Clemson Men's Volleyball offers competitive volleyball practice and play to those that enjoy the sport. We offer the competitiveness and skill of collegiate programs without the rigors of training and travel schedule

name: Muslim Students Association
summary: The Clemson Muslim Students Association is the primary representative of Muslim students at the university. Our main goal is to promote relations between Muslims students, and with non-Muslims by promoting cultural awareness.

name: Clemson National Organization of Minority Architecture Students
description: cNOMAS is a student chapter of the National Organization of Minority Architects. We gather together to celebrate the cultures, backgrounds, and identities that make us different and how they show in the architectural realm. We also create community with one another to uplift those in underrepresented or marginalized groups.
