summary: cNOMAS is a student chapter of the National Organization of Minority Architects. We gather together to celebrate the cultures, backgrounds, and identities that make us different and how they show in the architectural realm.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description: In 1974, Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. became the first NPHC organization chartered on the campus of Clemson University. Three years later, Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. was chartered, becoming the university's first NPHC sorority to be chartered. Since then, Clemson University has been home to each of the nine organizations within the NPHC, with charters for each organization. Currently, eight organizations are actively involved on campus. Clemson University's NPHC serves as an integral part of the Clemson community. Chapters host and participate in numerous service and philanthropy events throughout the year. In the fall of each year, the NPHC sponsors its annual Fall Fest Stroll Off and in the spring a week of service. Chapters provide both service and social opportunities to the campus and community. The council strives to promote excellence and achievement in all areas.
summary: National Pan-Hellenic is the umbrella for the 9 Black Greek Letter Organizations that uphold Scholarship, Service, and Sisterhood/Brotherhood.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description: The National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) is a leadership-based honorary comprised of exemplary student leaders who value recognition and service. NRHH seeks to inspire and engage a lifelong commitment to the values of recognition and service in ever-changing university environments while fostering community within on-campus spaces. As an honorary, NRHH unites campus leaders who dedicate themselves to recognizing and serving on-campus communities.
summary: A leadership-based honorary, comprised of exemplary students who value recognition and service within on-campus residence halls.

name: Nepalese Student Association
description:
"NepSA is a non-political, nonprofit group led by graduate students at Clemson University. We celebrate Nepali culture, host interactive social and cultural events for the Nepali community in Clemson, and most importantly, help new students from Nepal feel at home as they settle into life at Clemson."

For new incoming Nepali students in Clemson: "Whether you are looking for friendship, cultural connection, or practical guidance, NepSA is here to help you start strong and stay connected during your Clemson journey. Please reach out to us to be a part of NepSA Clemson."
summary: Non-political, non-profit organization for introducing Nepali Culture through various events and providing mutual assistance to Nepali students in Clemson.

name: Nutrition Club
description: The mission of the Nutrition Club is to make nutrition fun, accessible, and educational while creating a welcoming space for students of all majors to connect, learn, and build community. The club also supports Nutrition majors by providing exposure to professionals, speakers, and opportunities within the field. Email clemsonnutritionclub@gmail.com to receive emails regarding upcoming meetings and events!
summary: The Nutrition Club is open to students of all majors and provides a fun, educational, and welcoming space to learn about nutrition, connect with others, and explore nutrition-related careers.

name: Clemson Offroad Club
description: We are a group of students and faculty who enjoy the outdoors. We have wheeling trips throughout each semester all over the southeast to places like Black Mountain Offroad Park, Uwharrie National Forest, Gulches ORV, Coalmont, and some light-duty trips such as Hurricane Creek. We also do local rides on different trail systems and parks that are under 45 minutes away. Vehicles range from stock to wild in the club. We focus on custom fabrication, anything from bumpers, body protection, to complete axle swaps. You can have any level of knowledge about offroad vehicles, the club is open to anyone and we're eager to help.
summary: Jeeps, Toyotas and 4x4. No matter what it is, if it's got 4wd come wheel with us.

name: Omicron Delta Kappa
description: Omicron Delta Kappa, the National Leadership Honor Society, was founded December 3, 1914 at Washington and Lee University in Lexington, Virginia by 15 student and faculty leaders. The founders formulated the idea that leadership of exceptional quality and versatility in college should be recognized, that representatives in all phases of college life should cooperate in worthwhile endeavors, and that outstanding students, faculty, and administrators should meet on a basis of mutual interest, understanding, and helpfulness. O∆K was the first college honor society of a national scope to give recognition and honor for meritorious leadership and service in extracurricular activities and to encourage development of general campus citizenship. Since its founding, Omicron Delta Kappa has initiated over 300,000 members.
summary: Omicron Delta Kappa National Leadership Honor Society recognizes Clemson University students who are top leaders in scholarship, athletics, service, mass media and performing arts at the University and in the community.

name: Order of Omega
description: The Epsilon Kappa chapter of Order of Omega is the premier Honor Society for Clemson's Fraternity & Sorority leaders. Order of Omega is comprised of the top 3% of the Clemson Fraternity & Sorority community, with members from the Interfraternity Council (IFC), Panhellenic Council (CPA), National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC), and Multicultural Greek Council (MGC). OoO recognizes men and women that have attained a high standard of leadership within the campus and Greek Community while providing opportunities for campus engagement, personal and professional development, and community building. Order of Omega unites Fraternity & Sorority leaders and allows members to have the opportunity to engage with Clemson Administration on many issues concerning the fraternity & sorority experience at Clemson. To be eligible for membership, you must have a cumulative GPA of at least higher than your council's average, have completed 30 credit hours, and be a current undergraduate Clemson student.
summary: Order of Omega is the premier leadership honor society for members of Greek organizations. Comprised of members of each Clemson Council, OoO unites Fraternity & Sorority leaders and provides members the opportunity to engage with Clemson Administration.

name: Orientation Ambassadors
description:
2026 ORIENTATION AMBASSADOR APPLICATIONS HAVE CLOSED. 


*YOU MUST BE LOGGED IN TO SEE THE APPLICATION LINK (Scroll down to "Forms")

 

Do you want to help support new Tigers and their families in the start of the Clemson journey? Become an Orientation Ambassador! This is a paid position that will allow you to develop leadership and other transferable skills, give back to the Clemson experience, all while making memories and lasting connections!

 

Applications have been extended!!! They are live here on Tiger Quest through November 3 at 11:59 PM! Check out the 2026 OA Recruitment Timeline below:

 

OA Recruitment Timeline:

Applications Close: 11/3/2025 at 11:59PM EST

Round 1: Group Interviews: 11/11/2025 – 11/13/2025

Round 2: Individual Interviews:11/17/2025 - 11/20/2025 

Offers Go Out: By the end of November!

Questions?! Contact the Orientation Staff:

orientation@admission.clemson.edu

https://www.clemson.edu/ug-orientation/become-an-orientation-ambassador.html

 

Our organization's main goal is to welcome new students and their families to Clemson University. By facilitating informational sessions, answering questions, and serving as guides, role models, and everything it means to be a Clemson Student. Our organization hopes to help ease the transition to Clemson to all new students. This group of students helps implement both summer and January orientations while serving as a resource and role model all year long. Want to leave a legacy at Clemson? Become an Orientation Ambassador!
summary: Our organization's main goal is to welcome new students and their families to Clemson University!

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description:
Our organization provides a place of community and fellowship for Orthodox Christians, people interested in Orthodoxy, and anyone else looking for a friendly face! We hold weekly meetings throughout the semester for fellowship, learning, and worship. Follow us on Instagram @clemsonorthodox.

Meetings are currently held (as of January 2026) on Mondays and Tuesdays at 6 PM in Hardin 201. Our fellowship meetings are open to all!

For more information, feel free to contact us! We typically communicate through groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/16326377/D3eHafnb.

Contact the listed President's email to be added if you are interested.
summary: Our organization provides a place of community and fellowship for Orthodox Christians, people interested in Orthodoxy, and anyone else looking for a friendly face! We hold weekly meetings throughout the semester for fellowship, learning, and worship.

name: Graduate Association of Materials Engineering and Science
summary: Formerly MRS and Optica, GAMES now represents all MSE graduate students. It’s a great way to meet peers, get involved in the department, and grow through networking and professional opportunities.

name: College Panhellenic Association
description:
Greek life was founded at Clemson University in 1970. There are currently four Greek councils: the Interfraternity Council, Multicultural Greek Council, National Pan-Hellenic Council and the College Panhellenic Association, for a combined total of 47 chapters.

 

The College Panhellenic Association serves as the governing body for 13 National Panhellenic Conference chapters on campus. As an association, we advocate the advancement of the sorority experience for each of the 3,000+ Panhellenic women at Clemson University. 

 

We seek to strengthen the Panhellenic community by promoting positive values in our members. Panhellenic membership at Clemson University is based on four core values: scholarship, sisterhood, leadership and service. Through educational, service, and social activities, the College Panhellenic Association strives to develop its members during and also post college.

 

The College Panhellenic Association is led by an Executive Board, composed of elected leaders, representative of different chapters on campus. We ensure that our chapters have the resources they need to succeed and that they provide an enriching experience for our members while complying with our policies and procedures.
summary: The College Panhellenic Association serves as the governing body for 13 National Panhellenic Conference chapters on campus. As an association, we advocate the advancement of the sorority experience for each of the 3,000+ Panhellenic women at Clemson.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated
description: The purpose of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity Inc. is to uplift the community by striving for excellence and helping others achieve. Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. was founded January 9, 1914 on the campus of Howard University. The fraternity's vision has been crystallized into the three principles of brotherhood, scholarship and service. The motto of the fraternity is: "Culture for Service, Service for Humanity."
summary: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. was founded January 9, 1914 on the campus of Howard University. The Omicron Lambda Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated was chartered on Clemson University's Campus on April 27, 1984.

name: Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia - Sigma Mu Chapter
description: Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia Fraternity of America (also known as Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia, Phi Mu Alpha, or simply Sinfonia) is an American collegiate social fraternity for men with a special interest in music. The fraternity is open to men "who, through a love for music, can assist in the fulfillment of its Object and ideals either by adopting music as a profession, or by working to advance the cause of music in America." Phi Mu Alpha has initiated more than 150,000 members, known as Sinfonians, and the fraternity currently has almost 6,500 active collegiate members in 237 collegiate chapters throughout the United States.
summary: Social Fraternity with a love for music.

name: Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity
description:
The Beta Kappa Chapter of Phi Sigma Pi at Clemson University is a gender-inclusive National Honor Fraternity dedicated to fostering scholarship, leadership, and fellowship among high-achieving undergraduate students. Membership is open to any Clemson undergraduate with a minimum 3.0 GPA (unless otherwise unestablished) who is not in their final graduating senior semester (unless approved by the Chapter body).

Phi Sigma Pi is founded on a tripod of principles—scholarship, leadership, and fellowship—which serves as the foundation for personal growth, meaningful service, and lifelong professional development. Through these guiding values, members are encouraged to lead with integrity, support one another academically and socially, and engage in service that positively impacts the campus and broader community.
summary: The Beta Kappa Chapter of Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive national honor fraternity that welcomes eligible Clemson students the opportunity to join. We value a tripod of scholarship, leadership, and fellowship that guides our goal of social service.

name: Pi Kappa Phi
description: Pi Kappa Phi was founded by exceptional men who chose to lead. For more than a century, we have stayed true to our roots, developing the next generation of servant leaders who put service before self and work to improve the world around them. Discover what makes our brotherhood so remarkable. Our mission is to create an uncommon and lifelong brotherhood that develops leaders and encourages service to others for the betterment of our communities. Our vision is a future where every Pi Kappa Phi embraces his role as a leader, puts service before self and improves the world around him. Our values are: Common Loyalty, Personal Responsibility, Achievement, Accountability, Campus Involvement, Responsible Citizenship, Lifelong Commitment.
summary: Social fraternity comprised of members committed to academic excellence, campus involvement, and strong brotherhood.

name: Pi Tau Sigma
description: Pi Tau Sigma is a mechanical engineering honor society that focuses on sound engineering ability, scholarship, service, leadership, and integrity. Membership is by invitation only and reserved for the top 25% of students in the department. As a chapter, we hope to plan one event per semester focusing on community and leadership opportunities.
summary: Pi Tau Sigma is a mechanical engineering honor society that focuses on sound engineering ability, scholarship, service, leadership, and integrity.

name: Clemson University Public Relations Student Society of America
description: The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is a student organization for students looking to gain valuable information, skills and networking opportunities to help thrive in their future Public Relations careers. Any student interested in Communication, Marketing, Management, Advertising or Public Relations is welcome to join!
summary: The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is a student organization for students looking to gain valuable information, skills and networking opportunities to help thrive in their future Public Relations careers.

name: Recreational Golf Club
description:
Discover the perfect blend of competitive spirit and recreational enjoyment at The Recreational Golf Club. Tailored for golfers of all skill levels, our club provides a monthly haven for enthusiasts seeking both fun and camaraderie on the green. Immerse yourself in our exciting tournaments, held at renowned local courses, including the prestigious Walker Course. Please reach out for the GroupMe code as well!

Membership is a breeze at just $25, offering flexibility and minimal commitment. Embrace the opportunity to forge connections with fellow golfers, all while competing for fantastic prizes. Whether you're a seasoned pro or a casual player, our club is designed to accommodate your preferences.

For more information about the Clemson Rec Golf Club, check out our Instagram and join the GroupMe, both listed below!
summary: The RGC provides the opportunity for golfers of ALL LEVELS to easily participate at a friendly yet competitive level of play. The club promotes the social aspects of the sport, creating a closer community among Clemson students.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description:
RUF is a Christian ministry of the Presbyterian Church in America (PCA) at Clemson University. Regardless of your beliefs or doubts, RUF is a place for you to explore Christianity and grow in your understanding of Jesus Christ and what it means to follow Him in community with others at Clemson. 

Find our linktree here!

Incoming Freshmen fill out this form!
summary: RUF is a Christian ministry of the Presbyterian Church in America (PCA) committed to "reaching students for Christ and equipping students to serve."

name: Reformed University Fellowship International
description:
Who are we?

Reformed University Fellowship International (RUF-I) is a recognized student campus organization for International students/scholars and their families from all over the world and American Christians who meet for friendship, intercultural exchange, sharing of ideas, and understanding of the Christian faith. We are affiliated nationally with the Presbyterian Church in American (PCA), and locally with Clemson Presbyterian Church which is located less than a mile from campus on Vineyard Road off of Highway 93.

Where do we meet?

We meet at the Fellowship Hall of Clemson Presbyterian Church. The address is 346 Old Greenville Highway, Clemson.

Who can participate?

Any international student(s), scholar(s), and their families of any culture, ethnic or religious background are welcome and invited to participate in any of the activities and programs we offer.
summary: We provide welcome events and activities as well as the opportunity to explore the teachings of Christianity for those who are interested. You can find out more about us by following this link https://linktr.ee/ruficlemson

name: Residence Hall Association
description:
We, the Residence Hall Association, a residential resource organization, advocate for on-campus residents and promote leadership, services, and opportunities in order to create a link between the residents and the University by educating the residents, promoting diversity and serving as a voice.

 

The Residence Hall Association serves as the voice of on campus residents. Our purpose is to better the “on-campus living experience” by building community, listening to student’s concerns, and providing social and educational programs for residents. We do this through two separate entities; the RHA General Assembly and the Community Councils. Each community council serves as the voice of the residential community they preside in. Councils meet weekly to plan programs for residents, evaluate concerns within the specific residence hall(s), and work on advocacy initiatives. Each community council has nine members; 3 Senators, 2 EcoReps, 1 Governor, 1 Secretary, 1 Events Chair, and 1 Community Advocate. The three senators from each council attend weekly General Assembly meetings which are held by the RHA Executive Board. Here, they are allowed to vote on legislation for RHA, connect/collaborate with other community councils, and learn leadership skills that can be applied to their position in the council.

 

Current Budget

RHA Calendar (now color-coded!) - 'weekly' view is best

Constitution & Policy Manual 

General Assembly Minutes 

Exec Board Minutes
summary: The Residence Hall Association is a residential resource organization that advocates for on-campus residents by promoting leadership, service, and opportunities in order to create a link between the residents and the University.

name: Clemson Rotaract Club
description: Rotaract is the collegiate level of Rotary International, an association of service-minded professional leaders (Interact is the high school equivalent). Rotaract's primary activities include community service projects. We volunteer with many organizations near the Clemson community as well as have small service opportunities at our meetings. Some of our service projects include Ronald McDonald House, Rebuild Upstate, Helping Hands, and Clemson Community Care. Rotaract is an awesome way to meet new people, create friendships with other student leaders, and make an impact in the local community. Members also have the opportunity to develop professional development skills!
summary: A community service and professional development organization for all majors! Flexible with your schedule, Rotaract is a great way to develop leadership skills and boost your resume. Make new friends while volunteering in the local Clemson community!

name: School of  Computing Graduate Student Association
description: The School of Computing Graduate Student Association (SoCGSA) is the primary representative body for graduate students within the School of Computing. Our mission is to enhance the graduate student experience by promoting interdisciplinary collaboration and fostering valuable industry connections. Through initiatives like alumni panels, skill-building workshops, and networking events, we empower students to thrive academically, and professionally. SoCGSA is a community for inspiration, growth and development—where graduate students thrive together!
summary: SoCGSA aims to enhance the graduate student experience by fostering interdisciplinary collaboration and industry connections. Through initiatives like alumni panels and workshops, we empower students to thrive academically and professionally.

name: Clemson Shotgun Team
description:
If you have any questions about the Clemson Shotgun Team, please visit our Facebook page and/or e-mail the current President.

In 2010, The Clemson Shotgun Team was reinvented. With a lot of hard work from club members and coaches, the team traveled to San Antonio, Texas to compete in its first ACUI Intercollegiate Clay Target National Championship. 

If you have any questions about the Clemson Shotgun Team, please visit our Facebook page and/or email the current President for more information.

The Clemson Shotgun Team was revitalized in 2010. Through the dedication and hard work of our members and coaches, the team traveled to San Antonio, Texas to compete in its first ACUI Intercollegiate Clay Target National Championship.

Since then, the team has experienced significant growth and success. Along with numerous regional victories, the Clemson Shotgun Team captured the 2013 Division III National Championship in San Antonio, Texas, and the 2015 Division II SCTP Collegiate National Championship in Ohio. Most recently, the team earned the ACUI Make-A-Break National Championship in San Antonio, Texas.

Today, we are a highly competitive program seeking motivated athletes with strong experience in competitive clay target shooting. The team competes across all disciplines, including American and Olympic trap and skeet, sporting clays, and five stand. We welcome shooters from a wide range of backgrounds.

If you are interested in potentially walking on to the team, please email the President or Advisor to arrange a tryout.
summary: The Clemson Shotgun Team is a highly competitive and student-led organization that competes in all disciplines of clay target shooting at the highest level.

name: Sigma Alpha Omega
description:
GOOGLE INTEREST FORM FOR SPRING 2025 RECRUITMENT: https://forms.gle/pm8Ec14Eu6Mf49nJ9

Sigma Alpha Omega is a Christian social sorority founded first in Christ and second in sisterhood. The sorority exists "to glorify God by reaching women across the world through the expansion of the sisterhood, by encouraging spiritual development through Christ inspired accountability and unity, by participating in evangelistic and philanthropic endeavors, and focusing on good scholarship and humble leadership." We want to grow together in love and encouragement through Christ! If you are interested in this organization, do not hesitate to reach out.
summary: Sigma Alpha Omega is a Christian Sorority that exists to glorify God through sisterhood! We are a group of women who encourage each other through the love of Christ, and share that love with each other and on campus.

name: Sigma Kappa
description: Welcome to the Kappa Pi chapter of Sigma Kappa Sorority at Clemson University. The Sigma Kappa chapter at Clemson was founded in 2009, and have used this time to build such a supportive community of women! Sigma Kappa's four values are Personal Growth, Friendship, Service, and Loyalty. The women of Sigma Kappa at Clemson embody these values daily while striving to better the Greek community, our campus, and the city of Clemson.
summary: Sigma Kappa, Kappa Pi strives to provide women with lifelong opportunities as well as support for social, intellectual, and spiritual development by bringing our members together to positively impact our community.

name: Sigma Nu
description: Sigma Nu enhances the experience of its members and builds a sense of community in a way that generates a desire to invest time, talent, and treasure in the development of both the organization and its future members, which is recognized by all as a contribution to the greater good.
summary: Fraternity

name: Silver Wings
description:
“Silver Wings is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.”

The mission of Silver Wings is carried out through the following objectives:

  1. Personal Development-Local chapter activities and projects provide growth through service to the community enabling the individual member to develop a sense of purpose within and a responsibility to the community.

 

  1. Professional Development-Local chapter activities and the organizational structure provide members with leadership opportunities and the chance to develop professional skills such as oral and written communication throughout the use of workshops, guest speakers, seminars, project planning, and committee membership.

 

  1. Civic Awareness-Exposure to Air Force issues and personnel enables SW members to develop a personal awareness of aerospace power and the role that it plays in national defense. The leadership and skills development objectives are enhanced by opportunities for members to actively promote aerospace issues in the community through educational programs, seminars, public relations activities, and voter awareness projects.
summary: Silver Wings is a national, co-ed organization that focuses on professional development through leadership opportunities, personal development through community service, and civic awareness through education of national defense and aerospace power.

name: Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics
description: SIAM is the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics. SIAM is an organization for students, professors, and professionals interested and involved in mathematics and its applications to the real world. The mission of SIAM is to build cooperation between mathematics and the worlds of science and technology through publications, research, and community. The Clemson University SIAM student chapter is organized by an officer crew elected each year by the students in the department. Boshi Yang serves as the faculty advisor.
summary: SIAM is an organization for students, professors, and professionals interested and involved in mathematics and its applications to the real world.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers - Clemson University Chapter
description:
History: The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) was founded in Los Angeles, California, in 1974 by a group of engineers employed by the city of Los Angeles. Their objective was to form a national organization of professional engineers to serve as role models in the Hispanic community. The concept of Networking was the key basis for the organization. SHPE quickly established two student chapters to begin the network that would grow to encompass the nation as well as reach countries outside the United States.

Today, SHPE enjoys a strong but independent network of professional and student chapters throughout the nation.

Mission:

SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

Vision:

SHPE’s vision is a world where Hispanics are highly valued and influential as the leading innovators, scientists, mathematicians, and engineers

Putting aside all this professional and serious talk, we would like to remind you that we are
all a family. Despite the nature of our name, our chapter has seen members from various
backgrounds since its inception. We love diversity and we welcome all of you. Whether you just need someone to study with, an internship or a group of people you can call friends, SHPE is the organization you are looking for.
summary: Join the Familia! Our goal is to empower all student to develop their full academic and professional potential. We host Industry Night, professional workshops, social events, K-12 outreach, and attend SHPE National Convention.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description: The Society of Women Engineers exists to empower women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expand the image of the engineering and technology professions as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity and inclusion.
summary: The Society of Women Engineers empowers women to achieve full potential as engineers and leaders, expands the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrates the value of diversity and inclusion.

name: Solid Green Student Club
description:
***Click JOIN to be added to our email list!***

Solid Green is Clemson's official sustainability brand as well as a student club dedicated to making Clemson a more environmentally-friendly campus through community service. Solid Green Club has had tremendous success with recycling and anti-litter campaigns on campus, which took off in 2009. Today, Solid Green encompasses many more aspects of sustainability and collaborates closely with Clemson Facilities, Recycling, and other student clubs to improve the everyday sustainability of our campus. We seek to educate the Clemson community about the importance of sustainability through outreach events and activities.
summary: Solid Green is Clemson's sustainability brand as well as a student club focused on making Clemson a more environmentally-friendly campus and community. We seek to educate the Clemson community on the importance of sustainability through outreach events.

name: Student Personnel Association
description: SPA is a social and professional development organization for graduate students, faculty and staff within Student Affairs. Throughout the year, the organization hosts social events and professional development opportunities to holistically develop professionals in our field!
summary: SPA is a social and professional development organization for graduate students, faculty and staff within Student Affairs.

name: Student Veterans Association
description:
The Clemson Student Veterans Association is a student organization founded in 2008 by student veterans in order to advocate for military veteran and military-connected students at Clemson University. We promote their educational, social, recreational, and cultural interests and cooperate with other organizations on campus. We do this by providing a community of support for student military veterans, as well as their family members and loved ones. We also serve on a number of student councils, speak before the student government and communicate with the National Student Veterans Association to find ways to better serve Clemson’s military population.

Keep up to date with the Clemson SVA by connecting with our social media accounts: https://linktr.ee/clemsonuniversitysva
summary: Welcome to the Clemson Student Veterans Association TigerQuest page!

name: Clemson Club Tennis
description: Clemson Club Tennis aims to provide Clemson University students an outlet to share their passion for tennis with others during their college careers. We organize tennis practices, tournaments, and tennis-related activities. We are nationally recognized by the USTA’s “Tennis on Campus." We are affiliated with other universities’ Club Tennis teams nationwide. We play teams close to us, such as the University of South Carolina, and have played teams as far away as the University of Arizona. Although we are considered a Club Sport, we do NOT cut anyone. This means whether you’re just looking to hit around or travel to tournaments, you can make your experience with us as competitive as you like. We have practice Monday through Thursday from 7-8:30 PM on the outdoor varsity courts at the Duckworth Tennis Facility. If you love tennis, are eager to try something new, or are looking to make new friends, Club Tennis is the perfect choice for you!
summary: We love tennis!

name: The Navigators
description:
The Navigators are non-denominational Christian organization that seeks to exalt Jesus in all of life. We are committed to the study and application of God’s Word, and desire to see students grow in the knowledge, fear, and love of God as they are gripped with the purity and power of the Gospel of Jesus Christ. Our ultimate goal is to equip students to fulfill the truth of 2 Timothy 2:2, to teach pass-on the what they have learned to others.

We'd love to have you check us out.  Our information, current events and activities are available online at our website https://clemsonnavs.weebly.com/ . If you have any questions please feel free to contact us at thenavs@g.clemson.edu.

Thanks! 

-Jason, The Clemson Navigators Campus Director
summary: The Clemson Navigators is a community that exists to train and equip students to walk with Jesus and help others do the same for the rest of their lives.

name: The Pendulum
description: The Pendulum strives to contextualize Clemson University’s curricula within the broader global community. The world’s burdens demand international engagement; we believe Clemson students should be both knowledgeable of past, current, and future global issues and aware of their ability to improve the world through the application of their diverse skill sets.
summary: The Pendulum, founded in 2014, is a student-run, student-written publication and has become Clemson University's premier international affairs magazine.

name: Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity
description:
The Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity is a close group of co-ed undergraduate engineering students focused on service, professional development, and brotherhood. Theta Tau is the oldest and the largest engineering fraternity, consisting of 100+ chapters across the country.

 

The Lambda Gamma chapter of Theta Tau was granted its charter by the national committee on January 13, 2001. Since then, our members have been driven to improve their community and their relationship among themselves through weekly events and meetings. From professional networking events-to-elementary school STEM nights-to-formal events, we guarantee that Theta Tau has something to offer you.

 

In Spring 2023, the Lambda Gamma chapter hosted the Southeastern Regionals at Clemson University.
summary: Theta Tau is the oldest and largest co-ed professional fraternity for engineers. The Lambda Gamma chapter within Clemson University consists of 70+ active members who constantly strive for professional improvement while developing lifelong friendships.

name: Transfer Council
description: Transfer Council is the branch of student government that advocates for transfer and bridge students. Transfer Council provides events, both academic and social, to help improve transfer students' transition to Clemson University.
summary: Transfer Council is the branch of student government that advocates for transfers students. Transfer Council provides events, both academic and social, to help improve transfer students' transition to Clemson University.

name: Volunteers4LIFE
description:
Volunteers4LIFE strives to provide fun, genuine, and loving volunteers for the ClemsonLIFE program. There are plenty of volunteer opportunities with the ClemsonLIFE students! We would love for you to join our organization to coordinate volunteers and become a volunteer for the ClemsonLIFE program! Please send an email to volunteers4life@clemson.edu to get involved.
https://volunteers4life.volunteerhub.com/vv2/
summary: Volunteers4LIFE strives to provide fun, genuine, and loving volunteers for the ClemsonLIFE program. We seek to empower LIFE students to reach their full potential and raise awareness for the program across Clemson's campus!

name: Clemson Vida Volunteer
description: Vida's Clemson Chapter is dedicated to providing free medical, dental, and veterinary care to needy communities in Central America. Our members gain unique, hands-on, mind-enriching volunteer experiences and an extensive knowledge base in their healthcare field of interest. We fundraise, collect donations, and recruit new members during the school year, all in preparation for our summer trip. Members interested in travel spend several weeks in Guatemala, Costa Rica, and/or Nicaragua working towards our goals of helping fellow human beings and animals, promoting cultural integration, and enlightening members about global healthcare and human issues so they may help solve these problems when they become licensed professionals.
summary: Clemson Vida Volunteer is designed to help impoverished communities in Central America by providing medical, veterinary, and dental services.

name: Clemson Watersports Club
description:
Clemson Watersports is both a recreational club and a competitive 3 event waterski team. The club members have access to a 2023 MasterCraft NXT 20 and may wakeboard, wakesurf, wakeskate, slalom or trick ski. The ski team members have access to the 2025 Master Craft Prostar as well as represent Clemson University at NCWSA collegiate tournaments. There is no experience required to join the team or club and has all the equipment you will need! If you would like to join please fill out the google form at the bottom of the description or email clemsonwatersports@g.clemson.edu

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1cADYeGPQAfyMafDCh6WHr_4X3EdtvrJ9su5FC7ImUBU/edit
summary: We take any and all skill level Clemson students on the beautiful lake Hartwell in our state-of-the-art boats to participate in any and all watersports!

name: Clemson Wesley, A United Methodist Campus Ministry
description:
Welcome! We are so glad you are here!

Clemson Wesley is a Christ-centered community where ALL are welcomed as they come, treated like family from day one, challenged to grow into faithful disciples of Jesus, and encouraged to love God and neighbor in concrete ways.

There are many opportunities to connect with our Christ-centered community every week: 

Wednesday Wesley Worship (with FREE meal and student praise band!)

Weekly Small Groups

Regular social, outreach, and community service events

Outside of worship our community is active and busy, from mission trips to weekly ultimate frisbee games, movie nights and cheering on the Tigers together in Death Valley. 

 

We can't wait to meet you!

Contact Steve (info below) for more information and to get connected. 

Check us out on Instagram @clemsonwesley for upcoming special events.
summary: Clemson Wesley is a Christ-centered community where ALL are welcomed as they come, treated like family from day one, challenged to grow into faithful disciples of Jesus, and encouraged to love God and neighbor in concrete ways.

name: Women's Club Lacrosse
description:
Our program participates in the Southeastern Women's Lacrosse League, or SWLL which is a division of WCLA (Women's Collegiate Lacrosse Associates)

We are a completely student run organization with two president-elects and a full executive board. We currently have two volunteer coaches who are amazing! Our team has many on and off campus events. From fundraising to practice and volunteering, we are fully involved. Since we are completely student run, we do not participate in any sort of recruiting.

Our program has become more competitive with additional traveling over the past few years and every year we continue to improve our standings in the league. Recently we have traveled to Arizona, Colorado, Nashville, and Texas. Most recently we attended the WCLA National Tournament in 2025,  in which we finished 4th in the Nation.
summary: Clemson Women's Club Lacrosse is a student-run team that competes against other club lacrosse teams in the Southeastern Women's Lacrosse League (SWLL). For more information, visit our website at https://clemsonwlax.wixsite.com/clemsonlax.

name: Clemson Women's Rugby Club
description: The women's rugby team is a diverse group of young women dedicated to learning, improving, and contributing to the sport. This organization competes with collegiate level teams along the east coast throughout the fall and spring semesters. We are one of the most successful women's collegiate club teams in the south, and are proud to have a history of dominating our competition. We are always accepting any and all women who are interested in being a part of this sport. No experience necessary!
summary: The Women's Rugby Club is dedicated to providing a rugby experience which fosters a lasting sense of community through a welcoming team environment and successful intercollegiate competition.

name: Women's Club Volleyball
description: We are a very competitive club made up of girls who love volleyball. The club competes in multiple tournaments each semester, which includes the  NCVF National Championships in the spring. We host tryouts each semester and can't wait to hear from you. Go Tigers!
summary: We are a very competitive club made up of girls who love volleyball. The club competes in multiple tournaments each semester, which includes the NCVF National Championships . We host tryouts each semester and can't wait to hear from you. Go Tigers!

name: Wrestling
description:
Clemson Wrestling provides a fun atmosphere as well as a good outlet for wrestlers of any skill level to learn and interact with other wrestlers. We focus not only on wrestling but also on building leadership, growing within our Clemson community, and creating an environment for all members to enjoy! Practices are not mandatory, but the more you come to practice, the more in shape you'll be for our competitions if you choose to compete.

We hold practices:

Sunday 11-12:30

Tuesday: 6-7:30

Thursday:6-7:30

 

For any additional information, contact us at clemsonwrestling@gmail.com or at the phone number provided.
summary: All Pin since 1997

name: WSBF-FM Clemson
description:
WSBF-FM is Clemson’s college radio station! We’re the only radio station in the Upstate dedicated to playing true alternative music that doesn’t have the same broadcasting opportunities as top 100 artists/songs. We’re a loose-knit community centered around friendship, inclusion, and above all, good music! Any and everyone is welcome to join our family! There are countless ways to get involved, such as:

  • Becoming a DJ if you’re interested in broadcasting, chatting over the air, or sharing music
  • Working in graphic design or social media relations within our Promotions team
  • Working with software development or equipment engineering
  • Helping out with live shows, set-up/take-down, audio technology, or videography

 

At WSBF you can be involved with as little or as much as you want. If interested in joining us, feel free to reach out on any of our social media platforms (Instagram: wsbf_fm, Facebook: WSBF FM Clemson 88.1, Twitter: @WSBF_FM, Reddit: u/wsbfclemson) or email to announcer@wsbf.net. Feel free to visit our website wsbf.net to find out more about who we are!
summary: WSBF-FM is Clemson University's student-run radio station.  Broadcasting on 88.1 FM and online through our website wsbf.net, we offer a wide variety of programming. WSBF also puts on live concerts throughout the semester.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated-Eta Nu Chapter
description: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated was founded in 1920 at Howard University. The organization's principles are scholarship, service, sisterhood, and finer womanhood. The Clemson chapter, Eta Nu, was chartered in 1986. We take pride in serving our community and supplementing our peers with life lessons through programming and service.
summary: The Eta Nu Chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was charted on April 26, 1986. We are the only organization of NPHC on Clemson Campus to have uninterrupted service.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
description: Zeta Tau Alpha was created to make a difference in the lives of our members by developing the potential of each individual. We foster strength of character by emphasizing leadership development, service to others, academic achievement and continued personal growth, with a commitment to friendship and the future.
summary: Zeta Tau Alpha aspires to seek the noblest on our campus and in our daily lives.

name: Anthropology Club
summary: Anthropology Club aims to create a space for any students or faculty to discuss and investigate anthropological topics through meetings, events, and more.

name: Chi Sigma Alpha
description:
Chi Sigma Alpha is an international student affairs academic and professional honor society of graduate students, alumni, faculty members, and student affairs professionals committed to excellence in academics, research and service to the profession. The purpose of Chi Sigma Alpha is for its membership to maintain the highest standard of excellence in the profession of student affairs.

Clemson University is home to the Beta Chapter of Chi Sigma Alpha and graduate students must have completed one full semester prior to induction.
summary: Chi Sigma Alpha honors and recognizes professionals, faculty, and graduate students in student affairs for excellence in three pillars: academics, service to the profession, and research.

name: Clemson Gospel Choir
description: We are a 3-part student led choir with the mission of seeking to worship Christ through song and spreading the Gospel. Singers and musicians have the opportunity to come together to perform Gospel songs from various genres. Along with singing, we are committed to mirroring the love of Christ through service opportunities in the community and surrounding areas.
summary: We seek to worship Christ in song and spread the Gospel.

name: Clemson Club Gymnastics
description: Our competitive team members compete at meets throughout the year under the NAIGC. They are required to attend at least 8 practices per month, 2 fundraisers per semester, and 2 meets per year. Our non-competitive team is mostly for members who have never done gymnastics before who just want to learn some new skills and to have fun in a laid-back environment. We practice at Upstate Gymnastics in Pendleton, SC. Practice times vary depending on the day.
summary: Clemson Club Gymnastics allows former gymnasts a chance to get back into the gym and compete at a fun college level. We have non-competitive and competitive teams both made up of women and men. We accept new members of all skill levels.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The Clemson Society of Physics Students is an organization of any students interested in any aspect of all field of physics, including and not limited to astronomy, quantum physics, particle physics, and all others. SPS is interested in providing a friendly and encouraging environment for learning more about physics in a college university setting, as well as increasing awareness of modern scientific and physics-related advances and events. This will ideally be accomplished via public events, talks, and other outreach activities. SPS also provides a pathway by which undergraduate and graduate students in physics can join the national Physics Students Honor Society, Sigma Pi Sigma.
summary: A group of students that have a passion for physics, math, computer science, and outreach.

name: Whitewater Club
description: The Clemson Whitewater Club is located in Clemson, South Carolina. We are here to help you have as much fun as possible while paddling the Southeast's most beautiful and, if you choose so, challenging rivers available. We can help assist you in the development of basic whitewater skills, and expose you to the safety guidelines, risks and dangers associated with whitewater sports. Weekly roll practices and stroke instruction will take place at Y-Beach!
summary: We are dedicated to introducing students to the epic sport of whitewater kayaking.

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description: Alpha Chi Omega dedicates itself to augment the lives of its members through sisterhood, leadership, philanthropy and scholarship. Alpha Chi Omega is a down-to-earth organization where you will find the genuine, passionate, and free-spirited women who make up this group. Here at Clemson University, The Theta Lambda chapter of Alpha Chi Omega is devoted to embody all that Alpha Chi Omega stands for nationally. We strive to be the best image and example of Alpha Chi Omega that we can be. Since its founding in 1885, Alpha Chi Omega has initiated over 230,000 women into our sisterhood.
summary: Alpha Chi Omega takes pride in upholding the standards of character, academic interest, leadership, responsibility, and personal development. As a sorority, we support the fight against Domestic Violence.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated
description: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated was founded on December 4th, 1906 on the campus of Cornell University by seven visionary founders who we call our Jewels. The Pi Alpha Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated was charted on Clemson's campus on February 23rd 1983.
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated promotes brotherhood and academic success through the aims of manly deeds, scholarship, and love for all mankind. Our motto is, First of All, Servants of All, We shall transcend all.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
We are Alpha Phi Omega, a national co-ed service fraternity founded under the cardinal principles of leadership, friendship, and service. We have developed an outstanding service program, working with Adopt-A-Highway, the SC Botanical Gardens, Boy Scouts of America, Helping Hands, Wilderness Way, Our Daily Rest, Habitat for Humanity, and countless other service partners. We also host the largest annual blood drive in the state of South Carolina, the Clemson-USC Blood Bowl!

 

Voted Clemson's Best Service Organization for 2023 and 2024 in The Tiger!

 

Clemson's chapter of APO has been on campus for over 80 years! Our goal as an organization has always been to develop leadership, promote friendship, and provide service to humanity in order to better our community and the world beyond it. While providing service, we strive to create a strong sense of brotherhood by engaging in various social events throughout the year such as tailgates, game nights, formal, intramurals, and many more!

 

If you are interested in joining APO during Spring 2026, please attend our rush events:

Sunday 1/18 | Trivia Night | 7-9 PM | Poole 174
Wednesday 1/21 | Midnight Pancakes | 9-12 AM | Lehotsky Lobby
Friday 1/23 | Project Linus | 6-8 PM | Hendrix David Peebles Room
Sunday 1/25 | Coffee & Cleanup | 12-2 PM | Cox Plaza
Tuesday 1/27 | Formal Informational | 7-9 PM | Poole 174 | *Business Casual Attire*
Thursday 1/30 | 0% Vote | 8 PM | (TBD, please check our insta @clemson_apo) | MANDATORY ATTENDANCE | *Business Casual Attire*
 
All rush events (with the exception of the 0% vote) are optional!
summary: We are Alpha Phi Omega, a national, co-ed service fraternity founded on the cardinal principles of leadership, friendship, and service.

name: Alpha Pi Mu
description: Alpha Pi Mu is an honor society that recognizes top junior and senior industrial engineering students for their exceptional academic success. The organization serves to bring greater recognition to the industrial engineering program at Clemson. Members are involved in several different initiatives which include supporting incoming industrial engineering students, facilitating student mentoring, hosting community-building events, and organizing various professional development events and workshops.
summary: Honor society for Industrial Engineering undergraduate and graduate students

name: Clemson Criminal Justice Club
description: The objectives of the Clemson Criminal Justice Club, an affiliate of the American Criminal Justice Association - Lambda Alpha Epsilon, are: To improve criminal justice through educational activities, to foster professionalism in law enforcement personnel and agencies, to promote professional, academic, and public awareness of criminal justice issues, to encourage the establishment and expansion of higher education and professional training in criminal justice, to provide a unified voice for professionals in, and students of, criminal justice, and to promote high standards of ethical conduct, professional training, and higher education within the criminal justice field.
summary: The Clemson Criminal Justice Club strives to improve criminal justice and foster professionalism in law enforcement. We promote professional, academic, and public awareness of criminal justice issues. We are an affiliate of the ACJA/LAE.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
description: The Clemson AIAS endeavors to promote mentorship, professionalism, civic engagement and supplemental educational opportunities to the students of Clemson University School of Architecture. By actively seeking out and collaborating with complimentary organizations, both within the field of architecture and beyond, the Clemson AIAS strives to create a well-rounded citizen architect. We mentor our membership by facilitating peer-to-peer learning through our Studio Culture Initiative, by engaging our devoted architectural alumni via the Clemson Advancement Foundation for design+build and our close relationship with state and local AIA. We promote professionalism through our interaction with stakeholders in the built environment with lectures and other social events. We engage our community through Freedom by Design, a national AIAS program that provides architectural students the opportunity to address the needs of disabled individuals through design+build, and by crossing the aisle to other student groups to accomplish institutional goals beyond the reach for any single organization. We supplement our educational experience by hosting workshops and professionals in the field to further bridge the gap between academia and practice. In this manner we are a dynamic and responsive voice for our members and for the realm of architecture as a whole.
summary: We are a student led organization that promotes leadership, design, and service by providing opportunities for networking, skills development, and service to the local community.

name: BANGLADESH ASSOCIATION CLEMSON
description: BAC fosters communication, cooperation, and relationships between the Bangladeshi community and diverse cultures at Clemson University. It supports new Bangladeshi students and preserves cultural heritage through events, networking, and community-building initiatives.
summary: BAC fosters communication, cooperation, and relationships between the Bangladeshi community and diverse cultures at Clemson University. It supports new Bangladeshi students and preserves cultural heritage through events and networking.

name: Clemson Club Baseball
description: Welcome to the home of Clemson Club Baseball. As a member of the National Club Baseball Association, our team gives students a chance to compete at the collegiate level. We are proud to say that we are among one of the most successful clubs in the country having reached the South Atlantic Conference regional tournament this past year, the NCBA D1 World Series as recently as 2025, and being consistently ranked among the top 10 in the nation. For more information, feel free to browse our site (https://www.test.optimxsports.com/?team=k8HW5Fk0HtgPeuQMAzgzATkqYyH2) or visit us on X (@clemsonclubbsbl)
summary: Club baseball team, travel throughout the south competing against club teams from nearby universities.

name: Bass Fishing
description: The Bass Fishing club competes against other colleges across the nation in regional and national tournaments. They strive to educate anglers and develop angling skills with an emphasis on bass fishing. The club promotes sportsmanship through athletic competition; they develop and train student athletes for participation in tournament angling.
summary: Bass Fishing Team

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Sciences Honor Society
summary: TriBeta is an honorary society for students who are interested in improving the understanding and appreciation of study in biological sciences. In addition, the organization is committed to service and to increasing knowledge through scientific research.

name: Alpha Omega
description:
We are a campus ministry of truth-seekers focused on living out the Bible. We aim to restore the roots of Christianity by imitating the first century church. Acts 2:42-46

"They devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer. Everyone was filled with awe at the many wonders and signs performed by the apostles. All the believers were together and had everything in common. They sold property and possessions to give to anyone who had need. Every day they continued to meet together in the temple courts. They broke bread in their homes and ate together with glad and sincere hearts, praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. And the Lord added to their number daily those who were being saved." -Acts 2:42-47

The purpose of the organization shall be:
* To courageously advance the inspiring, challenging, and unchanging purpose of Christ to our campus
generation, thus strengthening the church of today and providing leadership in the church of tomorrow.
* Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength. (Mark 12:30)
* Love your neighbor as yourself. (Mark 12:31)
* Be devoted to the apostles’ teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer. (Acts 2:42-47)
* Lay your life down for the church. (Eph. 5:25)
* Seek and save the lost. (Luke 19:10)

Weekly Events:

- Family night midweek (Wednesdays 7:00 PM, congregational midweek once a month Thursday at 7:00 PM, please contact for details)
- Family night dinner, devotional and small event/game (Fridays at 6:30 PM, please contact for details)
- Clemson Foothills Church Worship Service - Sundays at 4 PM at the Clemson Church of Christ Building (167 Old Greenville Hwy)
summary: We are a campus ministry of truth-seekers focused on living out the Bible. We aim to restore the roots of Christianity by imitating the first century church. Acts 2:42-46

name: Canterbury of Clemson
description: Canterbury is a campus ministry of Holy Trinity Episcopal Church, the Episcopal Diocese of Upper South Carolina, and the Episcopal Church. All are welcome at Canterbury, and we require no commitment. Activities and events throughout the year include: Weekly Meal and Worship/Program, Football Tailgates, the Vocare Retreat, Service Projects, and many Others. We welcome anyone seeking a friendly, supportive and accepting Christian community, regardless of background or denomination. Wherever you are on your spiritual journey, we welcome you.  So even if you are just questioning or curious, come and spend some time with us. We’d love to get to know you!
summary: Canterbury of Clemson is campus ministry is a ministry of Holy Trinity Episcopal Church. We are a group of friends trying to be more like Jesus. We have home-cooked meals weekly, and an activity following dinner. Come for food, and stay for fellowship!

name: Chi Psi Fraternity
description: Chi Psi Fraternity is one of the oldest and most respected fraternities in the country. We are a private fraternity of gentleman devoted to the personal and collective needs of its members. We are a selective association of diverse individuals dedicated to the cultivation of a unique environment, which instills a lifelong commitment to brotherhood. Our mission is to create and maintain an enduring society which encourages the sharing of traditions and values, respect for one’s self and others and responsibility to the University and community. Alpha Chi Delta of Chi Psi Fraternity has been an active chapter of Clemson University's campus for 54 straight years and offers members many enriching activities in academics, athletics, service and social life. Brothers volunteer in the community and can be found leading organizations around Clemson’s campus. We are looking for men of character and leadership.
summary: Chi Psi Fraternity is one of the oldest and most respected fraternities in the country. We are a selective association of diverse individuals dedicated to the cultivation of a unique environment, which instills a lifelong commitment to brotherhood.

name: Clemson Collegiate Farm Bureau
description:
The objectives of the Clemson Collegiate Farm Bureau are as follows: •To develop the leadership skill set of young farmers, agribusiness leaders and advocates for agriculture in all academic career paths preparing them for future leadership roles in Farm Bureau, their communities and state. •To inform and assist Farm bureau members in obtaining a working knowledge of Farm Bureau structure philosophy and policy. •To use the program as a tool to improve knowledge and leadership activities of all young farmers, agribusiness leaders and advocates for agriculture in all academic career paths, letting them draw on the collective knowledge of their more experienced leaders. •To serve as a collegiate level advocacy organization for the benefit of members, Clemson University, the college and the state Farm Bureau.

Look us up on group me and follow us on our social media for more up to date information
summary: An agricultural advocacy group that seeks to inform the campus and community of the importance of agriculture!

name: Clemson International Relations Association
description: Clemson International Relations Association aims to bring students together to further knowledge and involvement in various international political, economic, and social events that take place around the world. The organization also aims to improve students' research, writing, and public speaking skills with club activities. Finally, the organization aims to foster a variety of viewpoints to create open and intellectual discussion within members.
summary: Clemson International Relations Association aims to bring students together to further knowledge and involvement in various international political, economic, and social events that take place around the world.

name: Clemson Men's Club Basketball
description: We are a competitive Club Basketball Team that travels to other Universities across the Southeast (Georgia Tech, North Carolina, South Carolina, etc) to participate in competitive tournaments. We have 2-3 practices a week and attend around 3 tournaments a semester.
summary: Clemson Men's Club Basketball gives members an opportunity to participate in competitive basketball practices and intercollegiate tournaments.

name: Clemson Miracle
description:
Clemson Miracle is the largest student-run non-profit organization at Clemson University. We are a part of the Children's Miracle Network Hospitals national movement called Dance Marathon. 100% of the funds we raise are donated directly to our local Children's Miracle Network Hospital, Prisma Health Children's Hospital–Upstate.

 

Clemson Miracle raises funds and awareness for childhood illness through peer-to-peer fundraising, on-campus and community events, hospitality nights with local businesses, community sponsors and business partners, and hosting an end-of-year celebration called TigerTHON (formerly known as Dance Marathon).

 

TigerTHON is a 6 hour long event where students come together to symbolize the daily struggles children at Prisma Health Children's Hospital endure. It includes dancing, games, entertainment, and a total reveal to celebrate our year-long efforts.

 

In 2025, Clemson Miracle raised $107,255, which means that through its fifteen years of partnering with Children's Miracle Network Hospitals, Clemson Miracle has raised over $1.2 million #FTF (For Their Futures). Clemson Miracle has helped fund various projects, including a Healing Garden Renovation, Summer Camp Endowment, The Canine F.E.T.C.H. Unit Endowment Fund (Facility Dogs), Pediatric Intensive Care Unit Renovation, Neonatal Transport Ambulance, and the construction of a new Pediatric Hematology and Oncology Unit. This year's project will continue to be the construction of the new Pediatric Hematology and Oncology Unit!

 

If you are interested in becoming involved in Clemson Miracle, please learn more or contact us at clemsonmiracle.org.
summary: Clemson Miracle is the largest student-run non-profit organization at Clemson University. We benefit our local Children’s Miracle Network Hospital, Prisma Health Children’s Hospital–Upstate.

name: Clemson Players
description: The home of all things Theatre at Clemson University! The Clemson Players student organization is a theatrical community in which members may participate in various productions, professional development, social, and fundraising events. The Clemson Players collaborates with the Performing Arts department’s theatre faculty on theatre productions and provides a supportive artistic network to all members, past and present.
summary: The home of all things Theatre at Clemson University! The Clemson Players student organization is a theatrical community in which members may participate in various productions, professional development, social, and fundraising events.

name: Clemson Ranger Club
description:
The Clemson Ranger Club is an all-volunteer club focused on professional development and discipline for the tactical and technical arts of the US Army. Membership is open to all Clemson University students, including those involved with ROTC as well as civilians.

 

The purpose of the club is to challenge students physically and mentally, promote self-confidence, increase leadership capability, increase military knowledge, and promote overall professionalism and integrity. The Clemson Rangers actively conduct leadership and discipline training, physical fitness enhancement, time management development, small unit cohesion development, rappelling, weapons training, small unit Infantry tactics, close-quarters combat techniques, combat survival training, and advanced first aid. The Clemson Rangers meet weekly for physical fitness training, monthly for administration meetings, and at least once a semester for field training exercises at training sites in the vicinity of Clemson University.

 

Membership in the club is attained by completing a indoctrination phase in the Fall, followed by a series of tests concentrating on physical fitness and specific military knowledge. During the indoctrination phase, prospective members are instructed and trained in areas of physical development and knowledge acquisition. Following successful completion of the indoctrination phase, a prospective member is awarded the coveted "Black Tee," and is formally recognized as a member of the Clemson Rangers. The remainder of the academic year dedicated to training all members.
summary: The Clemson Rangers provide all Clemson and cross-affiliated students the opportunity to join a club in which member’s military knowledge, small unit tactics, leadership skills, self-discipline and character development are enhanced.

name: Clemson ReCyclery
description: • The Clemson Recyclery advances sustainability and bicycle community development in Clemson by providing bicycles to student commuters, offering training in bicycle commuting and maintenance, and actively promoting a culture of active transportation through various community-building initiatives. We would like to encourage and support people interested in bicycling to address many environmental and social issues because of automobile dependency. Promote bicycle riding and commuting as a sustainable, efficient and healthy form of leisure and transportation.
summary: A student volunteer driven, University-supported abandoned bicycle refurbishment program & bicycling education initiative.

name: Entomology Club at Clemson
description:
Note: We formally meet once a month, but just about every Friday afternoon (~3pm) we have a get-together in 308 Long Hall – feel free to stop by! We also have a Discord where we send out additional club information (use the link to join: https://discord.gg/65pTSY4E2S)

      The Entomology Club at Clemson (Ent Club) is open to anyone in the Clemson community with an interest in insects. It was founded over forty years ago with the purpose of promoting environmental awareness and the discipline of entomology to the public. As such, club members participate in Extension and community service projects, including honey bottling and sales, outreach activities such as classroom demonstrations, and activities designed to spark entomological curiosity in anyone who might pass by.

      We sponsor student travel to scientific meetings and have sent teams to the Entomology Games at both branch and national meetings of the Entomological Society of America (ESA), as well as the ESA student debates. Undergraduate members of the Entomology Club have presented posters at these meetings as well, providing valuable opportunities for professional development – in addition to just plain fun!

HONEY AND T-SHIRT SALES

      The Entomology Club is selling 8 ounce jars of honey, supplied by Clemson bees, for $10 each. Clemson Entomology shirts, $15 for short sleeve and $20 for long sleeve, are also available.  Designs are available depending on sizes– we have infant, youth, and adult sizes, but some of them are sold out in some designs. Contact an officer to order!
summary: The purpose of the Entomology Club is to promote entomological interest and scientific development. Outreach opportunities help inform the community about the idea of Entomology and the fundamental processes it involves.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action
description: Planned Parenthood Generation Action strives to fight against oppression on Clemson's campus, nationally, and internationally, as well as stand with the oppressed. The purpose of PPGA is to provide a space for people to discuss and learn about gender equality, LGBTQIA+ inclusion, racial disparities, the concept of privilege, and other intersectional social issues. We hold two monthly events to facilitate discussion and foster action within Clemson's student body according to the feminist agenda. As a Planned Parenthood affiliate group, PPGA additionally focuses on sexual education, reproductive justice, sexual health, and access to sanitation and privacy. The executive board leads with the intention of building a more inclusive community on campus and taking part in feminist activism.
summary: The progressive agenda is to stand with the oppressed and promote equality for everyone. We aim to create a space where individuals can learn, share, and take voluntary action against the social injustices present on Clemson's campus.

name: Fly Fishing Club
description: The purpose for this club and its reason of existence will be to learn and teach others how to fly-fish in both fresh and salt water. Our primary concentration will be the wonderful area of the Southern Appalachian Mountains. The club will also learn the history of fly-fishing and what all it involves. One of the things that make these mountains so incredible is the beauty of the streams and casting a fly into them and pulling out a wild trout. Another purpose is to learn how to go out into the mountains and relax and just enjoy being outdoors in this scenic area. The club will occasionally have individual competitions just for fun. The club wants to take advantage of our university’s geographic location. Our objectives are overall to have fun in the outdoors. The group wants to get out of the stress of our everyday lives and just enjoy being in the wilderness. The club also wants to do all kinds of fly-fishing. The club wants to fish for wild trout and stocked trout. The club wants to catch fish on dry flies, nymphs, and wooly boogers. Each individual trip (time we fish) each member will set their own personal goals. They will vary for each person depending on their personal skill level. Another function of the CU Fly Fishing Club is to participate in local stream restoration and stabilization projects with Trout Unlimited, the nation’s premier coldwater conservation non-profit organization.
summary: The club seeks to use Fly Fishing as a tool to build community through service and conservation.

name: Clemson PrePharmacy Club
description: Clemson PrePharmacy Club is an organization devoted to educating students about pharmacy as a profession. The mission of the club is to promote educational importance, encourage dedication to a pharmaceutical discipline, stimulate contact between undergraduate students and professionals, and bind those with similar interests. The Clemson PrePharmacy Club hosts monthly meetings with guest speakers from various fields within pharmacy and representatives from pharmacy school programs.
summary: The PrePharmacy Club provides students with PCAT study materials, opportunities to network with school representatives/pharmacists, and provide a clear framework for achieving a career as a pharmacist.

name: Clemson Student Chapter of SHRM
description:
The SHRM Chapter at Clemson University holds many purposes. The first purpose of the chapter is to acquaint students, considering a future in business, with the field of human resource management and/or labor relations. Second, to keep students up-to-date on new developments in the field of human resource management. Third, to provide learning opportunities through interaction with human resource practitioners. And lastly, to promote the development of truly professional human resource managers for the future and to encourage adherence to the Code of Ethics of the Society for Human Resource Management. Find out more about SHRM at www.shrm.org.

 

To register as a national SHRM student member, visit https://store.shrm.org/membership. Our Chapter is #5075.

National SHRM members are given priority for shadowing and conferences, can register for the SHRM-CP exam at a discounted price, and are given a cord to wear at graduation.

 

Our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/108716982/bhYjcOU8
summary: Our chapter seeks to prepare students for a professional career in HR by providing networking opportunities with professionals in the field.

name: Club Softball
description: The Club Softball team is looking for passionate and experienced female softball players looking to continue their love for the game. We are a highly competitive team with multiple conference titles, regional titles qualifications for the post season world series, and a world series championship title. We compete in National Club Softball Association (NCSA) in the South Atlantic West conference. We travel a few times to series throughout the fall and spring semesters. Feel free to ask us other questions you may have! Practices: Wednesday 6-8pm and Sunday 1-3pm
summary: Club Softball is for those looking to continue playing softball in college. We travel and compete against other club teams at other colleges and universities in the region.

name: Clemson Collegiate FFA
description: A Collegiate Chapter of the FFA was first organized at Clemson University in the 1930's. The purpose of the organization was to promote the ideals of leadership, personal growth, and career development.The organization also promotes brotherhood and cooperation of students to always have a support system.  During the Spring of 1981, the group chose to realign with the State and National FFA Organization and is now an official Collegiate FFA Chapter. CCFFA will have monthly meetings; dedicated to professional development and fellowship. Participation in the State FFA Convention at Clemson and the National FFA Convention are highlights for the group. Each year the club will sponsor a community service project to be completed by our members in a nearby area. CCFFA also strives to build connections and relationships with local middle and high school chapters. CCFFA is not limited those students majoring in Agricultural Education. All students interested in Agriculture, Forestry, or the Life Sciences are eligible for membership. The Clemson Collegiate FFA abides by the same constitution and bylaws as our national organization which can be found at this link: https://www.ffa.org/documents/about_constitution.pdf
summary: Clemson's Collegiate Affiliation of the National FFA Association centered on Agricultural Education, leadership, and fellowship.

name: Clemson Collegiate Horsemen's Association
description:
Our club was created to help promote the upcoming professionals in the equine industry stemming from Clemson. We accept students of all majors and horse experience levels. Our club is also proud of being well diversified in riding disciplines, hosting members who compete in disciplines including hunters, jumpers, eventing, barrels, poles, reining, dressage, vaulting, therapeutic riding and trail riding.

Each year we participate in learning and networking activities such as hosting guest speakers and traveling to farm tours in surrounding areas. Also, each December we manage the Trotting Round the Tree horse show, which is comprised of open, hunter and dressage competitions. In the spring semester our members have the option of attending the National American Collegiate Horsemen's Association Convention, which changes location with a different host university each year.
summary: We are a professional development club for students interested in the equine industry. Students of any major are welcome to join. We do farm tours, host horse shows, and have a variety of guest speakers.

name: Clemson UNICEF (United Nations Children's Fund)
description:
The purpose of the Clemson UNICEF Club is to give back to disadvantaged communities through service work, to provide professional development and facilitate productive careers, and to help students become better persons who contribute to the world.
