Clemson UNICEF believes in fighting for the rights of children, regardless of gender, sexual orientation, religious affiliation, social status, political climate, or wealth across the 190 countries and territories. We accomplish this through, political advocacy, fundraising initiatives and community and student education. Clemson UNICEF promotes UNICEF’s global work and fundraises to support all the efforts and beliefs presented by UNICEF international.

Join Our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/102518822/YOI8W1dR
summary: Clemson UNICEF Campus Initiative engages with the local community to promote UNICEF's goal of child survival. We do this through campaigns, fundraisers, congressional advocacy, and volunteer work.

name: Council of Diversity Affairs
description:
CODA, initially established as the Minority Council in the 1990s to represent African American students, evolved into the Council of Diversity Affairs in 2001. It now serves a broader mission to address diversity and inclusion at Clemson University, advising the Undergraduate Student Government, encouraging minority involvement across campus, and fostering unity to ensure equitable access to "The Clemson Experience."

CODA organizes events such as Multicultural Dining Days, President's Table, and Blended World Week to promote cultural awareness and address social issues. Joining CODA can enhance your resume, teach professional skills, provide exposure to diverse perspectives, and build a support network.

 

To apply, watch for the online application sent by CUSG at the start of each semester, and contact coda@g.clemson.edu or follow @cusgcoda on Instagram for more details!
summary: (CODA) serves as a CUSG Cabinet Committee under the Student Body President. Our goal is to help promote unity and awareness among Clemson students, where access and equity to "The Clemson Experience" is accessible for all. All students are welcome!

name: Clemson Cru
description:
Cru is a community where we desire to introduce students to Jesus and help students grow in their faith. We accomplish that through a large group weekly meeting, small group Bible studies, and conferences/retreats.

Want more info on how to get involved? Fill out this contact card to learn more! https://ccontacts.app/s/20910
summary: Following Christ and making disciples to help impact the world.

name: Clemson University Dairy Science Club
description:
Clemson Dairy Science Club gathers students with different backgrounds who hold a similar interest in all aspects of the dairy and agricultural industry while forming friendships as well as advocating for the dairy industry.

The Dairy Science Club has two different sections, club and show team. The club participates in events to teach members about the dairy industry. As a part of these events, the club also hosts tabling events to educate the public about the dairy industry. We strive to have two guest speakers with diverse backgrounds to discuss aspects of the dairy industry and participate in farm tours locally and across the state. The club also plans social events as well as fundraising events in order to bring everyone together to benefit the club. 

Show team allows students to halter break and potentially show a heifer at four different shows throughout the year. Two of these shows are local events at the T. Ed Garrison Livestock Arena. The biggest event is attending the South Carolina State Fair where students can not only show their heifers but also enjoy the food and fun the fair has to offer.
summary: Clemson Dairy Science Club gathers students with different backgrounds who hold a similar interest in all aspects of the dairy and agricultural industry while forming friendships as well as advocating for the dairy industry.

name: Delta Zeta Sorority
description: A sorority built on sisterhood and supporting women!
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to unite its members in the bonds of sincere and lasting friendship, to stimulate one another in the pursuit of knowledge, to promote the moral and social culture of its members, and to develop plans for guidance

name: Food Science Club
description: The overall objective of the organization shall be to promote and implement initiatives which foster awareness and advancement of students who are in and interested in the Food Science and Technology major. Development of leadership, professional skills and scholarly excellence between the organization's student and professional members who are in the early stages of their careers shall be among the organization’s highest priorities.The organization shall promote and offer opportunities for participation in an active mutual support network to all of its members.
summary: The overall objective of the organization shall be to promote and implement initiatives which foster awareness and advancement of students who are in and interested in the Food Science and Technology major. Development of leadership, professional skills

name: Gamma Phi Beta Sorority
summary: We are Epsilon Theta Chapter of the Gamma Phi Beta Sorority here at Clemson University, a Panhellenic sorority based on the 4 principles of love, labor, learning and loyalty.

name: Global Medical Brigades
description: Global Medical Brigades, the world’s largest student-led movement for global health and economic disparities, is an international health organization determined to provide sustainable healthcare to underserved rural communities abroad. The foundation currently operates medical clinics in hundreds of local communities across Honduras, Guatemala, Panama, Ghana, Belize, and Greece. Pre-med students and any student interested in volunteering will have the opportunity to gain experience in a medical clinic setting while making a tangible impact in global health. After fundraising, the students will embark on a 7 to 9 day Brigade, shadowing local doctors during patient consultations, supporting medicine distribution within the pharmacy, and leading health education workshops. In addition to hands-on experiences, volunteers also learn about health determinants such as public health, WASH, and economic access while supporting the implementation of a water system and public health infrastructure.
summary: Global Medical Brigades, the world’s largest student-led movement for global health and economic disparities, is an international health organization determined to provide sustainable healthcare to underserved rural communities abroad.

name: Clemson Club Golf
description: Clemson Club Golf is the premier club for all Clemson students who love the game of golf! Our membership is structured into two types: the Social Membership, and the Competitive Membership. Being a member of our Social Membership allows you into a vast network of golfers, with events, golf outings, get-togethers, and countless opportunities to play for fun. Our Competitive Membership allows players to try out for our Competitive Team under the PGA, where players can compete in three tournaments per semester, and in most cases, the National Championship. We are co-ed, and there is no skill threshold (except for the Competitive Team), which makes Club Golf as inclusive and fun as any club could be.
summary: Clemson Club Golf is an organization of Clemson students that are dedicated to growing the game of golf. Our mission is to view this as an opportunity and give everyone the chance to play this great game!

name: Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter
description:
Habitat for Humanity Inc. is a global non-profit organization committed to providing permanent, affordable housing solutions to low-income individuals and families since 1976. With the help of volunteers, partnerships, and donations, millions of families have been able to access the invaluable opportunity to become homeowners and experience the strength, stability, and self-reliance that comes with homeownership.

The students of Pickens County Habitat for Humanity at Clemson (Campus Chapter) strive to produce a similarly positive impact on the local community with the involvement of the student body and Clemson community. In partnership with Pickens County Habitat for Humanity and Clemson University, our student club has built over 30 affordable homes for low-income families since 1993 through the annual Homecoming Build on Bowman Field. During the 10 days of Homecoming at Clemson University, we host hundreds of students to build a Habitat home from the ground – up! Our Student Chapter officers work hard throughout the year to not only orchestrate the logistics of this project, but they also raise the $160,000 needed to build the home! The Homecoming Build is a unique student-led experience that you don’t want to miss out on, and its all for a good cause!

 

PCHFH at Clemson Campus Chapter students continue to volunteer throughout the year past the Homecoming Build on other Habitat homes in the area and have multiple opportunities to participate in advocacy efforts concerning affordable housing.

 

If you believe housing is a human right or are simply looking for an exciting way to get involved on campus, we invite you to join our Campus Chapter! Our work is made possible through volunteer service, donations, and the passion of Clemson students - the impact we make would not be possible otherwise. We welcome volunteers of any and all backgrounds. With this club, you can support your community and meet others that share the same passion for service! 

How to Be Involved: Join our TigerQuest 

Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/112432926/ntRzfiEK

Follow us on Instagram: cu_habitat
summary: Clemson's chapter of Habitat for Humanity's vision is a world where everyone has a decent place to live, so every year we bring together over 500 Clemson students to build a house for a local family on Bowman field in just 10 days.

name: Hermandad de Sigma Iota Alpha, Incorporada.
description: Hermandad de Sigma Iota Alpha, Inc. is a Latina-based sorority whose mission is to uphold the goals set by our thirteen founding mothers. As Hermanas of this precious and rare organization, we work towards expanding the awareness of Latino and other cultures, promoting sisterhood and leadership among each other, and striving for excellence in education and achievement among women. Although we endeavor to constantly work together in achieving our goals, we value personal identity and individualism.
summary: Hermandad de Sigma Iota Alpha, Inc. (SIA) is an organization committed to the life-long goals of solidarity, inspiration, and friendship.  SIA empowers women through sisterhood, diversity, academic excellence, leadership and community service.

name: Clemson Eventing
description: The purpose of this team is to function as an intercollegiate eventing team, which includes participating in recognized intercollegiate team challenges and other USEA recognized events, as well as promoting, fostering, and encouraging participation in equestrian activities and the pursuit of equestrian education. We seek to promote eventing through good sportsmanship, willingness to help and lend advice to eventers both new and experienced, quality performance at recognized events, and a strong team presence at any events team members participate in, including clinics and intercollegiate shows.
summary: The purpose of the Clemson Intercollegiate Eventing Team is to foster the education and training of eventing team riders, and encourage our members to compete and grow in their equine experience and knowledge.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi
description: The Kappa Lambda Chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi was Chartered October 16, 1980 by seven brothers. Their group line name was the Klemson Krimson Seven. The fundamental purpose of Kappa Alpha Psi is "Achievement in Every Field of Human Endeavor."
summary: Founded January 5th, 1911, on the campus of Indiana University, Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity Inc. was the culmination of a vision shared by 10 astute gentlemen who believed in bringing likeminded men together to train them to become leaders in society.

name: Clemson Marketing Association
description:
The Marketing Association at Clemson University is a club open to all students at Clemson; not just Marketing majors. It is designed to bring students with common interests together and to help provide and expose them with the necessary skills to excel in the business world. We host bi-weekly meetings in the business school!

One of the purposes of CMA is to promote friendly relations between the student members, businesses, corporations, and university partners while increasing students' knowledge of the marketing and related fields. Our organization provides students interested in Marketing with resources and opportunities such as networking with informative speakers, information on internships, peer advising, and much more. We encourage members to get to know each other and participate in leadership roles within the association.

At meetings, we feature a speaker from a recognized company or corporation. This allows for our members to have the opportunity to socialize and network with the speaker and to learn about their personal journey through the business as well as any opportunities within the organization. We seek out professionals in marketing-related fields to come and speak about their career in marketing and/or sales.

We love to hear about how they got the position they currently hold at their company as well as advice for college students as we look for jobs and internships.

Dues:

*Dues cover the dinner provided for every meeting & speaker gifts!* 

Semester: $15 to our venmo account @clemson-marketingassociation

Spring semester:

All meetings are held at 6:30pm in the Business School (Powers G019)

See our emails or social media (@clemsoncma) for speaker and meeting info!
summary: The Clemson Marketing Association is an organization designed to expose students to unique business opportunities and careers while helping them establish a strong network of business contacts and important skills to utilize beyond college.

name: MBA Student Association
description: The Clemson MBA Student Association is an organization that provides social, philanthropic and professional activities to MBA students to promote involvement and personal and professional development. It is a way to enjoy time with your fellow students, give back to the community and represent Clemson University.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to organize philanthropic, social and professional activities to MBA students not offered directly as part of the MBA program course-work, but critical to the overall benefit of the post-graduate experience.

name: Men's Club Ultimate Frisbee
description: Our team, the Joint Chiefs of Waft, has been around since 1974, making us one of the oldest teams in the nation. We compete in 5-7 weekend tournaments each year. Most tournaments are in the Spring season, which concludes with the USA Ultimate college series tournaments to compete for the national championship.
summary: Clemson Men's Ultimate is a nationally competitive team that travels around the southeast in both spring and fall semesters to compete against other collegiate teams. Our regular season ends with the postseason series near the end of the spring semester.

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description: The Multiculutral Greek Council (MGC) was recently founded in November of 2015 by two distinct latin-based organizations: Hermandad de Sigma Iota Alpha, Inc. and Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity Inc. Since then, Delta Phi Lambda Sorority Inc. and Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority Inc., have chartered on campus and joined.  These students seek to engage the Clemson community and educate them about the cultural diversity they bring.  The MGC continues to promote unity through diversity by accepting differences and connecting common goals from each organization through brotherhood/sisterhood. The MGC also serves as a bridge between the larger Greek Community and Clemson University. These organizations look forward to collaborating with others associated in Greek Life and the community at large to form a well-rounded Clemson Family.
summary: The Multicultural Greek Council contains four culturally-based greek organizations on the Clemson University campus. This council not only encourages brotherhood and sisterhood, but also works to promote cultural diversity via greek unity.

name: Natural Resources Graduate Student Association
description:
The Natural Resources Graduate Student Association strives to help its members grow academically and professionally and get the most of their Clemson University experience. Established in 2002, the NRGSA works to provide students with opportunities to make new friends, work closely with their department faculty and also create important networking connections in the professional world. We host regular events and encourage all members to attend. Any graduate student enrolled in a natural resources program is welcome to join.

 

If you are interested in joining NRGSA, please fill out this form.
summary: NRGSA is committed to create, develop, and foster interaction between graduate students, staff, and faculty in different departments and disciplines within the field of natural resources.

name: Pershing Rifles Company C-4
description: Founded at Clemson University in 1939, Company C-4 of the Pershing Rifles is a professional military student organization dedicated to preserving Clemson's military heritage through the performance of color guards, 21-gun salutes, funerals, parades, and professional drill routines around the Clemson community. As 11-time national champions, Company C-4 of the Pershing Rifles is dedicated to precision and excellence in the areas of ceremonial drill and exhibition routines.
summary: Pershing Rifles at Clemson develops students professionally, mentally and physically through discipline, drill, and other militaristic principles. We uphold Clemson's rich military history and promote the University's long standing traditions.

name: Phi Gamma Delta
summary: The Chi Alpha Chapter of Phi Gamma Delta is a part of the IFC Council here at Clemson University. Commonly referred to as Fiji, the organization recruits college men who are academicallly motivated, respectful, and seeking a greater college experience.

name: Phi Sigma Kappa Upsilon Septaton Chapter
description:
The brothers of Phi Sigma Kappa Upsilon Septaton Chapter here at Clemson are a diverse group with the goal of living the motto "Do unto others as you would have them do unto you," also known as the Golden Rule. We received our charter in 2005 and have continued to grow as a chapter. Many of our brothers hold leadership positions across campus in different clubs and organizations and we consistently take our academics very seriously, sporting a top GPA amongst the 23 IFC chapters.

Our philanthropic efforts are equally important, as we support the Special Olympics organization through philanthropic donations and hosting a Polar Bear Plunge event. We also perform regular highway cleanups for our "Adopt-a-Highway".

Our Cardinal Principles are to Promote Brotherhood, Stimulate Scholarship, and to Develop Character, while our Core Values are to Demand Excellence, Commit to Lifelong Learning, Meet Challenges with Innovation, and to Value Brotherhood Above Self.
summary: Nationally recognized Social and Academic Fraternity. Member of Clemson's InterFraternity Council (IFC)

name: Clemson Pre-Dental Club
description: The Pre-Dental Club serves as an outlet for undergraduate students interested in pursuing a career in dental medicine. It provides students with numerous opportunities to further their knowledge of the field of dentistry and the expectations associated with acceptance into dental school. The Pre-Dental Club also strives to be active in the community and give back through volunteer service and philanthropic support. The purpose of the organization shall be to establish a community of students interested in the field of Dentistry that will facilitate relationships and provide a support network. We also hope to promote dental health within the university and community at large. We provide a way for pre-dental students to access help with dental school applications, testing, and general knowledge.
summary:
Welcome to the Pre-Dental Club of Clemson University! 
We look forward to working with you as you pursue a career in dentistry.

name: Psi Chi, The International Honor Society in Psychology
description: • Psi Chi, The International Honor Society in Psychology, is a distinguished organization that recognizes and fosters excellence among students, educators, and professionals in the psychology field. Founded in 1929, Psi Chi's purpose is to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in psychological scholarship, while advancing psychological science through research and collaboration. Psi Chi empowers members to grow personally and professionally by providing valuable academic, professional, and personal development opportunities to its members. The mission of Clemson University's Psi Chi chapter is to promote excellence in Psychology by supporting student achievement, encouraging professional growth, and creating a sense of community among psychology students. Our chapter provides Clemson psychology students with meaningful opportunities for scholarly growth, leadership, service, and campus engagement. We are also committed to helping students prepare for life beyond undergrad whether that be graduate school or the workforce. Membership in Psi Chi is by invitation. Clemson's chapter conducts one induction ceremony per semester during the regular school year. Invitations are sent out early each semester and interested students are encouraged to apply.
summary: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: Psychology Club
description:
As a member of Psychology Club, one will be able to immerse themselves into the realm of Psychology. This club operates on the objective of advocating, educating, and exploring the many psychological components that frame our daily lives. Throughout the year, we invite speakers to discuss various topics within the field of Psychology, organize discussion forums, host on-campus activities, and also provide volunteer opportunities. The purpose of this organization is to enrich an individual on the different branches of Psychology, establish networking opportunities, and prepare one for life after undergraduate schooling.

It is not required for a member to be majoring or minoring Psychology; we are opened to all majors, minors, and/or concentrations. We look forward to welcoming you as future members of this club!
summary: Are you a Psychology major, minor, or just interested in psychology in general? If so, come join our club! As a member, one will be able to immerse themselves in the field of Psychology and explore the many psychological components that shape our lives.

name: Clemson Crew
description: Founded in 1988, Clemson Crew is now the largest club sport at Clemson. Our 100-member student-run team competes against other collegiate teams at regattas along the East Coast. Our team practices on Lake Hartwell from 5:00 - 7:00 PM on weekdays, operating out of our boathouse located on Clemson’s campus. Clemson Crew is a co-ed team that provides athletes of any experience level the opportunity to learn the sport of rowing and compete at a high level.
summary: Clemson Crew is a student-run co-ed club sport dedicated to being one of the best club rowing teams in the southeast. In addition to winning on the water, we strive to support our crew family and have fun both in and outside of practice.

name: Clemson Running Club
description: The Clemson Running Club is open for all Clemson and Bridge to Clemson students. The club accepts all types of runners, regardless of speed, distance ability, or running background. We meet Monday through Thursday for a daily run at 5:30PM - however, runs are not mandatory, as you can show up to whatever best fits your schedule. We run on campus, on the roads in the community, and in the forests surrounding the area. The club offers several other options to our members aside from daily runs. If you are interested in racing, we compete in the National Intercollegiate Running Club Association, a collective of D-1 schools with running clubs just like ours. In the fall, the running club participates in NIRCA cross country races and various road races. In the spring, the club participates in NIRCA track and field and road races. We also have a outreach projects working with local elementary schools and other organizations. Fun club activities include team dinners, frisbee, tailgates, hiking around the Upstate, and other cool events. Feel free to reach us at run@clemson.edu for more info. Happy running and go tigers!
summary: The Clemson Running Club is open to all members of the Clemson student body. It's not about how fast or how far, it's about how fun!

name: Scabbard and Blade
description: We foster officer development by offering cross-training opportunities for outstanding Army and Air Force Cadets, rigorous training, academic excellence, and community service.
summary: We are dedicated to developing aspiring and current military officers that emulate the Five Star qualities while fostering strong joint-service relationships.

name: Sigma Alpha
description: Sigma Alpha is a professional agricultural sorority. We strive to promote leadership, fellowship, service, and scholarship among our members. We promote women pursuing careers in agriculture by advocating for and networking within the agricultural industry and providing our members with professional development opportunities. We partake in philanthropy and service events that benefit local, state, and national agriculturalist.  We also participate in social and sisterhood events to maintain a positive and fun environment in the group. Sigma Alpha is a great way to meet girls with similar interests and majors and to form bonds that will last a lifetime.
summary: Sigma Alpha is a professional agricultural sorority with the mission of Cultivating Professional Women in Agriculture. We strive to promote leadership, fellowship, service, and scholarship among our members.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Inc.
description:
Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Incorporated was founded by seven lovely ladies on the campus of Butler University on November 12, 1922 in Indianapolis, Indiana. We are the FIRST and the ONLY black-lettered Sorority to be founded on a Predominately White Institution (PWI). We were also the last sorority to join the National Pan-Hellenic Council which is comprised of 9 Black Greek lettered organizations.

 

My WondRHOful seven founders overcame many obstacles as they prevailed through adversity, fought racism and persevered through sexism while still achieving in all areas of scholarship. Our founders even established this sorority while undergoing constant racial tensions brought on by the leader of the Ku Klux Klan called the Grand Dragon.

 

 All seven of my founders were educators and believed that education was essential to enhancing the quality of life within our community. Today, my Sorors and I take this to heart and strive to enhance our communities on a daily basis with a number of programs catered to our specific communities. I believe as well as others, that education is key to success regardless of age, race, or gender. However, especially in black communities and significantly for our young black females, education can be a tough challenge and excelling in the workplace where we are more likely to be underpaid compared to male counterparts, can be a journey.Public service, leadership development and education of the youth are hallmarks of the organization programs and activities.

 

We have a number of programs to address concerns that impact society educationally, civically and economically. Those programs are geared towards awareness and educating the community such as our A3: Act Against AIDS Leadership Initiative (AAALI) which is catered towards educating the community about HIV and AIDS. Swim 1922 is a national program designed to teach people how to swim, increase swim participation and decrease drowning rates in the community. Operation Big BookBag is specially designed to donate school supplies and mentor children who come from low income homes that don’t have the necessary supplies to learn in school.

 

Recently, our organization has grown significantly over the past few years. We even have Sorors who are achieving great milestones. 2016 MISS USA DESHAUNA BARBER is a member Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Incorporated and is among the few black winners of MISS USA Pageant.
summary: We are the Mu Psi Chapter of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. Our organization was the last sorority to join The National Pan-Hellenic Council, which consists of 9 Black Greek lettered organizations. We strive to uphold Scholarship, Sisterhood and Service.

name: Clemson Event Planners Association
description: The Clemson Event Planners Association is a professional organization that caters to students pursuing a career in the events, hospitality, public relations, and communications industries. Clemson Event Planners Association exists to develop the future leaders of the industry through networking events, professional seminars, and event experience.
summary: Clemson Event Planners Association, or CEPA, is a group of students with career interests in events, hospitality, and marketing.

name: Student Nurses' Association
description: Clemson SNA is an undergraduate student organization that supports our nursing students in building relationships with their classmates, sharing experiences with one another, promoting academic excellence, building a nursing community, and connecting members to outside sources including nurse recruiters and healthcare facilities for possible post-grad employment.
summary: Clemson SNA is an undergraduate student organization that supports our nursing students in building relationships with their classmates, sharing experiences with one another, promoting academic excellence, building community, and connecting members.

name: Tau Beta Pi
description: The Tau Beta Pi Association was founded at Lehigh University in 1885 by Edward Higginson Williams, Jr., to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering, and to foster a spirit of liberal culture in engineering colleges. Our chapter here at Clemson University is South Carolina Alpha. Membership is by invitation only. The minimal requirements for to be invited for membership in Tau Beta Pi are the following: undergraduate juniors who rank in the top eighth of their class and seniors who rank in the top fifth of their class. Invitations are sent out at the beginning of each semester to qualified students. These members must then show character and commitment throughout the semester by going to events and participating in Tau Beta Pi activities. Near the end of the semester these candidates are elected for membership from current chapter members.
summary: Tau Beta Pi is the oldest national engineering society in the United States honoring engineering students who exemplify in academic achievement as well as character. A Tau Beta Pi member will also show a commitment to personal and professional integrity.

name: The Clemson Packaging Science Club
description: Networking, hands-on experience, and education for students studying or interested in the field of packaging science. We network with professionals from industry, professors in the department, and form a community with other students within the major all over the United States.
summary: Networking, hands-on experience, and education for students interested in the field of packaging science and related fields!

name: Tiger Brotherhood
description: Tiger Brotherhood is an honorary service fraternity at Clemson. Tiger Brotherhood promotes high standards of social and ethical conduct, while recognizing in its members an earnest devotion to Clemson, coupled with the integrity of character commensurate with a typical Clemson student. The organization embodies an unequaled cross-section representation of the Clemson community. Students, faculty and staff all work with the bonds of brotherhood to champion a closer relationship. One for all and all for one, with Clemson and its many traditions and undying spirit as the central focus, today provides viable, flexible and a continuing forum for ideas and unending service to Clemson.
summary: Tiger Brotherhood exists as an honorary fraternity to foster a stronger relationship with Clemson through capital projects, service projects, scholarships, student leadership development, and a strong sense of fellowship among Clemson individuals

name: Tigers for Tigers
description: Tigers for Tigers is a student-led organization that seeks to empower students in the world of tiger conservation and welfare. We aim to spread awareness both on and off campus, as well as raise funds to make a sincere effort in the real world.
summary: Tigers for Tigers is a student-led organization that seeks to empower students in the world of tiger conservation.

name: Tigervision
description: Tigervision is the student run TV station at Clemson University! Providing entertainment through movies and student created shows, Tigervision provides a unique outlet for students to be creative and learn valuable skills in TV production, editing, filming, management, and working as a team. Tigervision also provides other organizations and local businesses the opportunity to advertise as well as partner with Tigervision in order to promote themselves. Tune in to Youtube, Instagram, X, and more to check out our content!
summary: Tigervision is the student run TV station at Clemson University providing content to student's faculty, and the Community. Alongside broadcasting we offer sports photography, podcasting, video editing, and more! Check out our content on social media!

name: Clemson Triathlon Club
description:
-Weekly swim, bike, run, and lifting practices

-Stoke clinics, bike handling skills clinic, and bike maintenance clinics

-At least 3 races a semester 

-10 practices required to have race fees covered

-Community service and club collaboration
summary: Clemson Triathlon is an energetic group of individuals who not only love to workout and compete, but also those love to socialize and get involved in the community! We encourage athletes of all levels to join!

name: Clemson Women's Club Basketball
description: Clemson Women's Club Basketball is for girls who want to continue playing basketball at a competitive level. There will be practices 2 times a week, as well as home/away games and tournaments. This club is affiliated with the National Intramural-Recreational Sports Association and will travel to play various schools.
summary:
Clemson's Women Club Basketball team
Practice: Mondays and Wednesdays 8-10
Games: 3-4 Tournaments a semester on away football game weekends or by-weeks

name: Women's Club Soccer
description: We participate in Region 2 of the NIRSA league and attend the regional tournament every fall. We strive to attend nationals every year by having a fun and competitive atmosphere at games and practices throughout the season. League play is only in the fall but we continue to play friendlies and attend tournaments in the spring. We also host our annual Socctoberfest tournament in the fall.
summary: Clemson Women's Club Soccer was created to encourage girls with varsity potential to keep playing at a fun but competitive level. We practice 3 days a week and compete with the goal of nationals in mind!

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description: Alpha Delta Pi was founded as the first secret society for women in 1851 at Wesleyan College in Macon, Georgia. Clemson University's Zeta Nu chapter was founded in 1976. Our sorority is committed to high academic standards, social responsibility, sisterhood, values, and ethics.
summary: Alpha Delta Pi - Zeta Nu Chapter

name: Clemson Sailing Club
description:
**Membership in Tiger Quest does not guarantee membership in the club. Dues must be paid in the designated window at the beginning of each semester before membership is granted.** 

Over fifty years ago, the Clemson Sailing Club was founded by five dedicated students to provide the Clemson family with the opportunity to sail on beautiful Lake Hartwell. While the club’s primary function is to make a place for sailors to enjoy their hobby and sport, prior sailing knowledge is not necessary to be a member of the club. There are many opportunities for members to get involved, whether it is by taking Learn To Sail classes, participating in intercollegiate regattas across the East Coast, or gaining valuable leadership experience as an officer on the club’s executive board. One of the main attractions of the club is the access it provides students and faculty to Lake Hartwell. Located in close proximity to campus, the Sailing Club allows students to relax on or near the water after a stressful day or week of classes. For club members who have no previous sailing experience or those who wish to refresh their skills, learn to sail classes are offered at no extra cost. Many of the club’s most active sailors join the club with no previous sailing experience whatsoever. An Intermediate course is also available for those that have fundamental sailing knowledge but hope to advance to the Clemson Sailing Team. The race team is composed of club members and allows sailors to refine their skills and compete in regattas at venues ranging from New York City, NY, Galveston, TX, San Francisco, CA, and down to Miami FL. Sailing, unlike many others, is a sport in which a sailor can continue to compete at different skill levels throughout his or her life. While college sailing does emphasize competition, the camaraderie of collegiate sailing is second to none. With over 475 active members, the club creates countless friendships that will last a lifetime.

https://youtu.be/zKMaUsydK2s
summary: Home to both a competitive sailing team and a recreational social club, the Clemson Sailing Club is Clemson's only sailing interest club! Come visit us at Campus Beach or send us an email to learn more!

name: Catholic Tigers
description: We are the Catholic Campus Ministry for Clemson University. We offer ongoing opportunities for Clemson students, to live out their Baptismal call, to grow in faith, service, and community, within the life of the Catholic Church.
summary: Catholic Campus Ministry for Clemson University

name: Agricultural Mechanization and Business Club
description: The Ag Mech Club of Clemson University strives to aid in the networking of current, former, and future students to help all those succeed in life.
summary: Our club is designed for people within the Agricultural Mechanization and Business major and other majors who are seeking internship and job opportunities in the agricultural industry.

name: Clemson Amateur Radio Club
description: We aim to encourage the science and art of amateur radio with Clemson students and the Clemson community through education, experimentation and friendship.
summary: We aim to encourage the science and art of amateur radio with Clemson students and the Clemson community through education, experimentation and friendship.

name: Clemson Improv
description:
Mock Turtle Soup is an on-campus improv comedy troupe at Clemson University. Formed in Fall of 2002 by Bryan Buckley and Katie Jones, the organization's goal is to present high-quality long and short form improvisational comedy to the student body. The group is part of the official Clemson student organization Clemson Improv. Mock Turtle Soup is the BEST and ONLY improv troupe to grace Clemson's campus thus far.
 
Important information for this semester:
 
Mock Turtle Soup performs approximately four times a semester on-campus in Lee Hall. MTS also sponsors trips to Greenville & Chicago where its members perform, take classes from professional improvisers, and attend improv shows to increase their knowledge and skill. Members of Mock Turtle Soup practice the art of improvisational comedy 3 days a week through interactive workshops focusing on the different aspects of improv. 
 
To join, everyone must go through our audition process. Auditions are held annually in early September. Typically, there are two days of auditions (you only have to attend one) and then a day for callbacks. Be sure to check out our social media below or email us if you have any questions!
 
Be sure to check out our website! We have a very helpful FAQ for all of your burning questions!
 
Any Clemson student can (and should!) audition for Mock Turtle Soup. We strive to be an LGBTQ+ affirming group and we welcome anyone & everyone to our auditions!
summary: Clemson Improv is the premier organization for improvisational comedy at Clemson University. The name of our troupe is Mock Turtle Soup and we perform 4-5 shows per semester in Clemson's Lee Hall. Auditions are held once annually during the fall.

name: Club Sports Association
description:
The Club Sports Association (CSA) consists of the 39 Independent Student Organizations (ISOs) that are classified as Club Sports by the Department of Campus Recreation. The purpose of the CSA is to provide leadership, support, direction, and equal representation for every Club Sports team at Clemson University. Membership in the CSA consists of individuals that currently serve in an executive board role within their respective club (President, Vice President, Treasurer, etc.). The CSA and its executive board will aim to improve the operations and legislation of the Club Sports program as well as improve the overall experience for Club Sports participants.

 

The CSA typically meets monthly, or as needed. Each team should designate one (1) representative from their executive board to attend CSA general body meetings.
summary: The Club Sports Association serves in an advisory role, and in support of Campus Recreation's 33 Club Sports. Required forms and contact information can be found on this page.

name: Gamma Epsilon Tau
description: The fraternity is a national co-educational honor society focused on graphic arts education. GET provides professional and social networking opportunities to undergraduates and alumni. Prospective members must meet certain academic, moral, and participatory standards to join the organization
summary: Gamma Epsilon Tau (GET) is the honorary/service organization for Graphic Communication Students. As a member of GET, you will have the opportunity to participate in workshops and department events and learn more about your major, faculty, and peers!

name: Indian Cultural Association
description: The Indian Cultural Association (ICA) is an undergraduate club at Clemson University dedicated to spreading awareness of Indian culture to the community. Through events such as Navratri, Diwali, and Holi, we strive to educate and celebrate our knowledge of India and its people, as well as demonstrate the country's diversity in religious, cultural, and social aspects. Furthermore, we strive to provide a community away from home for the Indian undergraduate population.
summary: The Indian Cultural Association (ICA) looks to bring about aspects of Indian culture and heritage to Clemson's campus and community through a variety of different events that help spread cultural awareness of Indian traditions, festivals, holidays, etc.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
description: Our Clemson IISE Chapter’s Vision: To enhance the professional development of Clemson IE students and connect students to the greater IE community.
summary: IISE, the world's largest professional society dedicated solely to the support of the profession, is an international, nonprofit association that provides leadership for the application, education, training, research, and development of IEs.

name: Language & International Health Society
description:
Public health is the science of protecting and improving the health of people and their communities. This starts with awareness and conversation. The purposes of the Language and International Health Society shall include:

1. Spreading knowledge of current public health issues and raising awareness of culturally relevant solutions through seminars, symposiums, watch parties, and other events.

2. Helping students prepare for international travel through contact with guest speakers, graduates, study abroad advisors and other campus and community resources.

3. Providing opportunities for student development through volunteer work, networking, scholarships, leadership positions, and discussions that foster cultural competence and humility. 

4. Fostering a community for LIH majors and students interested in global health by creating a welcoming environment for learning, new ideas, and effective communication.
summary: An organization for those interested in public health issues, different cultures, and helping people. We invite aspiring doctors, journalists, politicians, scientists, nurses, economists, and any student interested in fostering change and understanding.

name: MANRRS
description:
MANRRS is a national society committed to excellence in leadership, scholarship, and service. The organization supports student engagement in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences. With over 70 chapters across high schools, colleges, and universities nationwide, MANRRS offers opportunities for growth through local chapter activities, regional events, and a national conference. These experiences help build strong connections between students and professionals from academia, government, and industry. MANRRS focuses on professional development, networking, and career readiness in a supportive environment. Membership is open to individuals from all backgrounds who are dedicated to advancing participation and achievement in agriculture and related career.

Membership Benefits:

  • Fosters leadership
  • Builds career networks
  • Attend conferences- National and Regional
  • Scholarship opportunities
  • Job and internship opportunities
  • Interaction with industry and science professionals

Fees:

$15.00 annual dues (non travel)

$20.00 dues 

 

Check out the national MANRRS website.
summary: Our Mission at MANRRS is to promote academic and professional advancement by empowering minorities in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
With more than 500 chapters and nearly 16,000 active members in the U.S. and abroad, the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE) is one of the largest student-governed organizations based in the United States.

NSBE, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in 1975, supports and promotes the aspirations of collegiate and pre-collegiate students and technical professionals in engineering and technology.  

 

NSBE’s mission is "to increase the number of culturally responsible Black Engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."
 

The NSBE Torch symbolizes the organization’s everlasting, burning desire to achieve success in this competitive society and to effect positive change in the quality of life of all people. The goal of the Society is to replicate its mission and vision in countries around the world, creating a global network of Black Engineers, scientists and technologists.

 

NSBE has accomplished more for black engineering students than any other organization in the world.

 

If you are interested in joining our NSBE Chapter, Send us a message!

 

http://www.nsbe.org/About-Us
summary: NSBE's mission is to "increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."

name: Phi Kappa Tau Fraternity
description: Phi Kappa Tau is a Fraternity at Clemson University that is dedicated to leading, learning, and serving our brothers and other Greek communities, Clemson University, and our peers nationwide.
summary: IFC fraternity devoted to developing men of character into men of distinction.

name: Student Alumni Association
description: The Student Alumni Association, SAA, is an organization comprised of members of the student body whose primary mission is to gain knowledge and involvement with campus traditions. Each tradition strengthens the bond between alumni, current, and future students who seek to experience the unity of the Clemson Family. The Student Alumni Association aims to get students involved with the Alumni network before graduation to make the transition between current student and Alumni easier after graduation.
summary: The Student Alumni Association, SAA, is an organization comprised of members of the student body whose primary mission is to gain knowledge and involvement with campus traditions.

name: The Tiger
description: Roaring for Clemson since 1907, The Tiger strives to serve the Clemson University community as the student newspaper of record. Currently publishing online each week and printing biweekly, The Tiger is read by students, staff, administrators, families, alumni, and more. From hyperlocal reporting on campus events to coverage of Clemson Athletics, The Tiger's team works day-in and day-out to produce authoritative, relevant and impartial content and contribute positively to campus discourse.
summary: Roaring for Clemson since 1907, The Tiger strives to serve the Clemson University community as the student newspaper of record.

name: The Wildlife Society
description:
Clemson Wildlife Society is a professional/academic/social organization aimed at providing students who are interested in wildlife and wildlife-related careers a means of finding other students on campus who share their interests as well as the opportunity to work hands-on with professionals in the field. This organization provides professional development for its members to help prepare them for their future careers. 

Join us on GroupMe to learn more! https://groupme.com/join_group/102478843/ZNDfvSaS
summary: Our mission is to represent and serve the professional community of scientists, managers, educators, technicians, planners, and others who work actively to study, manage, and conserve wildlife and habitats worldwide.

name: Women's Club Ultimate Frisbee
description:
GroupMe Link

We have three 2-hour practices each week: Monday and Wednesday from 8-10 PM and Thursday from 6-8 PM. We compete in both unsanctioned and USA Ultimate sanctioned tournaments in the fall and spring semesters. Clemson Women's Ultimate welcomes women from various different backgrounds with a wide variety of skill ranges. If you want to be a part of a fun group of people with a competitive edge, we encourage you to come out and see if we are a good fit for you!
summary: Clemson Women's Ultimate is a competitive club sport that travels throughout the southeast, participating in tournaments with other colleges. Whether you have never touched a disc or are a seasoned veteran, we encourage you to come out!

name: William T. Howell Pre-Law Society
description:
*Please sign into TigerQuest to request to join!* The mission of The William T. Howell Pre-Law Society is to further educate Clemson Students about the process of applying to law school, the degree of dedication that law school entails, how to pay for law school, and the benefits of a Juris Doctorate degree. Furthermore, the William T. Howell Pre-Law Society is committed to creating and sustaining a unique environment promoting interaction between prospective law students and fostering prosperous relationships.  

Join our GroupMe to keep up with events and announcements for the 2025-2026 year! 

https://groupme.com/join_group/51336567/nOexi3QZ
summary: Our mission is to further educate Clemson Students about the process of applying to law school, the degree of dedication that law school entails, how to pay for law school, and the benefits of a Juris Doctorate degree.

name: Clemson Athletic Ticket Office
summary:
The Clemson Athletic Ticket office is responsible for coordinating block seating for student organizations. If your organization is interested in blocked seating, please fill out our form.
Questions? Email custudenttix@clemson.edu

name: Beta Upsilon Chi
description: We are the only Christian fraternity recognized at Clemson as an IFC affiliated fraternity. We hold the purpose of establishing brotherhood and unity among college-aged men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ here at Clemson.
summary: We are the only Christian fraternity recognized at Clemson as an IFC affiliated fraternity. We hold the purpose of establishing brotherhood and unity among college-aged men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ here at Clemson.

name: Japanese Cultural Association
description:
The purpose of the Japanese Cultural Association is to extend the experience of learning the Japanese language and culture beyond the classroom. From language/conversation tables to movie and cooking nights, even to the celebration of Japanese holidays, the JCA offers a unique opportunity to learn more about Japan's language and culture. 

 

 Instagram

 

Although this organization has members who are Clemson University students and may have University employees associated or engaged in its activities and affairs, the organization is not a part of or an agency of the University. It is a separate and independent organization which is responsible for and manages its own activities and affairs. The University does not direct, supervise, or control the organization and is not responsible for the organization's contracts, acts, or omissions.
summary: JCA's purpose is to help establish a more complete understanding of the Japanese culture and language at Clemson. We are involved in many cultural events around campus and we hope that you will join! *Japanese language proficiency not required*

name: Psi Upsilon
description:
Lifelong friendship

Psi Upsilon unites its members in a diverse brotherhood of firm and enduring friendship that will last a lifetime.

Moral leadership

Psi Upsilon's system of self-governance nurtures development of mature decision-making, individual responsibility and moral leadership.

Intellectual engagement

Psi Upsilon fosters individual and chapter support for the academic mission of the college and creates opportunities for co-curricular learning and personal development.

Responsible social conduct

Psi Upsilon’s members are guided by principles of responsibility, civility and courtesy.

Service to society

Psi Upsilon encourages service to college, community, country and Fraternity.
summary: United in friendship, Psi Upsilon members aspire to moral, intellectual and social excellence in themselves as they seek to inspire these values in society.

name: Dynamix
description: Dynamix is an auditioned, all-gender a cappella group. This organization encourages Clemson students to express themselves fully and develop relationships with others through music.
summary: Dynamix (formerly Clemson’s Dynamic Harmonium) is an auditioned, co-ed a cappella group. This organization allows Clemson students to collaborate, express themselves, and make relationships with others through music.

name: A.Bevy Collegiate Group
description:
A.Bevy is an introspective organization focused on increasing self-awareness and maximizing potential through events and media! We inspire young adults and light the path to a brighter future, providing the tools to chase dreams in both career and personal interests.

 

We believe the college journey should be transformative, nurturing personal growth and development. We're committed to educational and career advancement, championing mental health and inner motivation. Our community offers a safe space for self-expression, encouraging authenticity, creativity, and the pursuit of lofty goals. Join us in paving the way for a hopeful, growth-filled tomorrow!
summary: Join us in sparking greater self-awareness! Our mission is to empower young adults with hope for a brighter future, the courage to chase their dreams, and the realization of their true purpose.

name: Clemson Club Swim Team
description:
Ranked in the country every year, the Clemson Club Swim Team is a family comprised of Clemson students of all abilities! We have over 100 active members each year, and we practice Monday-Thursday from 8 pm-9:30 pm at the McHugh Natatorium in the Fike Recreation Center. We compete against dozens of other universities across the country!

 

If you would like to find out more information about the team and/or be added to our team email list, email swim@clemson.edu.

 

Due to the Clemson Community's efforts, we were able to start a petition in the summer of 2019 to keep the pool open for the benefit of the community. We were blessed to have such a great network of alumni, students, parents, and Clemson Administration that worked with us during that time of need. Dr. Miller, Dean of Students, was a special asset to us, as he agreed to have a meeting with us and our student body president, Logan Young, to work out how to keep the McHugh Natatorium open.
summary:
Ranked in the country every year, the Clemson Club Swim Team is a family comprised of Clemson students of all abilities!

If you would like to find out more information about the team and/or be added to our team email list, email swim@clemson.edu.

name: Sri Lanka Student Association
description: Welcome to Sri Lankan Student Association at Clemson University! Our objective is to share Sri Lankan culture and values here in Clemson with everybody!
summary:
SLSA is promoting Sri Lankan cultural activities and serving the community
while providing an opportunity for its members to work together to develop personal and professional
relationships, learn leadership and organizational skills.

name: Clemson Roundnet (Spikeball)
description:
Clemson Roundnet welcomes players of all skill levels!


We meet every Tuesday and Thursday at 5:00 PM on the upper IM fields (Bowman Field at the beginning of the year). Whether you're new to the game or a seasoned player, come out, meet new people, find a teammate, and jump into the next game- don't be shy!

Our goal is to create a fun, supportive community where students can take a break from academics, de-stress, and enjoy a couple of hours playing Spikeball. We offer opportunities for those interested in exploring the competitive side of Roundnet, as well as occasional opportunities to get together outside of the club!

Want to get involved?

Click the following link: https://linktr.ee/clemroundnet 

Join our GroupMe where all of our communication takes place, follow our Instagram, and join our club on TigerQuest.

We’d love to have you join the fun!
summary:
Love Spikeball? Looking for a fun, active community?
We have a spot for you! Come hang out, meet awesome people, and be part of a club that’s all about good vibes, great games, and building connections on and off the net.

name: Fraternity and Sorority Life
description: The Office of Fraternity and Sorority Life's TigerQuest page is managed by the FSL staff and is a hub of information about upcoming events sponsored by the office.
summary: Clemson's 48 fraternities and sororities in CPA, IFC, MGC, and NPHC are student led organizations that enhance the student experience inside and outside the classroom in leadership, service, scholarship, and friendship.

name: Clemson Greek InterVarsity
description: Can you be Greek and Christian? We think so! We are a community where being Greek and Christian come together. No matter your Greek chapter or council or your faith background, Greek IV is a safe place for you to explore and grow in your faith and your fraternity or sorority experience. We offer spiritual mentorship, support for chapter Bible studies, and outreach and community-building events. Follow us on IG @clemsongreekiv or contact us to learn more!
summary: Greek InterVarsity is multi-council, multi-chapter, and multi-ethnic community where being Greek and Christian come together with a vision to see every Clemson Greek student one friend away from knowing Jesus.

name: INFORMS Student Chapter at Clemson University
description: The INFORMS Student Chapter at Clemson University is a venue for learning and professional networking. Our members comes from diverse disciplinary backgrounds but share the same interests in Operations Research and Management Science (OR/MS). The Chapter also enables students to go beyond the bounds of course work and institution as they engage in research activities that lay the groundwork for their future as OR/MS professionals.
summary: INFORMS Student Chapter brings together people who share interests in Operations Research and Management Sciences.

name: Clemson Bioengineering Organization
description:
CBO is the Clemson University graduate and undergraduate student chapter of the Society For Biomaterials and the Biomedical Engineering Society. The Clemson Bioengineering Organization is a recognized organization on the Clemson campus with representation in the graduate student government. The purpose of CBO is to support students pursuing an education and career in bioengineering by providing professional development and engineering education support. CBO can provide funding to those students who wish to attend professional meetings, such as the Society For Biomaterials and the Biomedical Engineering Society annual meetings. CBO strives to foster cooperation and networking among members and throughout the Clemson community by sponsoring social and community service events as well.

Click here to join CBO's GroupMe.
summary: CBO is the graduate and undergraduate student chapter of the Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES) at Clemson University focused on providing its members with opportunities for Professional Development, Social Engagement, and Community Outreach.

name: Sister-2-Sister
description: Sister-2-Sister seeks to positively impact the lives of its members through creating supportive networks among women of color, encouraging and assisting with personal and professional development, and providing strategies for educational success.
summary: Sister-2-Sister seeks to positively impact the lives of its members through creating supportive networks among women of color, encouraging and assisting with personal and professional development, and providing strategies for educational success.

name: Pakistan Cultural Association
description: Pakistan Cultural Association (PCA) promotes awareness and appreciation of Pakistani culture at Clemson University through cultural events, social gatherings, and educational activities. PCA provides a welcoming space to celebrate traditions, food, music, and community.
summary: Pakistan Cultural Association promotes awareness and appreciation of Pakistani culture within the Clemson community by creating fun, inclusive opportunities to explore and celebrate Pakistan’s traditions, heritage, and values.

name: Clemson Economics Club
description:
The Clemson Economics Club is dedicated to providing students with a platform to explore, discuss, and deepen their understanding of economics. Through guest speakers, networking events, career workshops, and collaborative discussions, the club empowers students who are passionate about economics to expand their knowledge, engage with real-world applications, and connect with like-minded peers. Whether you're pursuing a career in economics or simply curious about how the world works, the club offers opportunities for intellectual growth and professional development.

If you are interested in joining please send an email to jraup@g.clemson.edu, and give a brief description of what about the club interests you.
summary: The Clemson Economics Club is dedicated to providing students with a platform to explore, discuss, and deepen their understanding of economics.

name: Clemson Table Tennis Club
description:
The Clemson Table Tennis Club serves as an organization where both competitive and recreational table tennis players can practice, play, and compete.

FIKE LOWER FITNESS STUDIO - all the info you need is on our Instagram
summary:
The Clemson Table Tennis Club serves as an organization where both competitive and recreational table tennis players can practice, play, and compete.

FIKE LOWER FITNESS STUDIO - all the info you need is on our Instagram

name: Clemson University Tour Guides
description:
The Clemson University Tour Guides, founded in 1984, is a select group of students who serve the University as goodwill ambassadors to campus, providing a variety of educational and public relations services.

Tours are conducted from the Nieri Family Alumni and Visitors Center on a regularly scheduled basis five-six days a week, excluding Sundays and selected holidays. Other services are handled upon request and include athletic and special tour needs.

Tours serve as a general introduction to Clemson University. The campus tour should be an enjoyable experience for our visitors. Guides should put themselves into the tour and personalize it with bits of information and anecdotes. These things make the tour more than just a routine walk around campus.

Members are required to participate in continuing guide education/training programs, regularly scheduled meetings, and strongly encouraged to participate in activities that surround the University, so that they are more knowledgeable of the surrounding areas and are better equipped to answer questions from our visitors.

The Clemson University Guide Association is recognized by the Executive Office, Alumni Association, and University administration as a leading organization at Clemson University. In addition, the organization is recognized as a Delegated Student Organization (DSO) by the Division of Student Affairs.

If you are interested in applying, check our Instagram page for more information on our fall application process!
summary: The Clemson University Tour Guides is a student organization whose members serve as University-sponsored tour guides. Guides are responsible for giving all campus tours through the Nieri Family Alumni and Visitors Center.

name: Clemson Skydiving Club
description:
The skydiving club allows Clemson students to experience the thrill of skydiving. We are one of the oldest clubs at Clemson, established in 1959. We work with students and our local dropzone to provide a safe, supportive place for students where they can learn, practice, and compete in the sport.

 

If you are interested in joining the club send a dm on our instagram @clemsonskydiving
summary:
The Clemson Skydiving club is an organization dedicated to helping Clemson students learn how to skydive safely. We jump locally and work with our dropzone to provide student licensing programs.

Message us on Instagram @clemsonskydiving

name: Clemson University Makerspace
description:
Check out our website to get started!

https://www.cumaker.space/
summary: The Clemson Makerspace provides a safe, collaborative environment for students to work together on scientific, creative, or artistic endeavors. Currently the Makerspace provides students with access to 40 different 3D printers, 2 laser cutters, and more!

name: Clemson University Student Government
description:
As the official representative of the Clemson University Undergraduate Student Body, Student Government was created to be and still acts as the liaison between the students and the administration, faculty and staff. From its inception in 1957, Student Government has sought to be the chief student advocate in all areas of campus life. In addition to serving as the governing body of the student population, Student Government also participates heavily in the daily affairs of the University. Through interaction with University officials and representation on University committees and councils, Student Government representatives provide continuous input to the administration and offer many recommendations with regard to student sentiment. Student Government also provides a wide array of services and programs for the mutual benefit of all Clemson students.

 

Every Clemson student is represented through the elected and appointed members of the Executive, Legislative, and Judicial Branches of Student Government, and those representatives welcome and encourage visits from students who wish to voice their ideas, opinions, and concerns. As the chief student advocate on campus, Student Government is dedicated to the service of all Clemson students and the enhancement of the Clemson experience. The active student input is the most vital ingredient in the functioning of the Clemson University Student Government.
summary: There are several times throughout the year that students can join Clemson’s Undergraduate Student Government. Each of the three branches have their own procedures and times for adding new members.

name: Gantt Center
description:
About Us

The Harvey and Lucinda Gantt Center is committed to creating learning environments that foster the holistic development of our students.
summary: The Harvey and Lucinda Gantt Center is committed to creating learning environments that foster the holistic development of our students.

name: Genetics and Biochemistry Graduate Student Association
description: The Genetics and Biochemistry Graduate Student Association strives to serve the needs of graduate students in the Genetics and Biochemistry department in two particular ways. First, we aim to advocate for graduate students by collecting information on the needs and desires of students, then interface with the department and college level administration to pursue and implement solutions. Second, we recognize the importance of community, and therefore are invested in fostering it. With this in mind, we foster community by planning various social events, formal or informal, that offer opportunities for graduate students to get to know each other.
summary: The GBGSA pursues two goals. We desire to communicate and pursue the needs of graduate students with the departmental and college level administrations as well as develop community among graduate students.

name: Pi Beta Phi
summary: Founded in 1867, the mission of Pi Beta Phi is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service.

name: Young Americans for Liberty
description: Our goal is to spread the ideas of liberty, free markets, and limited government here at Clemson and bring together students who care about those values. We’ll put on events like speaker talks, activism projects, and hangouts to grow the chapter and get more people involved. Join here today.
summary: Young Americans for Liberty (YAL) is the largest, most active, and fastest-growing pro-liberty organization on America's college campuses. YAL seeks to identify, educate, train, and mobilize young people committed to winning on principle.

name: Clemson Hope
description: Clemson Hope is a student-run nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering students and teachers by meeting the basic needs of local Title 1 Elementary Schools. We partner with James M. Brown Elementary School, Westminster Elementary School, and Orchard Park Elementary. These schools have a student population of more than 60% below the poverty line. Our organization provides mentoring and pen-pal programs to uplift and support these students. Additionally, for our largest program, Adopt-a-Classroom, we collaborate with the local community to provide each student at our three partnered schools with a hand-wrapped Holiday gift. 1,400 gifts are given to these amazing students as well as Holiday gifts to the teachers!
summary: Clemson Hope is a non-profit organization dedicated to empowering students and teachers by meeting the needs of local Title 1 Elementary Schools. We partner with three local schools to provide mentoring programs and events for at-risk students.

name: Clemson University Virtual Reality Club
description:
Join us on Discord!

The Clemson University Virtual Reality Club provides access to virtual reality hardware and software for the entire Clemson Community through weekly club meetings, facilitation of a virtual reality student center called The Immersive Space, and various outreach initiatives across campus and in the local community. At club meetings, held weekly in the Watt Family Innovation Center in room 203, we foster a community of student involvement through gaming and competitive esports, development nights and hackathons, as well as bringing in industry speakers throughout the semester to discuss how virtual reality is changing how they approach real world problems. 

 

The Immersive Space (a fully student-led VR center located in WFIC 203) is open Monday 10am–2pm and Tuesday–Friday 12pm–5pm (24 hours/week) for demos, projects, and study. The goal of the Immersive Space is to provide access to virtual reality technology for everyone here at Clemson, and make the technology readily available for all student needs.

 

Our outreach programs stretch all across campus partnering and teaming up with other organizations to facilitate virtual reality nights, and we also have efforts off campus bringing this virtual reality technology to local schools and providing the younger generation hands-on experiences with technology of the future.

 

More information on our club can be found on our website, vrclub.people.clemson.edu , by contacting us via email @Virtual Reality Club , by simply stopping by the Immersive Space, or on Tigerquest.
summary: This organization serves to educate the Clemson University community on the uses of virtual reality, to provide an environment within which to experience virtual reality, and to support and explore projects using virtual reality.

name: College of Science Student Advisory Board
description: SciSAB (The College of Science Student Advisory Board) selects department representatives who serve as liaisons between department leaders and students to pursue initiatives to improve the experiences of students in their department. In addition, SciSAB members collaborate with other departments within the College of Science and across the university as a whole to organize outreach events in both the campus and broader community. We recruit members as positions open up and actively recruit undergraduate students.
summary: We seek to improve the experiences of students in the COS through departmental, college, and university-wide initiatives. We select members as positions open up in each department and are open to grads and undergrads looking to make a positive change!

name: Clemson's Welcome Week
description:
Weeks of Welcome is an extended Orientation program aimed at helping students learn “how to college” and “how to Clemson” as they transition to campus. Programming is designed to properly equip you with necessary tools and relationships to guide you through your first semester and beyond.

Weeks of Welcome Leaders are student volunteers who work directly with groups of new students, helping them navigate campus, build connections, and feel welcomed throughout Welcome Week and all six Weeks of Welcome. As a Weeks of Welcome Leader, you play a unique role in helping plan and implement Weeks of Welcome events. While you will spend the majority of your time assisting in the traditional first week of programming (Welcome Week), you also have the opportunity to serve as a resource to students throughout the entire semester.

 

If you’re interested in being a Weeks of Welcome Leader, be sure to complete the application under “Forms” by March 23rd at 11:59 PM. 

 

Weeks of Welcome Program Details

Weeks of Welcome programming is available throughout the first six weeks of your semester, starting as new students as you move in. From signature events like New Student Convocation, Welcome Rally, Clemson Leader Day, and Class Photo, to skill-based workshops like Money Management 101, Navigating Conflict and Healthy Habits, as well as community-oriented events like our Clemson Community Partner Fair and Day of Service, Clemson’s Weeks of Welcome exist to fully immerse new students into the Clemson community and equip them to be successful.
summary: Clemson's Welcome Week serves to welcome new first year students through specific social and academic events.

name: Chi Phi Fraternity
description: The Chi Phi Fraternity is the oldest social college secret Fraternity in north America. Founded in 1824 at the College of New Jersey (now named Princeton University), our mission is to build better men through lifelong friendships, leadership opportunities, and character development.
summary: We are the Chi Phi Fraternity and our mission is to build better men through lifelong friendships, leadership opportunities, and character development.

name: Clemson Club Cheer
description:
Clemson Club Cheer is an organization that works to promote cheerleading as a sport as well as school spirit and teamwork. The club offers undergraduate students, who are passionate about competition cheerleading, the opportunity to work on skills and/or compete at the national level. Whether interested students cheered in prior years or are just looking to try something new, Clemson Club Cheer is open to all. We have two teams. 

Orange Team: Our competitive team that competes at NCA Nationals in addition to local showcases/competitions. 20 full time members along with alternates are taken onto the Orange team. You must be a Clemson student to be considered for the Orange team.

Purple Team: A non-competitive, practice team for members to maintain their tumbling and stunting skills throughout the season without any strict commitment. This team is ideal for members looking for a lower time commitment or bridge students. The Purple team’s status is dependent on the level of interest shown by athletes both at and after tryouts.

Follow our social media to stay up to date with our team! 

Fill out our interest form HERE!
summary: Clemson Club Cheer is an organization that was founded to promote cheerleading as a sport as well as school spirit and teamwork. The club competes at the national level and works to join students together who share a passion for cheerleading!

name: Collegiate DECA at Clemson
description:
We are a chapter of the national DECA organization. Through this business organization, students are provided with networking opportunities as well as competition. We aim to empower students of all disciplines to be better leaders and improve their soft skills. Being a member of this chapter also enables students to attend leadership conferences as well as apply for scholarships offered by DECA.

Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/101441836/sT0k6ojN
summary: A chapter of the national DECA organization centered at Clemson University that provides students the opportunity to network and compete with peers from other colleges in business related challenges

name: Action Shooting Sports Club
description:
