The Clemson Action Shooting Sports Club is designed to provide an opportunity for members to improve their practical shooting skills while practicing firearm safety and creating and fostering a campus interest in competitive shooting. Any skill level is allowed at practices. For those that do not have their own firearms, the club has firearms for you to use. We meet every Tuesday and Wednesday from 4:15-5:45pm at Pickens Bend Range.

Please email us for any questions. We only accept TigerQuest requests for active and signed up members.

 

Although this organization has members who are Clemson University students and may have University
employees associated or engaged in its activities and affairs, the organization is not a part of or an agency ofthe University. It is a separate and independent organization which is responsible for and manages its ownactivities and affairs. The University does not direct, supervise, or control the organization and is not responsiblefor the organization’s contracts, acts, or omissions.
summary: The Clemson Action Shooting Sports Club is a student organization for those who participate in pistol shooting competitions such as USPSA, Steel Challenge, and Scholastic Action Shooting Program. All shooters and skill levels are welcome.

name: Clemson Wiffle Ball
description: Clemson Wiffleball Club is a community of recreational and competitive Wiffleball enthusiasts. The club currently has over 400 active co ed members. Please contact us about getting involved and check out our Instagram link to learn more! Our main goal is for YOU to have fun and form great relationships that will last a life time. Make sure to follow the instagram @cu_wiffleball for news and updates.
summary: Clemson Wiffleball Club is a community of recreational and competitive Wiffleball enthusiasts. The club currently has over 400 active co ed members. Please contact us about getting involved and check out our Instagram link to learn more!

name: Phi Delta Epsilon
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon provides experience, information, and essential skills while allowing members to improve their networks and accompany themselves with many pre-medical students.  The prestigious pre-medical fraternity was founded in 1908 at Cornell University and has continued to improve the character of the medically-acclimated students that it admits.

 

If you are interested in joining PhiDE, please fill out the form below to join our interest list. Once you join the interest list, you will receive information about recruitment and informational meetings. 

Interest Form for Spring 2026
summary: PhiDE is a pre-medical undergraduate fraternity that looks to bind together its members with the common interest of pursuing medical school.

name: Student Chapter of the Institute of Management Accountants at Clemson University
description: IMA membership provides opportunities for students to associate with business professionals; to see how they live, work, and apply their knowledge; and to learn that professionals are seriously devoted to ethical standards and to the advancement of their profession.
summary: Institute of Managerial Accounting, introducing Accounting and Finance students into the world of Managerial Accounting and Manufacturing in the Corporate world.

name: Minority Business Student Association
description:
The Minority Business Student Association is dedicated to the professional, academic, and personal experiences of its members and the recruitment of minority business students into the College of Business. We strive to develop diverse leaders and maximize their career potential in business.

YouTube Video
summary: The Minority Business Student Association is dedicated to the professional, academic, and personal experiences of its members and the recruitment of minority business students into the College of Business.

name: Career Ambassador Program
description: Career Ambassadors are student leaders who serve fellow students in the Center for Career and Professional Development. Through in-depth training and guidance in professional development, career ambassadors provide peer advising to undergraduate students on career related topics. In addition, they provide a friendly face for both the Michelin Career Center, Clemson University and to all patrons, including employers recruiting on campus during the fall and spring semesters. The career ambassadors not only help peers with items like resumes and cover letters during drop-in hours, but also create resources and initiatives for the Center for Career and Professional Development that are utilized for years to come.
summary: The Career Ambassador Program through the Center for Career and Professional Development allows undergraduate and graduate students to serve as ambassadors to provide peer advising on career related topics through a variety of avenues.

name: Tigers for Babies
description:
The mission of the March of Dimes is to better the health of babies by preventing premature birth, birth defects, and infant mortality. Because 1 in 9 babies in SC are born premature, we aim to better those numbers for families. This organization gives Clemson University students the opportunity to help provide a fighting chance for every baby.

Through our efforts to raise awareness on campus, this group will allow students to get hands on experience with working for an official non-profit. The work that this club does helps the March of Dimes raise critical life saving funds for families and babies everywhere.
summary: Tigers for Babies is Clemson University's Official Club supporting the March of Dimes. This group of students will assist in raising awareness and funds for the March of Dimes urgent mission to better the health of babies.

name: Clemson Neuroscience Club
description:
Our Mission at Clemson Neuroscience Club is to spread knowledge of and affection for Neuroscience across campus, and to foster relationships between students, faculty, and professionals.

This is done by holding weekly meetings that include group discussions of member-chosen topics, presentations by guest speakers (or willing club members), tours of labs on campus, and opportunities to engage in neuroscience events outside of Clemson, such as conferences hosted by the Society for Neuroscience.

This club strikes a balance of two things:

1. Casual discussions that are always welcoming to new members, because everyone should learn how their brain works!

2. Providing additional social and academic support for those intending to develop professional skills and connections to aid in future work in neuroscience, the medical field, psychology, or anything related.
summary: The mission of the Neuroscience Club is to increase awareness of the field of neuroscience, encourage exploration of the field, and create relationships between students, faculty, and professionals.

name: Clemson Badminton Club
description:
The Clemson Badminton Club is open to all students and faculties of all levels. Members are welcome to come and play on Court 4 & 5 at the Fike Recreation Center during the badminton open rec. hours. The open rec. hours for Spring 2026 is listed below.

Friday          6 pm - 10 pm

Saturday     11 am - 2 pm
summary: The Clemson Badminton Club is open to all students and faculties of all levels for the purpose of recreation, physical fitness, and the development of skills in the sport.

name: Clemson Horticulture Club
description: The functions of the horticulture club are to expand student’s knowledge and awareness as well as their passion for horticultural topics. This will be accomplished through monthly meetings, field trips, guest speakers, volunteering, as well as engaged discussions with peers and professors.
summary: The Horticulture Club is dedicated to learning and teaching about the diverse realm of the horticultural sciences. It strives to broaden members' perspectives and experiences in the agricultural sciences as well as provide awareness.

name: Brother-2-Brother
description:
Brother 2 Brother, Clemson University's chapter of the Student African-American Brotherhood (SAAB), focuses on the success, retention and graduation of Black and Hispanic/Latino males. B2B is a dynamic organization established specifically to assist our participants to excel academically, socially, culturally, professionally and in the community. SAAB is primarily comprised of male students who strive for academic excellence and make a commitment to plan and implement programs that benefit their community at large. We encourage our participants to embrace leadership by being positive examples for each other through a strong commitment to academic achievement, brotherhood and community service.

Brother 2 Brother is a student led initiative at Clemson University that prepares men of color for their roles as citizens and leaders through community-based educational and service experiences. Active members of Brother 2 Brother are engaged in a meaningful way, through promotion of academic achievement, character building, fostering leadership skills and encouraging empathy and civic responsibility for all students on Clemson's campus.
summary: Brother 2 Brother, Clemson University's chapter of the Student African-American Brotherhood (SAAB), focuses on the success, retention and graduation of Black and Hispanic/Latino males.

name: Clemson Chinese Language Club
description:
Our Chinese Language Club is a vibrant and diverse group dedicated to exploring and promoting the Chinese language and culture. Our activities encompass language learning, cultural exchanges, and celebrating traditional holidays. Whether you are a beginner or a fluent Chinese speaker, you are welcome to join us. In the Chinese Language Club, we delve into the essence of the Chinese language and culture through courses, lectures, research, and social activities. Whether it's learning Chinese characters, savoring delicious Chinese cuisine, watching Chinese movies, or hosting traditional Chinese New Year celebrations, we are committed to creating a friendly and interactive environment to facilitate Chinese language learning and cultural experiences. Whether you are interested in Chinese or seeking to connect with like-minded individuals, the Chinese Language Club is a fun and inspiring place to be.

我们的中文俱乐部是一个充满活力和多样性的团体，致力于探索和推广中文语言与文化。我们的活动范围涵盖了语言学习、文化交流和庆祝传统节日等各个方面。无论你是初学者还是流利的中文使用者，我们都欢迎你的加入。在中文俱乐部，我们通过课程、讲座、研究和社交活动，共同深入了解中国语言和文化的精髓。无论是学习汉字、品尝美味的中餐、观看中国电影，还是举办传统的春节庆典，我们都致力于创造一个友善和富有互动的环境，以促进中文学习和文化体验。无论你是对中文感兴趣，还是希望与志同道合的人建立联系，中文俱乐部都是一个充满乐趣和启发的地方。

https://groupme.com/join_group/96130035/KaKtPIVk
summary: We aim to share the Chinese culture with more people, whether it's traditions, music, food, etc.

name: Frontline Clemson
description: Frontline equips cadets and military leaders in unique aspects of faith-based leadership in ways that "change the temperature in the room" as well as create a safe for cadets who are struggling. This is an organization open to all students but focuses mainly on those who will be entering in to the military after college.  The goal is to develop servant leaders who understand the importance of trusting God, building community, and forging resilience.
summary: Frontline Clemson is a faith-based leadership organization centered around the Clemson ROTC programs. Our mission is trusting God, building community, and engaging brokenness.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
description:
IEEE-Eta Kappa Nu (IEEE-HKN), the honor society of IEEE, is dedicated to encouraging and recognizing individual excellence in education and meritorious work, in professional practice, and in any of the areas within the IEEE-designated fields of interest.

In addition to excellence in character, HKN members are distinguished as the top fifth of sophomores, top quarter of juniors, and top third of seniors.
summary: Electrical and Computer Engineering Honors Society

name: Clemson University Graduate Student Government
description:
The Graduate Student Government (GSG) is the official representative of Clemson University’s graduate student body. We are here to ensure that you have an amazing and memorable graduate student experience at Clemson University.

As the official voice of graduate students, we are the outlet for graduate students to enact change and improve this university. We also provide engagement opportunities for graduate students and their families through numerous events and activities both on the main campus, Greenville, and other satellite campuses throughout the state of South Carolina. Student life at Clemson University is defined by the student body who bring our campus to life. We believe that no matter where you devote your time at Clemson University, your opinions should be heard. Your voice matters.

GSG is dedicated to the service of all graduate students and the enhancement of their Clemson experience by helping provide opportunities for personal and professional growth. Having active student input is the most vital ingredient in our functioning.
summary: The Graduate Student Government (GSG) is the official representative of Clemson University’s graduate student body. We are here to ensure that you have an amazing and memorable graduate student experience at Clemson University.

name: Black & Gold Club
description: The Black & Gold Club is designed to serve as the primary booster club for Clemson University Army ROTC and provide opportunities for cadets in the realms of leadership, budgeting, and professional development.
summary: The Black & Gold Club is designed to serve as the primary booster club for Clemson University Army ROTC and provide opportunities for cadets in the realms of leadership, budgeting, and professional development.

name: CUhackit
description: CUhackit aims to create a diverse, inclusive, and empowering environment for students of all backgrounds to come together, collaborate and learn new skills in computing and engineering. Our goal is to foster a sense of community, where students can share their knowledge, develop their skills, be inspired by the innovation and creativity of their peers, and engage with industry. Together, we strive to drive positive change in the tech industry by empowering the next generation of professionals to be inclusive, innovative, and impactful.
summary: CUhackers promotes innovation and creativity to its members and Clemson University students through the planning and organization of hackathons.

name: Believers in Christ at Clemson
description:
Believers in Christ at Clemson University is a student group composed of believers from different backgrounds who want to grow in their Christian life together. We stand absolutely for the faith common to all Christian's. Our goal is to build up the body of Christ together in the love of God (Jude 1:20-21). We are a non-exclusive club and have no denominational affiliation. As Christian students, we desire to give Christ the first place in all things (Colossians 1:18) and we do this through Bible studies, home fellowship, seminars, retreats, and much more.

Please check out our Instagram Page to see upcoming events, things we have done in the past, and learn more about our club!
summary: Believers in Christ at Clemson University is a student group composed of believers from different backgrounds who want to grow in their Christian life together. Our goal is to build up the body of Christ together in the love of God (Jude 1:20-21)

name: Theta Chi Fraternity Eta Alpha Chapter
description:
Theta Chi is one of the oldest men’s college fraternities in North America. Founded in 1856, more than 180,000 members have enjoyed the Fraternity’s traditions, ideals, and fellowship.

Founded on the principle of friendship through the mutual assistance of every member, Theta Chi aims to provide its members with an enjoyable and fulfilling collegiate experience that also prepares them for rewarding, successful lives after graduation.

Our undergraduates are leaders on campus, in the classroom, and on the field. We invite you to learn more about who we are and what we do.
summary: We are a fraternity on campus founded on the ideals of The Sacred Purpose, Resolute Men, and The Helping Hand.

name: Tigers PA Club - INACTIVE SP26
description:
Tigers PA Club is an organization dedicated to equipping Clemson students for a career as a Physician Assistant through education about prerequisite requirements, networking with PAs, and service opportunities for healthcare experience. We hope to build a community of students that is eager to become the next generation of healthcare professionals. 

Join us at our next meeting! Click on the link below to join our email list (link is also in our Instagram bio). Our weekly emails will tell you about our semester events and other helpful links to assist you on your journey to PA school. Dues are $5 per semester, and will verify your membership upon payment. Members have special perks, including access to documents concerning your pre-PA journey and meeting notes. Email us to learn more about becoming a member!

https://linktr.ee/pawsforpa

Keep up with us on social media to learn about our upcoming meetings! 

Instagram: @tigerspaclub

Email: cupaws4pa@gmail.com
summary: Tigers PA Club is a student led organization for undergraduate students pursuing a career as a Physician Assistant.

name: Clemson Cru Grad
description: Clemson Cru Grad is a community of Graduate Students at Clemson University connecting deeply with God, one another, our calling and the mission of helping others do the same.  We seek to accomplish this by providing an environment for graduate students to meet one another, study the Bible together, and interact on a social basis. Our goal is for this community to help Christian graduate students thrive during their time at Clemson.
summary: Clemson Cru Grad exists to provide a place both for Christian grad students to connect with each other, and grads who are inquisitive about Christianity to find answers. We accomplish this through weekly Bible studies, mentoring, and social activities.

name: Clemson Film Club
description:
Clemson Film Club introduces students to the filmmaking process by meeting weekly to work on video projects. Club activities include writing screenplays, storyboarding, location scouting, filming days, and post-production workshops. We are open to students of all major and skill level!

 

Come join our Discord to chat with us! https://discord.gg/RmCWbAq

 

We meet on Thursdays at 8 PM in the Watt Center. Stop by if you'd like to join, or shoot us an email at clemsonfilmclub@gmail.com!
summary: We are student filmmakers. Our organization focuses on providing students access to cinematography and the film production process.

name: The Hills Retreat
description: The Hills Retreat is a retreat in the summer before freshman year. Its goal is to get new students, primarily freshmen, plugged into the body of Christ before they get to school. We aim to unite believers, connect students to the local body, and equip them with the tools necessary to grow their faith and engage in biblical community throughout college.
summary: An organization whose main goal is to get new students plugged into the body of Christ before they get to school. This is a retreat in the summer to unite believers and connect them to the local churches and ministries.

name: The Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description:
The Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students (MAPS) is a nationally recognized organization dedicated to supporting underrepresented students pursuing careers in medicine and health care. As the undergraduate branch of the Student National Medical Association (SNMA), MAPS provides mentorship, resources, and opportunities that help prepare students for the medical school application process and beyond.

Through professional development workshops, community service initiatives, networking events, and collaborations with medical students and physicians, MAPS empowers members to excel academically, grow as leaders, and advocate for diversity in health care. The organization fosters a supportive community where students can connect with peers who share their goals and challenges, while also gaining exposure to the realities of medicine through shadowing, volunteering, and outreach.

At its core, MAPS is committed to addressing health disparities, increasing representation in medicine, and equipping future physicians with the tools to serve diverse communities with compassion and excellence.

We would love to have you as a MAPS member and if you're interested, please contact us and we will try to respond as soon as possible! 

Go Tigers!
summary: The MAPS initiative aims to represent underrepresented health profession students. Through collaborations with the community and opportunities for networking, we are trying to increase the numbers of health professionals of color entering the workforce.

name: MedEx Academy Club
description: MedEx is an undergraduate pre-professional health club directly associated with the MedEx Academy in Greenville, SC. MedEx Clemson seeks to provide students with professional networking and development opportunities, as well as to promote mental health advocacy for students on campus.
summary: Healthcare networking club associated with the Medical Experience Academy in Greenville, SC

name: Center for Student Leadership and Engagement
description:
Through our three focus areas, the Center for Student Leadership and Engagement helps students facilitate connections to leadership and engagement experiences on campus and within the local community. We support student transitions to campus via orientation, offer opportunities for students to participate in leadership development, 500+ student organizations, social and entertainment programs, and community service. We help students Begin, Belong, and Become. 

 

Student Transitions

Create a welcoming and seamless transition into and through the University by providing connections, resources, and experiences that empower students to be involved and grow as Clemson leaders. 

 

Student Involvement

Facilitate a connective and relational experience that builds a foundation for leadership and community.

 

Leadership Education

Empower and enable students to be agents of change by developing foundational leadership capacities, fostering social responsibility, and providing opportunities for meaningful application and practice of leadership now and in the future.
summary: We seek to create a uniquely Clemson experience that prepares every Clemson student to be a leader in their profession and community. We strive to prepare students to lead with honesty, integrity, and respect.

name: Housing Operations
description: Housing Operations assists students throughout their Clemson experience from providing assistance when they are locked out of their room, to greeting them when they arrive at move in, wishing them well when they move out for the last time, and everything in between.
summary: Housing Operations is a part of Clemson University Housing & Dining. This department provides students and parents with details regarding Housing Safety and Security, while also serving as a resource hub for navigating the residential experience.

name: Marketing & Communications
description:
The Division of Marketing and Communications is comprised of team members across the university working collaboratively to improve the visibility and protect the reputation of the Clemson brand. Our centralized team offers core marketing and communications products and services across brand experiences, strategic communications, presidential communications, marketing operations, social media, marketing strategy and analytics, and advancement marketing.

We help tell the Clemson story through strategic planning and creative thinking by providing innovative and well-researched approaches to marketing and communications across the print, digital and social platforms our audiences use.
summary: University Relations exists to protect and enhance the Clemson University brand by effectively and thoughtfully sharing what we know and love about Clemson University with the world.

name: Parking and Transportation Services
summary: Parking and Transportation Services is responsible for the management of Clemson University's parking and transit systems including the administration of parking permits, coordination of bus services, and education on alternative transportation modes.

name: Clemson Outdoor Recreation and Education
description:
The Clemson University Outdoor Recreation and Education Program provides the Clemson University community with opportunities to participate in a variety of outdoor adventure activities. Trips and instructional programs are scheduled throughout the year and include adventure activities such as: backpacking, whitewater rafting, paddleboarding, sea kayaking, skiing, and rock climbing. Trips are designed for every skill level, from the beginner to the advanced adventurer. As part of CORE's commitment to education, skills workshops are also offered in areas such as Leave No Trace, rock climbing, whitewater kayaking, and bicycle maintenance. In addition to these programs, CORE also provides an outdoor equipment rental service, a commuter bike program, and staff who can serve as knowledgeable resources to help people plan their own adventure trips. 

 

ALL REGISTRATION FOR CORE PROGRAMMING CAN BE FOUND HERE: www.clemson.edu/core.
summary: The Clemson Outdoor Recreation and Education Program provides the Clemson University community with opportunities to participate in a variety of outdoor adventure activities through both an outdoor trip programs and an outdoor equipment rental program.

name: Housing Summer Programs
description: University Housing has the opportunity to host guests every summer, from adults to youth participants in summer academic programs and sports camps.
summary: University Housing and Dining - University Housing has the opportunity to host guests every summer, from families for Bring Your Daughter to Clemson to participants in summer academic programs and sports camps.

name: Residential Living
description: Our goal as Residential Living is to be a resource for all of our residents, from the programs run by RA’s to the support of the professional staff. Through the efforts of our staff, living on campus is an inclusive, safe, fun and diverse learning and living environment for all of our residents.
summary: We shape residential environments to enhance learning and growth.

name: Staff Senate
description: The Senate is charged with communicating ideas and concerns which specifically relate to or affect staff employees and the University mission. The Senate is composed of elected members representing 3200 staff employees of Clemson University. The Senate membership is 1 staff senator to every 75 full-time staff employees among Budget Centers.
summary: Staff Senate acts as a conduit between the University community and staff. The Senate is the recognized voice for staff. The Senate currently represents classified and unclassified full-time permanent, time limited, and temporary grant employees

name: Office of Community and Ethical Standards
summary: The OCES provides students with an environment that is conducive to learning with behavioral expectations and encourages free inquiry and expression that is essential to the development of responsible citizens.

name: Glenn Department of Civil Engineering
description: Accredited Civil Engineering Department offering B.S., M.S., and PhD degrees in Civil Engineering.
summary: Academic Department

name: Student Health Services
summary: Student Health Services is an integrated, outpatient organization comprised of three divisions: Medical Services, Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS), and Healthy Campus.

name: Brooks Center
description:
In addition to professional productions, the Brooks Center is also home to the Department of Performing Arts, offering a dynamic, hands-on undergraduate experience. Through conferences and professional engagements made possible by donors, our students add to their resumes and build connections that will jumpstart their careers when they graduate with a Bachelor of Arts in productions studies in performing arts.

Established in 1994, the Brooks Center has been the hub for the performing arts in Clemson for more than two decades. This 87,000-square-foot facility serves as a professional roadhouse, hosting world-class professional dance, theatre, and musical performances throughout the academic year in its spacious, yet intimate, 969-seat proscenium auditorium. Its 100-seat blackbox theatre is home to exciting student theatre performances, and a small recital hall serves as a multi-purpose space for classes, gatherings, and student performances.

In addition to professional productions, the Brooks Center is also home to the Department of Performing Arts, offering a dynamic, hands-on undergraduate experience. Through conferences and professional engagements made possible by donors, our students add to their resumes and build connections that will jumpstart their careers when they graduate with a Bachelor of Arts in productions studies in performing arts.

The department is a vibrant part of the College of Architecture, Arts and Humanities with more than 50 faculty and staff, more than 100 performing arts majors, and more than 5,000 students participating in ensembles and taking classes. With courses ranging from music theory and theatre history to scenic design and stage management, students of all majors are welcome to take classes and perform in ensembles.
summary: The department is a vibrant part of the College of Architecture, Arts and Humanities with more than 50 faculty and staff, more than 100 performing arts majors, and more than 5,000 students participating in ensembles and taking classes.

name: Automotive Engineering
description: • – The Department of Automotive Engineering is part of Clemson University’s College of Engineering, Computing, and Applied Sciences. Currently, more than 200 students are pursuing graduate degrees in automotive engineering. Our diverse student body shares two things – a hunger for knowledge, and a passion for the car. We are housed in the Carroll A. Campbell Jr. Graduate Engineering Center (CGEC) at the Clemson University’s International Center for Automotive Research (CU-ICAR) campus located in Greenville, South Carolina. CGEC is a state-of-the-art building equipped with full-scale vehicle testing equipment. Coupled with the services of our highly-skilled technical staff, the facilities are a wonderland for bright, inquisitive students.
summary: The Department of Automotive Engineering is part of Clemson University’s College of Engineering, Computing, and Applied Sciences. We are located at the Clemson University International Center for Automotive Research in Greenville, SC.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description:
Greetings from the Legendary Lambda Theta Chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated!

 

Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated was founded on January 15, 1908 on the campus of Howard University. Since then, the sorority has been on a mission comprised of five basic tenets that have remained unchanged since the sorority’s inception more than a century ago. Alpha Kappa Alpha’s mission is to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improve their social stature, to maintain a progressive interest in college life, and to be of “Service to All Mankind."

 

The Lambda Theta Chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. was chartered on May 2nd, 1977 on the campus of Clemson University. The Lambda Theta Chapter was the first black sorority on Clemson's campus and the second NPHC organization chartered on campus.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. is the first intercollegiate historically African American sorority. The sorority is one of the nation's largest Greek-letter organizations consisting of college-educated women of many diverse backgrounds.

name: Residential Learning
description: We create and enhance collaborative, outcome-based, and innovative learning experiences to foster student success.
summary: Supporting student learning in residential communities.

name: Student Funding Board
description:
The Student Funding Board (SFB) of Clemson University is a Delegated Student Organization with authority granted from the Vice President of Student Affairs to distribute the Student Activity Fee to registered student organizations. The SFB is a policy group that funds organizations in a fair and equitable manner. Furthermore, the views and opinions of organizations funded through SFB do not reflect the official position of SFB or the university. 

Procedures for distribution of funds is the responsibility of Student Affairs Business Operations. 

For more information about FY26 Annual Funding or FY25 Contingency Funding please see the Resource Library and email orgfund@clemson.edu with any further questions.
summary: Student Funding Board is made up of students across Clemson University who are charged with the responsibility to allocate Student Activity Fee dollars by the Vice President of Student Affairs.

name: Biosystems Engineering Student Club
description: The mission of BE Club is to provide, professional development/networking opportunities,  community service projects and social events to foster community and success within the biosystems engineering major and support sustainability efforts with other sustainability-focused student organizations. We have been creating a foundation for our members to make a more sustainable world by fostering a strong and supportive community for 15+ years. Every year a significant number of its students participate in the Southeastern Student Rally, hosted by its professional organization, The American Society of Biological and Agricultural Engineers (ASABE). "Go BE!"
summary: The mission of BE Club is to provide social interaction for BE students between classes, professional networking opportunities, and community service opportunities in order to create a foundation for our members to make a more sustainable world.

name: CHANGE (Creating Habits and Norms Guiding Ethical Decisions)
description: CHANGE (Creating Habits and Norms Guiding Ethical decisions) is an academic student organization composed of both undergraduate and graduate students that serves to promote ethics and ethical-decision making at Clemson University. We consist of at least one student from each of the seven colleges at Clemson University in order to promote ethics in every college. We host programs every semester, plan a High School Ethics Case Competition each spring, and publish the bi-annual Ethics Editorial newsletter.
summary: The mission of CHANGE is to inform and educate students of Clemson University about the importance of ethical decision-making and to encourage them to act as ethical leaders in their respective fields.

name: Delta Chi
description: Delta Chi, originally a law fraternity founded at Cornell in 1890, began as a small brotherhood of 11 men who believed that great advantages were to be derived from a brotherhood of college and university men, appreciating that close association may promote friendship, develop character, advance justice, and assist in the acquisition of a sound education. The success of those men and their ideals have built the Delta Chi organization into what it is today, with over a 100 chapters nationwide, and over 100,000 members between those chapters. We cultivate inclusive environments for any young man who reflects and lives our values of promoting friendship, developing character, advancing justice, and assisting in the acquisition of a sound education. It’s through these values that our members become men of action and grow to become the best version of themselves while living, leading, and growing each day with integrity. ITB!
summary: Clemson IFC Fraternity

name: Women in Business
description: The purpose of Women in Business is to facilitate events and programming focused on furthering women’s knowledge on how to succeed in the professional business world. The club’s mission is to provide undergraduate students within the College of Business at Clemson University with access to female business leaders and professionals in order to expose students to various business-oriented learning and networking opportunities. Women in Business will work to accomplish this mission by introducing speaker series, workshops, networking events, and other targeted activities to prepare students to be young, female professionals.
summary: The purpose of Women in Business is to facilitate events and programming focused on furthering women’s knowledge on how to succeed in the professional business world through speakers, workshops, panels, and more.

name: Association for Women in Sports Media
description: AWSM (Association for Women in Sports Media) is dedicated to empowering and supporting women who are interested in sports media, including, but not limited to, reporting, editing, broadcasting, social media, and media relations. The Clemson chapter is part of the nationally recognized AWSM network of schools across the country, working together to support and advance women in sports media. We are committed to fostering a supportive campus community that provides mentorship, professional development, and networking opportunities to hear from women in various sports-related roles. We aim to prepare Clemson students for success in future roles in sports media, equipping them with the skills, connections, and confidence to thrive in the industry. All students are welcome - please reach out if you have any questions!
summary: AWSM (Association for Women in Sports Media) is a group of women who are interested in working in sports media. We have a passion for sports & are involved in the Clemson Athletics community, as well as with current sports professionals.

name: Pre-Occupational and Speech Therapy Club
description: This club was founded to create a community of undergraduate scholars interested in pursuing the disciplines of Occupational Therapy and Speech-language Pathology. Since Clemson does not offer degrees in these fields to undergraduate students, we took it upon ourselves to compile resources and offer opportunities. Club members love to better our community by volunteering and fundraising at local clinics and facilities. We seek to educate, prepare, serve, and inspire undergraduate Clemson students through guest speakers, workshops, volunteer opportunities, a resource database, and a strong network of hardworking individuals.
summary: Pre-OST Club supports and challenges members interested in pursuing graduate education in the fields of  Occupational Therapy and Speech Language Pathology.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers Geo-Institute Graduate Student Organization
description:
The mission statements towards achieving the goal are provided below:

  1. Increase the interaction between students and faculties.
  2. Develop a platform to discuss and share the research interests among other fellow students.
  3. Introduce the students about Geo-Institute (G-I) and encourage them to become the G-I student members.
  4. Encourage students to participate in different G-I activities.
  5. Increase the awareness about geotechnical engineering among the undergraduate students and explain them about graduate school opportunities in the field of geotechnical engineering.
  6. Provide leadership opportunities.
  7. Establish network with the industries to explore career opportunities.
summary: The ASCE Geo-Institute Graduate Student Organization (GI-GSO) at Clemson University is established with the goal of enriching the geotechnical engineering study experience of the students.

name: Late-Night Board Games Club
description:
We meet from 9:00 pm-12:00 am every Tuesday and Thursday during the school year. We usually go for a couple of hours, so feel free to pop in for as long as you want. Whether you are a board game veteran or a novice, you are welcome to come!

Join our Discord server to have a chat and stay in the loop: https://discord.gg/gDMfG4WYBV

For questions, email clemsonboardgames@gmail.com. Email responses may be slow in the summer, so feel free to ask in the club discord.
summary: We play board games (note: not RPGs) late at night during the week to give students a chance to socialize and take a break from their studies.

name: Health Occupations Students of America
description: The division of HOSA-Future Health Professionals specifically for students pursuing a baccalaureate degree and looking to pursue a career in a health-related field. With competitions, scholarships, and volunteer opportunities for university students, HOSA has it all! HOSA is an international student organization recognized by the U.S. Department of Education and the Health Science Education (HSE) Division of ACTE. HOSA's two-fold mission is to promote career opportunities in the health care industry and to enhance the delivery of quality health care to all people. HOSA provides a unique program of leadership development, motivation, and recognition exclusively for secondary, postsecondary, adult, and collegiate students enrolled in health science education and biomedical science programs or have interests in pursuing careers in health professions!
summary: The division of HOSA-Future Health Professionals specifically for students pursuing a baccalaureate degree and looking to pursue a career in a health-related field.

name: Clemson Red Cross Club
description: The Clemson Red Cross is affiliated with the Upstate SC Chapter of the American Red Cross. The American Red Cross prevents and alleviates human suffering in the face of emergencies by mobilizing volunteers' power and donors' generosity. Red Cross volunteers turn compassion into action under our five lines of service: Disaster Relief, Blood Services, Training & Certification, Service to the Armed Forces and International Services. As a service and advocacy organization, we aim to serve the better health of the community.
summary: The Clemson Red Cross is affiliated with the Upstate SC Chapter of the American Red Cross. As a service and advocacy organization, we aim to fundraise, host safety events, and serve the better health of the community.

name: Nurses' Christian Fellowship
description:
The Purpose of Nurses' Christian Fellowship is to establish and advance in nursing, within education and practice, witnessing communities of nursing students and nurses who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord: growing in love for God, God's Word, God's people of every ethnicity and culture, and God's purposes in the world.

Nurses' Christian Fellowship (NCF) at Clemson University builds community among nursing majors and nursing professors through weekly devotionals on Mondays at 5 PM.
summary: A community of Christian nursing students and professors who desire to glorify God through diligence, care, mentorship, devotion, and fellowship.

name: Alpha Tau Omega Fraternity
description: The Eta Pi chapter of the Alpha Tau Omega fraternity is a fraternity based on christian principles. We have about 100 active members from all over the country actively pursuing college degrees. Our main focus is to maintain and build better men, ones of which are larger then themselves. We hold a plethora of awards including multiple chapter of the year awards as well as the True Merit award which is the highest honor we can receive. We also hold numerous philanthropy events each year, our main one being Viking week which raises money for Upstate Warrior solution who benefits veterans and their families in the update of South Carolina. We currently hold and have broken the IFC record the past two years for most money raised by a fraternity at Clemson and are hopeful in making it a third consecutive year this year.
summary: Fraternity

name: Rho Zeta Chapter of Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority, Inc.
description:
The Rho Zeta Chapter of Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority Inc. is a multicultural Greek organization that encourages its members to celebrate all cultures and take pride in their own. We are empowered women leading the world, and are open to all shapes, colors, sizes, sexualities, and ethnicities!

Check out out Linktree!
summary: The Rho Zeta Chapter of Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority Inc. is a multicultural Greek organization that encourages women to celebrate all cultures and take pride in their own.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description:
Pi Kappa Alpha was founded in Room 47 West Range at the University of Virginia in 1868. Since then, it's developed into an international brotherhood and become one of the largest fraternal organizations in the world.

How? It's simple. Pi Kappa Alpha has remained dedicated to developing men of integrity, intellect, success and high moral character, and to fostering a truly lifelong fraternal experience.

Pi Kappa Alpha is composed of more than 275,000 lifetime members, 220 chapters and 150 alumni associations in North America. In the last 10 years, PIKE membership grew by 51% and maintains the largest average chapter size of North American fraternities. Between 2014 and 2015, Pikes raised over $2.125 million and contributed 300,000+ hours in community service
summary: Pi Kappa Alpha is an IFC chapter since 2019. Interested in learning more, reach out and let us know!

name: Military & Veteran Engagement
description: Military and Veteran Engagement engages with student veterans and military-connected students through intentional programming and support services, equips them with relevant skill-building tools and resources, and empowers them to be leaders on campus and in their communities. Key functions of our department are military onboarding orientation and veterans graduation programming, managing the M&VE resource center, collaborating with the Clemson University Veterans Commission, facilitating Green Zone Training, advising the Student Veteran Association and SALUTE National Honor Society, advocating for military-connected student needs, and honoring veterans through designated military appreciation events.

name: Young Women for America at Clemson
description:
YOUNG WOMEN FOR AMERICA

 

Young Women for America (YWA) is a project of Concerned Women for America (CWA), the nation's largest public policy organization for women. At Young Women for America, we believe God has ordained each one of your days, placing you in our nation, for such a time as this. Young Women for America trains the next generation to protect and promote Biblical values and Constitutional principles through prayer, education, and advocacy.
summary: YWA is a student-led initiative for girls at Clemson University. The mission of YWA is to train the next generation to protect and promote Biblical values and Constitutional principles through prayer, education, and advocacy

name: Tigers for Green Innovation
description:
Tigers for Green Innovation (TGI) strives to push sustainability to the forefront of Clemson University through education and innovation. Utilizing on-campus lectures, community outreach, and engaging workshops, TGI aims to educate Clemson University students, faculty, staff, and the broader Clemson community on sustainability and environmental issues facing today’s world. TGI takes this education a step further by introducing a dynamic environment for students to discuss sustainable ideas with others who have a background in environmental work in order to help students from diverse educational backgrounds develop project ideas to submit to the Sustainability Fund, thus helping the University reach its established sustainability goals. The education and innovation streams of TGI will culminate at an annual day-long conference devoted to sustainability and other environmental issues, where students will hear talks from Clemson’s top faculty, industry, and government leaders who have experience in these fields. Furthermore, students have the opportunity to learn and grow by developing sustainable projects to be presented at the end of the day related to a problem we pose to them. On the whole, Tigers for Green Innovation looks to ignite a passion for the environment and solving today’s biggest sustainability issues in the Clemson community.

Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/71816358/6aXQteoK
summary: The Mission of Tigers for Green Innovation will be to create a greener, more sustainable Clemson University and Clemson community, through work in education and projects promoting on-campus innovation, including the Annual Sustainability Summit.

name: Clemson Shag Club
description:
The Clemson Shag Club is a Student Organization recognized by Clemson University Student Government. Our goal is to expand the knowledge and love of South Carolina's State Dance, the Shag, among Clemson's students, community, and beyond! If you like dancing, beach music, and having a good time with your friends and want to learn more about Shag, we'd love to have you in our club! Follow our Instagram @ClemsonShagClub for the most up-to-date information.

Members will have the option to purchase our annual club-t-shirt, will have access to regularly scheduled Shag lessons, and have the opportunity to participate in out of town social dances with other Shag groups in the upstate area.

You can also find us on Instagram and Facebook. If you have any questions regarding the club, you can always email us at clemsonshagclub@gmail.com. We hope to see you soon!
summary: Enjoy Carolina Shag social dance events, beach music, and Shag lessons with the Clemson Shag Club.

name: Chi Epsilon
description:
Chi Epsilon’s Purpose:

Dedicated to the purpose of maintaining and promoting the status of civil engineering as an ideal profession, Chi-Epsilon was organized to recognize the characteristics of the individual civil engineer deemed to be fundamental to the successful pursuit of an engineering career, and to aid in the development of those characteristics in the civil engineering student.

Engineering, the application of scientific principles to the practical needs of society, is assuming a constantly increasing responsibility for the well-being of all people, and thus calling for competence of the highest order. This responsibility can be discharged only by a professional group whose members are possessed of a good basic technical ability, intelligence, moral integrity, and effective social poise in their relationship with the larger community of which they are part.

To contribute to the improvement of the profession, Chi Epsilon fosters the development and exercise of sound traits of character and technical ability among civil engineers, and its members, by precept and example, toward an ever higher standard of professional service.

Vision:

By recognizing outstanding students and graduates for their scholarship, character, practicality and sociability, Chi Epsilon seeks to foster excellence, connectivity and engagement among those in the civil engineering community to improve our world.

Mission:

The mission of Chi Epsilon is to achieve its vision through its four pillars:

Scholarship - recognizing excellence and achievement in academic and professional endeavors

Character - upholding the integrity and responsibility of the civil engineering profession through our service

Practicality - educating and encouraging our members to improve our world through innovative and impactful solutions

Sociability - connecting our members while engaging the broader [engineering] community
summary: Chi Epsilon is the National Civil Engineering Honor Society in the United States, which honors students who exemplify the principles of “Scholarship, Character, Practicality, and Sociability” within the civil engineering profession.

name: Clemson Diplomacy Club
description: CDC works to create a network for Clemson students with Clemson alumni who work in diplomatic or nongovernmental organization careers. While being Clemson students, members of CDC will have the opportunity to attend guest lectures, find assistance in finding and applying to internships, travel to related conferences, and be introduced to potential jobs. Additionally, CDC will build a community on campus of students with similar interests while growing Clemson's credibility as an institution that produces future global leaders.
summary: Clemson Diplomacy Club (CDC) is an organization that unites undergraduate students interested in foreign affairs and international relations, specifically those who strive to hold occupations in diplomatic or non-governmental organization careers.

name: Environmental Engineering Club at Clemson University
description: The environmental engineering club is a club for undergraduates in the BS environmental engineering program and similar majors. The club provides a link between students, faculty, administration, and alumni in order to promote a network in department and field. The student chapter holds meetings, on-campus activities, and field trips to stay engaged with one another and the community. Overall, the club is a place for those with common interests in environmental engineering to connect.
summary: The EES Club is open to all undergraduate students who are environmental engineering majors or interested in similar topics. It is a place to connect with professionals and other environmental engineering students!

name: MEDLIFE
description: MEDLIFE is an organization that partners with low-income communities in Latin America and Africa to improve their access to medicine, education, and community development initiatives. We achieve our mission through Service Learning Trips, the MEDLIFE Project Fund, and student empowerment. This chapter of MEDLIFE would support the national mission of MEDLIFE while also fostering a local partnership with Clemson through community service and member events.
summary: MEDLIFE is an organization that partners with low-income communities in Latin America and Africa to improve their access to medicine, education, and community development initiatives.

name: Office of College Preparation and Outreach
summary: place holder

name: Clemson Residents in Science and Engineering
description: Residents in Science and Engineering (RISE) is a unique living-learning environment created for first-year students to excel in their chosen STEM discipline at Clemson University.
summary: Residents in Science and Engineering (RISE) is a unique living-learning environment created for first-year students to excel in their chosen STEM discipline at Clemson University.

name: Bridge to Clemson
description: The Bridge to Clemson program is a dedicated transfer pathway from Tri-County Technical College to Clemson University. Beginning with a strong first-year academic experience, the Bridge program includes targeted academic advising, student support services, and a student life component - all of which are designed to help students succeed, meet Bridge academic requirements (30 credits and a 2.5GPA) and transferring to Clemson. Advisors from both institutions assist students with selecting courses that transfer to Clemson. The successful collaboration between Tri-County and Clemson allows students to make progress in their intended Clemson major during their first year at Tri-County while preparing to enroll at Clemson beginning their second year.
summary: The Bridge to Clemson program is a dedicated transfer pathway from Tri-County Technical College to Clemson University.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Organization
description: The Chemistry Graduate Student Organization (CGSO) serves as the representative body for graduate students in the Department of Chemistry at Clemson University. Our mission is to advocate for student interests, foster a strong sense of community, and enhance the academic, professional and personal development of our members. CGSO supports students through: Departmental representation and advocacy Professional development events and career workshops PEG (Professional Enrichment Grant) travel funding for conferences STEM outreach and community events Social events to build connection and well-being.
summary: The Chemistry Graduate Student Organization (CGSO) serves as the representative body for graduate students in the Department of Chemistry at Clemson University.

name: Clemson YDSA
description:
Clemson YDSA is a political activism club founded in 2019. Our active members and alumni hold a diverse range of views under the socialist umbrella. Our club focuses on participation in mutual aid, direct action, harm reduction, and building class consciousness on campus by highlighting the social, economic, and environmental harm imposed by the capitalist class. We believe in awareness and taking action. 

The club began its political involvement by canvassing for Bernie Sanders during the 2020 election. We were lead organizers for the Take Back Pride marches, and Tuition Freeze 1 and 2 in a coalition with other student organizations. In efforts to reduce drug overdoses, we passed out free Narcan and educated students on when and how to use it. Currently we remain the most active group of volunteers at The Cleo Bailey Experiment, a community garden and future community center in Anderson, SC. Our members have aided in the cultivation, harvest, and local distribution of over 2,000lbs of food in the 2023 summer growing season alone. 

In the Fall of 2025, we began our Free Plan B and Contraceptives campaign to advocate for Redfern Pharmacy to make contraceptives free for all students, in order to keep up to pace with other colleges that make those services free and accessible. We combated racism and bigotry from conservative groups on campus that made attacks on minority communities at Clemson University by exposing them as a community safety risk on campus.

Join us at our next meeting to learn about our latest plans and activism.

For a better world.
summary: Clemson YDSA is a political activism club founded in 2019. Our club focuses on participation in mutual aid, direct action, harm reduction, and building class consciousness on campus.

name: American Mathematical Society
description: AMS is a graduate student organization in the School of Mathematical and Statistical Sciences.   We seek to serve graduate students by organizing professional development events as well as some social events.
summary: AMS is a graduate student organization in the School of Mathematical and Statistical Sciences.   We seek to serve graduate students by organizing professional development events as well as some social events.

name: Clemson Votes
description:
We are a group of faculty, staff and students advocating and raising awareness for voter registration and civic engagement at Clemson University.

Want more information on our goals and strategies? Want to know Clemson student voting rates? Want to add an event or program to our list? Want to join our coalition meetings? Contact Kate Radford (radford@clemson.edu)
summary: Focusing on broad-based, non-partisan efforts aimed at improving the voter engagement of our student population - graduate and undergraduate.

name: Wellness in Graduate School Coalition
description: The objective of WinGS is to improve graduate student mental health and well-being. WinGS seeks to assist graduate students in navigating the milestones of their academic year while emphasizing the importance of mental health stewardship and healthy habits at every stage. WinGS also seeks to assist first-year graduate students with their transition into graduate education and help them form healthy habits for continued success.
summary: WinGS seeks to improve graduate student mental health and well-being by assisting graduate students in navigating the milestones of their academic year while emphasizing the importance of mental health stewardship and healthy habits.

name: Pre-Physical Therapy Club
description: The Clemson Pre-PT Club meets at least twice a month during which we will have guest speakers, such as licensed physical therapists and athletic trainers. We also have PT and AT graduate school admissions representatives to help guide you to the right school! We host events such fundraisers, in-services, campus tours, resume workshops, community service opportunities, and more! Our club collaborates with other Pre-Health Clubs, such as the Pre-Dental, Pre-Vet, Pre-PA, and more! This club will show you the field of Physical Therapy and help prepare you for applying, choosing the right school, and to connect you with other future PTs.
summary: The Clemson Pre-PT Club is an organization for any undergraduate students interested in pursuing a career in physical therapy.

name: Trew Friends
description: Trew Friends is a part of the Heather Trew Foundation which aims to spread awareness of the significance of organ, eye, and tissue donation. The organization works to educate and encourage college students to register for the National Donate Life Organ Donation Registry. Trew Friends hosts events which spread awareness of the importance of organ donation including an annual Donor Duel alongside the annual Blood Battle competition between Clemson and USC. Do you desire to help others? Do you know anyone who has given, received, or is waiting for an organ? Do you like beating USC? If you got the chance to save a life, would you take it? If so, Trew Friends is the place for you.
summary: We, the members of Trew Friends, being aware of the increasing need for organ donors, realize the need to form an organization for the purpose of increasing the number of registered organ donors and raising awareness of the importance of organ donations.

name: The Clemson Period Project
description:
The Clemson Period Project is a nonprofit organization that serves to provide menstrual products to people in need. This is done through collection and donation events as well as delivery drop-offs to various locations. The Clemson Period Project is the first student chapter of this organization. At Clemson, we aim to not only collect and deliver to various campus locations but also educate students on women and gender minorities' health. The Clemson Period Project works to serve those on campus that are needing resources, as well as reduce the stigma around menstruation.

To learn more about our National Organization: The Period Project Nationals

To join our chapter email list: 

https://forms.gle/nHueCacihJxcwzFi6

To join our chapter GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/89080983/lGL7ScDh
summary: The Clemson Period Project is a branch of The Period Project national nonprofit organization that serves to alleviate menstrual equity issues within the Clemson community while also raising awareness for women's health issues.

name: Eta Sigma Gamma
description:
National Health Education Honorary that strives to:

  • Supporting the planning, implementation and evaluation of health education programs and resources
  • Stimulating and disseminating scientific research;
  • Motivating and providing health education services
  • Recognizing academic achievement
  • Supporting health education advocacy initiatives
  • Promoting professional standards and ethics
  • Promoting networking activities among health educators and related professionals
summary: The mission of Eta Sigma Gamma is promotion of the discipline by elevating the standards, ideals, competence and ethics of professionally prepared individuals in Health Education.

name: Clemson Association for Women in Mathematics
description: The Clemson Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) was created in 2013 to encourage those, particularly women, pursuing education and careers in fields related to the mathematical sciences by mentoring those at all stages of their mathematical career, and by promoting increased awareness of and interest in the mathematical sciences, and the role of women in the mathematical sciences, through acts of service.
summary: The Clemson Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) encourages those, particularly women, to pursue education and careers in fields related to the mathematical sciences by mentoring those at all stages of their mathematical career.

name: Clemson Boards
description:
Clemson Boards is more than just a group—it's a community where students come together to share a passion for skating. Whether you're into traditional skateboards, longboards, or the cutting-edge world of E-Skate, there's a place for you here. From the rolling hills of Death Valley to the High Rises, we're united by our love for the ride.
What We’re All About: Our mission is simple: to support, grow, and connect the vibrant community of boarders at Clemson University. Whether through organized rides, mechanical support, or peer-to-peer advice on our Discord, we're here to help you make the most of your boarding experience.
Beginner? No Problem! We offer lessons to help newcomers learn how to ride safely and confidently. Whether you’re just curious or already hooked, we welcome anyone with an interest in the sport—board or no board.
Why We Ride: Beyond the thrill, boarding promotes both fitness and eco-friendly transportation on campus. Plus, it's just plain fun!
Stay Connected: Our main hub for communication is Discord, so be sure to join us there! Follow our Instagram for updates, and check out our Linktree for all the ways to stay connected.
👉 Join Us Here
Looking forward to seeing you on a board!
summary: Clemson Boards is a longboarding, skateboarding, and e-boarding dedicated to bringing together students who love the hobby or are just getting into it.

name: Poultry Science Club at Clemson
description: The Poultry Science Club at Clemson is a group dedicated to exploring the poultry industry. We do not require club fees and we are open to all majors! PSC provides a unique view into a undervalued aspect of agriculture. We strive to provide our members with animal experience, poultry knowledge, and a friendly environment explore new avenues!
summary: PSC meets twice a month to discuss the poultry industry, practices, listen to guest speakers, play poultry-themed games and enjoy poultry products. Food & Fun are always provided!

name: It's On Us
description: Our goal is to bring awareness, validation, and support to survivors of interpersonal violence (relationship abuse, sexual assault, sexual harassment, interpersonal violence, stalking, etc.) at Clemson University. We want ALL survivors to feel seen, heard, and supported by the Clemson family. We aim to achieve this through building community and spreading awareness on campus. Our mission is to support survivors of interpersonal violence on Clemson's Campus as well as support all underrepresented and unsupported students.
summary:
​
Our goal is to bring awareness, validation, and support to survivors of interpersonal violence at Clemson University.

name: Experimental Forest Trail Runners
description:
The Experimental Forest Trail Runners exists to introduce students to trail running by organizing free races and weekly trail runs in Clemson’s Experimental Forest. As a club, we host weekly runs in the forest to give students a chance to experience trail running in a safe environment. Any student can join the Experimental Forest Trail Runners regardless of running ability or experience! Additionally, each spring we organize the Clemson Experimental Marathon which has grown into the largest free trail race in the South. This race serves to promote our local trail system and raise funds for trail maintenance.

 

If you would like to join, please join here on TigerQuest and join our GroupMe which is our primary form of communication.

Link to GroupMe:

https://groupme.com/join_group/87491799/a2bkMfBm
summary: The Experimental Forest Trail Runners exists to introduce students to trail running through free races and weekly trail runs in Clemson’s Experimental Forest. We accept all levels of runners regardless of ability!

name: CUSG Outreach and Engagement Committee
description:
SPRING 2026 TIGER PROWL REGISTRATION: https://bit.ly/3LIXsXE

We want to CU Involved!  The mission of the CUSG Outreach & Engagement Committee is to support & celebrate the work of the Clemson student organization community along with the entire study body.  We reach these goals by by providing programs and initiatives to connect, educate, and develop groups, leaders, and individuals.  

 

Calendar of Events:

Fall:

Tiger Prowl

Lighting of the Gardens

 

Spring:

Tiger Prowl

President's Banquet & the State of the Union Speech

 

Reach out to us to get involved, learn more about our events, and get connected at Clemson!
summary: The CUSG Outreach & Engagement Committee supports & celebrates the work of the Clemson student organization community. We serve as a liaison between CUSG and the student body at Clemson University.

name: College of Engineering, Computing, and Applied Sciences
description:
Our Mission

Our mission in the College of Engineering, Computing and Applied Sciences is to create future graduates who can be productive in a global economy. Because today’s global citizen is expected to have experience beyond his or her primary discipline, our academic structure encourages cross-disciplinary collaboration. Engineering, computing and applied sciences represent independent degree programs that stand united in contributing to the overall success of the college. In addition, we provide a number of experiential learning opportunities that require students to transfer academic knowledge and experience from the classroom to real-world work environments.

Our Vision

Our vision is to create engineers and scientists who are critical thinkers entrepreneurs and innovators who understand the social and business implications of their work. When attracting new and retaining existing talent, we should seek out individuals who are able to communicate their ideas coherently and work effectively in teams. Above all, the college provides leadership that works toward solving society’s big problems

The Journey of a Lifetime

Clemson University knows that preparing students for a career in the 21st century means providing much more than specialized technical knowledge. Engineers and scientists must also understand the social and business implications of their work, and they must be able to communicate their ideas to a wide range of people, work effectively in teams and, above all, be willing and able to provide leadership in solving society’s problems.
summary: Our mission in the College of Engineering, Computing and Applied Sciences is to create future graduates who can be productive in a global economy.

name: Health Professions Advising Ambassadors
description: The Health Professions Advising Ambassadors serve as a university-wide representation for all pre-health opportunities. The HPA Ambassadors provide information to prospective and current students, and families of students interested in pursuing professional school on the advising opportunities and resources that Clemson University offers. The HPA Ambassadors gain extensive experiential learning through events such as panel discussions and round table events, and speaking and presenting at open houses and table events. They also organize and participate in a wide variety of volunteer and service efforts and are exposed to numerous opportunities to gain valuable real-world experience in the health field of their interest. The HPA Ambassadors meet every other week in Long Hall with the Health Professions Advising Director, Harolynn Williams, to discuss upcoming pre-health events and various other topics. The HPA Ambassadors are committed to serving the Clemson University student body and providing the most up-to-date information for all pre-health students to ensure the best chances for students to be accepted to the program of their interest. The HPA Ambassadors are educated on, and able to discuss, all of the following professional health areas: Medical, Dental, Occupational Therapy, Physical Therapy, Pharmacy, Physician Assistant, Speech Language Pathology, and Veterinary Medicine.
summary: The HPA Ambassadors serve as a university-wide representation for all pre-health opportunities and provide information to prospective and current students on the advising opportunities that Clemson University offers.

name: Campus Reservations & Events
description: Campus Reservations and Events oversees the Hendrix Student Center, Memorial Auditorium (At Tillman Hall), Barnes Center, and Samuel J. Cadden Chapel. Additionally, we manage reservations for numerous beautiful outdoor, reservable spaces including the Carillon Gardens, South Green, Amphitheater, Military Heritage Plaza and the North Green. Each year, multiple major events are proudly presented in our venues as well as other campus locations. Our event planning team offers an array of event planning and coordination services.
summary: Campus Reservations and Events oversees a variety of indoor and outdoor campus life facilities including Hendrix Student Center, Barnes Center, Samuel J. Cadden Chapel, and Memorial Auditorium at Tillman Hall.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description: The main objective of the ASCE Student Chapter is to encourage its members to study civil engineering and to advance the civil engineering profession. The American Society of Civil Engineers, the oldest national professional engineering society, was founded in 1852 with an objective to enhance the welfare of humanity by advancing the science and profession of civil engineering. More than 125,000 individual ASCE members are engaged in engineering education, research, public agency, industrial, construction, and private engineering practice.
summary: The American Society of Civil Engineers represents  members of the civil engineering profession. ASCE is the nation’s oldest engineering society.

name: Black Improvement Association
description: We strive for equality and continually promote our culture in hopes of seeing other Black students living a happy and comfortable life on campus. We implement our goals by providing a safe space for all black students to be their authentic selves. We believe that our presence will be seen, and our voices will be heard, and we will continue to serve alongside students to advocate for our community.
summary: The Black Improvement Association is a student led organization that aims to promote unity and advocate for the Black community at Clemson University.

name: College of Education Undergraduate Student Advisory Board
description: The mission of the College of Education Undergraduate Student Advisory Board (USAB) is to advance the College of Education by promoting the welfare of the student body, to enhance relationships between students and faculty, and to serve as ambassadors.
summary: The mission of the College of Education Undergraduate Student Advisory Board (USAB) is to advance the College of Education by promoting the welfare of the student body, to enhance relationships between students and faculty, and to serve as ambassadors.

name: Clemson IEEE Power and Energy Society Student Branch
description: This organization is a group of undergraduate and graduate students enthusiastic about the domain of Power and Energy. As a student branch chapter under IEEE PES, the organization promotes academic and social activities for the development of power & energy
summary: This organization is a group of undergraduate and graduate students enthusiastic in the domain of Power and Energy. As a student branch chapter under IEEE PES, the organization promotes academic and social activities for the development of power & energy

name: Plant and Environmental Sciences Graduate Student Association
summary: The PES GSA provides social and professional development opportunities to foster meaningful relationships between PES graduate students, faculty, and staff on campus and the Research and Education Centers of Clemson University

name: Epsilon Tau Pi
description:
Email us at enmyers@g.clemson.edu if you would like to join!!

Epsilon Tau Pi is the premier Eagle Scout fraternity dedicated to brotherhood, service, and the principles of Scouting.

​

Brotherhood is very important within Epsilon Tau Pi.  The limited number of Eagle Scouts creates a tight knit organization of friends that share the same common experiences and many of the same goals for the future.  Epsilon Tau Pi builds brotherhood everyday through social activities such as camping, sporting events, barbecues and cookouts, canoeing, or just hanging out.

​

Service is also a large part of life in Epsilon Tau Pi.  Becoming an Eagle Scout is built upon service to others and it is such that Epsilon Tau Pi is built upon.  The brotherhood serves the community as well as the local Scouting communities as a way to give back to Scouting.
summary: Epsilon Tau Pi is the premier Eagle Scout fraternity dedicated to brotherhood, service, and the principles of Scouting.

name: Test Organization
description: This is a test student organization for the purpose of creating educational materials for the student organization community.  Want to learn more about using TigerQuest?  Click through our Student Organization Guides
summary: This is a test student organization for the purpose of creating educational materials for the student organization community.

name: College of Science Mentoring and Inclusion Collaborative
summary: COSMIC provides support to incoming first-year students from underrepresented backgrounds within the College of Science. First-year students receive guidance from upperclassmen from similar or shared backgrounds and interests.

name: Clemson Honors College Ambassadors
description: The Clemson University Honors College Student Ambassadors are Honors student volunteers who are official ambassadors for the Honors College and who are passionate about Honors at Clemson University. They meet with prospective students and their families to provide a student perspective on their various Honors experiences. They are a diverse group of students, representing each of Clemson's academic colleges listed below, and they are involved in all aspects of campus life. They are developing communication, teamwork, and leadership skills as they interact with prospective students and their families.
summary: The Clemson University Honors College Student Ambassadors are Honors student volunteers who are official ambassadors for the Honors College.

name: Best Buddies
description:
What to expect when you join Best Buddies

Join our New GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/60728718/vcoRxWvm

Follow us on our New Instagram @clemson_bb
summary: Best Buddies is dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, leadership development, and inclusive living for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: Clemson College Loops
description: College Loops is more than just programming. It is an on-campus organization that offers meetings and challenges focusing on honing computer science skills, engaging women to take action, and collaborating and creating with one another, all while building a community for the women in or interested in these fields.
