summary: College Loops is an international non-profit organization working to close the gender gap in technology and leading the movement to inspire, educate, and equip young women with the computing skills needed to pursue 21st-century opportunities.

name: Mindset
description: Mindset is a student-led discussion, education, and outreach group for anything mental health related. We focus on spreading knowledge of mental health issues to reduce stigma through monthly events and meetings. Our goal is to create a judgment-free zone where people can feel comfortable sharing experiences.
summary: Discussion, education, and outreach group for anything mental health related.

name: College of Business Recruiting Ambassadors
description: The Wilbur O. & Ann Powers College of Business Recruitment Ambassadors are a group of student volunteers who have been selected and trained to lead one-on-one information sessions with prospective families and represent the college across all university functions. These students invest their time in the College of Business because they want to give back to a program that has given so much to them.
summary: COBRA provides information sessions to prospective families and represents the college in many different ways.

name: Freshman Council
description: Freshman Council is a branch of the Clemson Undergraduate Student Government that gives 35 freshmen the opportunity to represent their class, develop leadership skills by meeting and listening to Clemson’s greatest leaders, and be paired with upperclass-men students that share similar interests. On top of this, it allows you to create a strong bond with 34 other freshmen that all have the same goal: making Clemson University a better place.
summary: Freshman Council gives 35 freshmen the opportunity to represent their class, develop leadership skills by meeting and listening to Clemson’s greatest leaders, and be paired with upper-classmen students that share similar interests.

name: LGBTQIA+ Engagement Committee
description:
The LGBTQIA+ Engagement Committee is an Executive Committee on Clemson Undergraduate Student Government. As a committee within the President's Cabinet, we serve as a liaison between students and administration. The main goals of this committee are to strengthen out LGBTQIA+ community, make Clemson more LGBTQ friendly, and to raise awareness about LGBTQ Issues through advocacy, community building, and resource sharing. 

The committee meets once a week.
summary: The LGBTQIA+ Engagement Committee is an Executive Committee on Clemson Undergraduate Student Government. This committee exists to serve our LGBTQIA+ community through advocacy, community building, and resource sharing.

name: Turning Point USA at Clemson University
description: Turning Point USA is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization founded in 2012 by Charlie Kirk. The organization’s mission is to identify, educate, train, and organize students to promote the principles of freedom, free markets, and limited government. Turning Point USA believes that every young person can be enlightened to true free market values. Since its founding, Turning Point USA has embarked on a mission to build the most organized, active, and powerful conservative grassroots activist network on high school and college campuses across the country. With a presence on over 2,000 campuses, Turning Point USA is the largest and fastest-growing youth organization in America. Our chapter is nationally recognized by the official Turning Point USA.
summary: Turning Point USA is a nonpartisan, nonprofit organization whose mission is to identify, educate, train, and organize students to promote freedom. Turning Point USA believes that every young person can be enlightened to true free market values.

name: Clemson Office of Sustainability
summary: To promote the growth of a sustainable ethos; fostering change in global consciousness, for today and tomorrow.

name: Clemson Geology Club
description:
The Clemson Geology Club welcomes all students interested in geology. We plan a variety of activities like field trips, guest speakers, and social events, to help students explore and connect with the natural world. Additionally, we host review sessions for introductory geology classes and collaborate with faculty and geoscience professionals to provide exciting opportunities for learning about careers and research. 

Whether you're into rocks or simply enjoy being outdoors and learning about our world, the Geology Club is a wonderful place to connect with others and get involved.
summary: If you have a love for rocks, hiking, or learning about the geology around you, then the Clemson Geology Club might be the place for you! Join us to meet like-minded individuals and learn something new!

name: TigeRPG
description: TigeRPG is Clemson's tabletop role-playing games club. Tabletop role-playing games, such as Dungeons & Dragons and Pathfinder, are entertaining and organized cooperative activities where players take on unique characters formed from their imagination in an interactive and responsive world facilitated by a game master. We organize opportunities for students to experience this outlet of creative expression with predictable rules and unpredictable outcomes. We welcome all types of tabletop role-playing games and all levels of experience as we share the games we enjoy with others.
summary: Clemson's tabletop role-playing games club. We organize opportunities for students to experience this outlet of creative expression.

name: College of Agriculture, Forestry & Life Sciences
description:
The College of Agriculture, Forestry and Life Sciences is committed to our students and enabling each individual to achieve their academic and personal goals.  With vast curricula that explores plant, animal, human, molecular, and packaging sciences, the college serves to build upon Clemson University’s world-class academic, research and extension activities to produce scientific discoveries of global significance and graduates prepared to be leaders in their chosen fields. CAFLS is grounded in the fundamental principle that Clemson University is committed by its land-grant legacy to ensure that higher education remains a public good, and that land-grant colleges link their expertise to the needs of the people.

The Bookhart Student Services Center is available to all CAFLS students and works to ensure the academic, career and character success of every student.  The center is located off the main lobby of the Poole Agricultural Center and professional staff is available by appointment scheduling within CU Navigate, email or even drop-in support.
summary: The College of Agriculture, Forestry and Life Sciences offers degree programs in 12 academic majors.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America
description:
The Association of Latino Professionals for America is the first national Latino professional association in the United States, established in 1972. Our mission is to empower and develop Latinx people as leaders of character for the nation, in every sector of the global economy.

ALPFA offers its members professional leadership development, cross-functional and cross-generational networking, career opportunities, and advancement all the way to America's board rooms with hundreds of Fortune 1000 corporate partners. ALPFA has helped thousands of Latino professionals to gain the skills and connections they need to move from the outside of America’s economic mainstream to the center of it.
summary: Our mission is to empower and develop the Latinx community as leaders of character for the nation, in every sector of the global economy.

name: Adobe Digital Studio
description: Read more information at our FAQ page.
summary: A creative environment dedicated to providing Clemson with digital media resources and support.

name: Clemson Art Club
description: Clemson Art Club is an ISO that supports the creativity of Clemson students while giving students an hour each week to express themselves in a safe environment. Clemson Art Club has different themed meetings each week. For example, Bob Ross paint night, Thanksgiving Cards, Acrylic Paint night, and more. We buy all the supplies, and help our members with financial freedom with this club. We meet every Wednesday at 7pm for one hour.
summary: Our mission is to bring students together through the creation of art.  All skill levels are welcome! 😊

name: Clemson Reel Dialogue
description: We are Clemson's film club that revolves around viewing and discussing films from around the world! We have group discussions, trivia nights, and an annual Oscars watch party. Our organization works with the World Cinema program to publish student work online and in print. Since Spring of 2023, we've released an annual print publication that collates the best of that student work. For any interest in joining or any questions about our club, email us at clemsonreeldialogue@gmail.com!
summary: Reel Dialogue is both Clemson's best club for people who love to watch and discuss movies and Clemson's only undergraduate journal dedicated to film studies.

name: Clemson Chick-fil-A Club
description: The premise of the club is to come together to learn more about Chick-fil-A while enjoying their delicious options. Our vision is for our club to convene once a month to enjoy some delicious Chick-fil-A and learn more about the organization while we eat. We conduct community service, games and meals that bring students together to celebrate Chick-fil-A together.
summary: The Clemson Chick-Fil-A club exists to educate students on the professional and philanthropic aspects of the Chick-Fil-A brand and to promote community among Clemson students as they enjoy Chick-Fil-A meals at each meeting.

name: Clemson Paw Pantry
description: The Paw Pantry is available to any Clemson students with CUID. We try to accommodate students as much as we can without asking for financial proof. We expect users to be respectful of the resources. There is a 15-credit limit per week.
summary: On-campus food pantry and resource center available to Clemson students free of charge.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description: Sigma Alpha Epsilon is an IFC Fraternity that focuses on the development of all members to ensure that they are able to uphold high levels of Scholarship, Leadership, Friendship, and Service. We maintain a close relationship between our brothers and Clemson University as a whole through Philanthropy and Social Events.
summary: South Carolina Nu colony of the largest national fraternity

name: Clemson Canine Companions
description: Canine Companions® is a non-profit organization that enhances the lives of people with disabilities by providing highly trained assistance dogs and ongoing support to ensure quality partnerships. Volunteer puppy raisers provide specially-bred puppies a safe home, take them to obedience classes, serve up a healthy diet, provide socialization opportunities and give lots of love. Being on a college campus provides the optimal amount of stimulus for service dogs and allows the students to benefit from the dog training as well. Members of the club will assist with fundraising, dog sitting, and may even be a primary recipient of a dog.
summary: Canine Companions for Independence® is a non-profit organization that enhances the lives of people with disabilities by providing highly trained assistance dogs and ongoing support to ensure quality partnerships.

name: Transfer Academic Programs
description:
The office of Transfer Programs collaborates with campus partners to provide academic and social programs to support new transfer students as they transition to Clemson.

Some of the ways Transfer Academic Programs provides support to new transfer students is through the Transfer2Tiger Mentor Program (T2T) and Clemson University Student Government - Transfer Council. In addition to these student organizations, the office of Transfer Programs hosts various academic workshops and social events geared towards connecting the transfer community to their peers, campus activities and academic resources.

We are here to help transfer students feel they are a part of the Clemson Family!
summary: The office of Transfer Programs collaborates with campus partners to provide academic and social programs to support new transfer students as they transition to Clemson.

name: Clemson University Honors College
description: We challenge our students to excel by offering specialized courses with Clemson's best professors; by encouraging participation in a wide range of innovative learning experiences on campus, across America, and around the world; and by promoting intellectual engagement through formal and informal interactions with other Clemson students, staff and faculty. We encourage our students to create their own ideal education, by sponsoring programs such as study abroad and undergraduate research, and by providing individualized funding for internships, educational travel and professional development.
summary: The Honors College is a community of over 1,600 students who want to make the most of their academic and personal experiences at Clemson. Through small class sizes, sponsored programs, mentoring, and networking, you can create your ideal education.

name: Clemson Tiger Strut Dance
description:
The team is comprised of anywhere from twenty to twenty-three girls from a wide variety of backgrounds. We spend the whole school year working in the community, perfecting competitive numbers, working on our technical skills, training our bodies through early morning team lifts/cardio workouts, and forming lifelong bonds that change each and every member for the better. 

Prior, this team competes throughout the Southeast at national competitions and has won numerous awards in multiple genres of dance. We now attend The College Classic Nationals each year in Orlando, taking home the national winning title in 2023, with hopes of continuing on the championship reputation. We also make an effort to perform at events on campus (student unites and clemson life miracle), local art showcases, and the Clemson Dancer's Club showcase every year in April.
summary: Tiger Strut is Clemson's student-led Competitive Dance Team that is auditioned based! We go to The College Classic Dance and Cheer Nationals every season in Orland, FL. Clemson Tiger Strut Dance Team has a D1A Hip Hop National Championship Title!

name: Bioengineering Student Advisory Council
description:
*APPLICATION ONLY*

The Student Advisory Council for the Department of Bioengineering is composed of engaged upperclassmen undergraduate students in the Bioengineering Department. These individuals are successful students who possess a strong academic record, a drive to involve themselves in extracurricular activities, and a desire to build strong relationships with students, faculty, and staff in the department.

By representing the greater Bioengineering student body, the Student Advisory Council provides a source of internal feedback to the department. The Student Advisory Council is responsible for promoting department-wide events and seeking ideas for improvement from their peers. Council members will help enhance the Bioengineering Department experience by working in close liaison between students and faculty.
summary: The Student Advisory Council for the Department of Bioengineering represent the greater Bioengineering student body to provide a source of internal feedback to the department.

name: Undergraduate Pediatric Society
description: The purpose of the Undergraduate Pediatric Society is to encourage undergraduate pre-health students with interest in pediatrics. This will be a community open to all undergraduates at the Clemson University, regardless of age, major, or track in the healthcare field. As a society, we hope to foster a community that inspires an academic and feasible pursuit of pediatrics. This will be achieved by exposing pre-health students to healthcare professionals and local graduate schools that come speak to our club, as well and provide professional development skills to members on topics such as MCAT strategies, resume help, or graduate school application tips. Additionally, we hope to provide our members with volunteer and outreach opportunities in the community. Our goal as a society is to equip every member with a meaningful bedside manner and a greater understanding of their goals in the medical field.
summary: The mission of the Undergraduate Pediatric Society is to support undergraduate pre-health students with an interest in pediatrics.

name: FSL Wellness Advocates
description: The Fraternity and Sorority Life Wellness Advocates promote the development of an inclusive, healthy, and educated fraternity and sorority community. We serve all four Greek councils (CPA, IFC, NPHC, and MGC) and through programs and events about holistic wellness and mental health.
summary: The Fraternity and Sorority Life Wellness Advocates provide an inclusive fraternity and sorority community, where students can reflect and learn about safety and holistic health by providing resources as well as implementation of our programming.

name: Clemson Artificial Intelligence Club
description: The club's mission is to help students become active participants in the expanding AI community. Our club hopes to achieve this by offering instruction in the fundamentals of AI and ML, providing valuable knowledge about available AI-related resources, engaging in collaborative projects, hosting industry speakers, keeping members informed about the latest trends in AI technology, aiding in resume building, and facilitating discussions on the ethical and societal implications surrounding AI, among other activities.
summary: A platform for students of all educational backgrounds to get involved in the growing community around AI at Clemson University and across the world.

name: Alpha Eta Mu Beta
description:
Alpha Eta Mu Beta is the National Biomedical Engineering Honor Society. Established in 1979, Alpha Eta Mu Beta was formed to recognize and encourage excellence in the field of Biomedical Engineering and Bioengineering. Membership into Alpha Eta Mu Beta consists of those individuals in the field of Biomedical Engineering or Bioengineering who through their attainments in college or in practice have manifested a deep interest and marked ability in their chosen life work. The purpose of Alpha Eta Mu Beta is to bring these individuals into closer union so as to promote an understanding of the profession. Membership to Alpha Eta Mu Beta is a privilege and an honor. Members of Alpha Eta Mu Beta are recognized in an outstanding manner for having conferred honor on their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship, exemplary character, honorable activities, and leadership.

Through various development, training, networking and service opportunities, members of Alpha Eta Mu Beta are able to learn and grow. In this process, they develop critical lifelong skills that prepare them to excel for the post college environment or to transform individuals at their present jobs into leaders and innovators.

The field of Biomedical Engineering and Bioengineering are constantly evolving, therefore over the years AEMB has transformed itself into a dynamic organization to meet the needs of its growing membership and alumni, the institutions that they serve and our society.
summary: Alpha Eta Mu Beta is the National Biomedical Engineering Honor Society. Here at the Clemson chapter, we bring exclusive opportunities to our members and strive to serve the local community.

name: Clemson Society of Future Actuaries
description: The Clemson Society of Future Actuaries (CSFA) serves as a hub for any Clemson University students who are in the actuarial science program at Clemson, or are interested in becoming an actuary. The primary event held by the CSFA is the Actuarial Expo, where many companies can talk to students interested in pursuing an actuarial career path. During the expo, students can get to know what the companies are like and learn more about being an actuary. This is a common event for companies to hire students for internships and/or full-time positions.
summary: The Clemson Society of Future Actuaries (CSFA) serves as a hub for any Clemson students who are in the actuarial science program at Clemson, or are interested in becoming an actuary.

name: Clemson Hillel Jewish Student Organization
description: Clemson Hillel (the only on-campus Jewish Student Organization) aims to foster a community that is welcoming and accepting while also enriching the lives of Jewish students so that they may enrich the Jewish people and the world.
summary: Clemson Hillel aims to foster a welcoming and accepting environment for all Jewish students at Clemson University.

name: Edisto Research Association
description: Our objective is to establish a sense of community among and advocate for graduate students stationed at Clemson University - Edisto Research and Education Center (EREC). Additionally, the purpose of this organization is to provide in-person opportunities for graduate students to get involved in something outside of research. Edisto Research Association will provide EREC students with leadership, outreach, and professional development opportunities.
summary: Organization for graduate students who are stationed at Clemson University - Edisto Research and Education Center, Blackville for the duration of their program.

name: Chi Zeta - Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc
description:
• The Chi Zeta Chapter was chartered at Clemson University on Saturday, April 6,1974

    as the First Black Greek Lettered Organization on Clemson’s campus
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college.

name: Office of Advocacy and Success
description:
The Office of Advocacy & Success is committed to serving all current Clemson students as a trusted place for care, advocacy and referrals to campus and community partners.

 

Office Hours: Monday – Friday from 8 am – 4:30 pm

In the event of an emergency, call 911 or contact the Clemson University Police Department at 864-656-2222.
summary: Serving all current Clemson students as a trusted place for care, advocacy and referrals to campus and community partners.

name: The Olympic Lifting and Strongman Club
description: Clemson Weightlifting is a student-run organization committed to progressing in the sport of Olympic weightlifting, and hope to grow the sport. We meet as a club to train together and attend USA Weightlifting competitions together.
summary: Clemson Weightlifting is a group of people committed to progressing in the sport of Olympic weightlifting. We meet as a club to train together and attend USA Weightlifting competitions together.

name: Clemson Autism Advocacy and Inclusion
description:
The Clemson Autism Advocacy and Inclusion Organization aims to create a student body that is informed about both the true characteristics, as well as common misconceptions, of individuals with autism spectrum disorder. By becoming more knowledgeable about the autistic community, members of the organization will also become more comfortable interacting with individuals diagnosed with ASD. We look to support the autistic community in several different ways throughout the year. All are welcome, you do not have to have an autism diagnosis (or self-diagnosis) to join us (only an interest in advocacy and inclusion)! Our meetings typically consist of activities and discussions surrounding ASD as well as planning for educational events on campus. If you have any questions please contact us!

Members will play a key role in educating the Clemson Family on ways to support positive change within the autism community as a whole. This will be accomplished through raising money to support autism research, raising awareness about current autism policy issues, and raising the standard for autism inclusion throughout South Carolina.
summary: CAAI members play a key role in educating the Clemson Family on ways to support positive change within the autism community. This is accomplished by raising awareness about current autism policy issues, to create a higher standard for inclusion.

name: Crosspoint
description: Our mission is to make disciples of all people for God's glory on the campus of Clemson University. We hope to be a people transformed by the gospel, living daily on mission, and sending laborers to the nations.
summary: A campus ministry that desires to be a spiritual resource for Clemson's campus.

name: TEDxClemsonU
description:
Oversees the planning and organization of the TEDx event series at Clemson University. TEDx events offer students and faculty a chance to hear passionate speakers discuss new ideas and perspectives, which is beneficial to the entire Clemson community.

MEMBERSHIP: Request membership in the organizer and sign up for the newsletter.
summary: Oversees the planning and organization of the TEDx event series at Clemson University. TEDx events offer students and faculty a chance to hear passionate speakers discuss new ideas and perspectives, which is beneficial to the entire Clemson community.

name: Pam Hendrix Center for Education Abroad
summary: Fill out later

name: General Engineering Advising Center
description:
The General Engineering Advising Center serves all new engineering students, including transfer students.

We assist students with:

  • course selection
  • degree progress
  • developing academic and career goals
  • personal and professional development opportunities
  • addressing academic challenges
  • connecting students with resources and services on campus
  • getting involved outside the classroom
  • and navigating university academic policies and procedures
summary: The General Engineering Advising center serves first-year Freshman and Transfer students who intend to pursue engineering degrees.

name: Academic Success Center
summary: Academic Success Center

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Council
description: MEGSC will work to improve the graduate student experience within the Department of Mechanical Engineering. MEGSC will work to plan and put on regular social events open to all ME graduate students. This may include planning intramural and social sporting events open for all ME graduate students to take part in. Finally, MEGSC will propose at least one annual trip that is open to all ME graduate students. These social experiences will allow for students to develop relationships within the department that can create collaborative opportunities.
summary: The Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Council (MEGSC) represents the views and recommendations of the ME graduate student body to the Department of Mechanical Engineering in a manner that will mutually improve both entities.

name: Eat Well with Clemson Home
description:
Students will be awarded 50 Paw Points who attend three Eat Well nutrition events and set up a meeting with the nutrition team. During the meeting students will set a nutrition SMART Goal, follow @clemsondining on social media, and complete a feedback survey. All tasks need to be completed by December 3rd, 2025. Consultations can be scheduled any time by emailing nutrition@clemson.edu with Eat Well in the subject line.

Eat Well Nutrition Event Schedule:

 

Thursday 12:30pm-2:30pm

 

September 12th in The Dish at McAlister Dining Hall

September 26th in Schilletter Dining Hall

October 10th in The Dish at McAlister Dining Hall

October 24th in Schilletter Dining Hall

November 7th in The Dish at McAlister Dining Hall

November 21st in Schilletter Dining Hall

 

Please make sure to sign-in with our nutrition team during each event.

 

FAQ's

 

Q: When will I see the Paw Points in my account?

A: At the conclusion of each semester. 

 

Q: How do I set up my meeting with the nutrition team?

A: Set up your meeting any time by emailing nutrition@clemson.edu with Eat Well in the subject line. This can be done at any time during the Eat Well process!

 

Q: Where is my in-person consultation meeting located?

A: McAlister Dining Hall, at the nutrition table right across from the salad bar

 

Q: How long do I have to complete the program?

A: All steps must be completed by December 3rd, 2024 to receive the Paw Points for the Spring semester. No exceptions!

 

Q: Can I complete the program again the following semester?

A: Yes! However, the program restarts and the events you attended a previous semester will not be counted.
summary: Eat Well with Clemson Home is an incentive program designed to get students involved with Clemson Dining and learn about nutrition resources available to them.

name: Women in Chemical Engineering
description:
Women in Chemical Engineering (WChE) is a rapidly growing organization that strives to educate, empower, and advocate for women in chemical engineering, and their supporters. To accomplish this, the organization provides an open space for dialogue, mentorship, collaboration, camaraderie, and skill building through professional development events, community networking, and outreach to primary, secondary, and post-secondary institutions. All students, regardless of gender, are welcome and encouraged to join.

Our goals for this organization are to engage people to make connections within our own chemical engineering community here at Clemson and a larger chemical engineering community outside of Clemson as we foster successful careers. We work with other organizations and faculty members to host collaborative events, share resources, and foster a dialogue about inclusion that extends far beyond gender identity or expression.
summary: We will share resources and attend events to promote the development of women in chemical engineering. Our goals are to engage people in the department and make connections with the larger chemical engineering community as we foster successful careers.

name: Tigers for Life
description:
Tigers for Life is a pro-life student organization that is committed to enhancing the dignity and value of all human life. We exist to educate, equip, and empower students at Clemson University to support pregnant and parenting students and a build a culture of life on campus.

 

We actively support local pregnancy centers, adoption agencies, and foster care homes, as well as advocate for pro-life policies on campus at Clemson. 

 

Follow our instagram to stay up to date about monthly meetings, campus outreach, and community projects: @cutigersforlife
summary: Tigers for Life is a pro-life student organization that is committed to advocating for the dignity and value of all human life.

name: American Medical Women's Association
description:
Our organization will involve advocacy for women's health through fundraisers and education campaigns, and promoting connections between women in medicine. AMWA membership is inclusive. We encourage membership for anyone interested in medicine, women's advocacy, and making a difference – on a large scale and in Clemson! 

The American Medical Women's Association is a National association founded in 1915, and it has a vast network of doctors, medical students, and undergraduate students as members. Members of the association have access to multiple discounts from MCAT, tutoring, admissions consulting, and financial aid partners of AMWA. AMWA members can also apply for educational scholarships, global health scholarships, and travel grants to the national association meeting. 

As a member of the Undergraduate branch of AMWA at Clemson, you will receive opportunities for mentorship, leadership, advocacy, and education!
summary: AMWA is an organization focused on advocating for women's health and advancing women in medicine through mentorship, leadership, advocacy, and education.

name: Clemson Semper Fi Society
description: The Clemson Semper Fi society aims to embody the spirit of Semper Fidelis across Clemson University and throughout the Clemson Community. This will be accomplished by honoring the history and ideals of the United States Marine Corps, and by providing a space for Marine Officer Candidates, Marine Reservists, Marine Veterans, and anyone interested to interact and grow together both personally and professionally.
summary: The Clemson Semper Fi Society exists to connect Clemson students who wish to commission in the US Marine Corps after graduation. CSFS organizes training events for Marine Corps candidates in order to prepare them for success as Marine officers.

name: Clemson Sports Car Club
description:
The Clemson Sports Car Club provides a unique and safe opportunity for current students and alumni to learn how to control their car in a high-speed environment. We utilize our Facebook page as the modem for all our communication and event postings. Facebook: Official Clemson Sports Car Club

We host Autocross events once a month. The dates for this semester are as follows:

AutoX 1: 1/31

AutoX 2: 2/15

AutoX 3: 3/25

AutoX 4: TBD

 

See our Facebook Group (Official Clemson Sports Car Club) for registration and updates!!
summary: We offer the opportunity for students to interact at car meets, autocross, and other social events. We began operation in 1968 and are the oldest university car club in the nation.

name: Marketing Student Advisory Board
description: The Marketing Student Advisory Board is a student group that provides insight and assistance to the Department of and The Wilbur O. and Ann Powers College of Business at Clemson University. The mission of the Board is to facilitate interactions and ideas among Board members and act as a liaison between the marketing student body and the marketing department faculty and staff while developing, planning, and implementing specific targeted activities that advance both the Department’s goals and the students within the department.
summary: We serve to better the marketing department by serving as a voice for students and finding creative solutions with the Marketing Department faculty. We send a monthly newsletter and plan various trips to help enhance the marketing student experience.

name: Clemson Surf Club
description: Bringing the lost surfers of Clemson back to the waves! Our aim is to simultaneously provide a fun and safe environment for Clemson students to experience the surf culture on campus (Socials, Grill-outs, Fundraiser-Events) while also establishing the surf culture down by the coast (Winter Break Trip, Hurricane Surf Trips, Spring Break Trips, Beach Weekends and more). For all who are considering, stop considering because it's completely free to get involved.
summary: The goal of the Clemson Surf Club is to provide a time where the displaced surfers of Clemson, those who valued their degree just a little more than the waves, can meet, discuss, and occasionally practice the sport they love.

name: Clemson Indian Students Association
description:
We help the Indian students after they land in the US, right from airport pickups to temporary accommodation. We also have an induction program wherein our adviser speaks with the new students and guides them about the rules and regulations of the USA. 

We also celebrate major Indian festivals around the year.

We currently have a core committee of 25 people, and we have around a hundred members.
summary:
We are an organization mainly focusing on Indian Students.
We help all Indian Students after they land in the USA. 
Our faculty adviser is Dr.Rajendra Singh.

name: National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers
description:
As declared by the NOBCChE National Council, 

"Our vision is to be an influential organization ensuring that African Americans, Africans, and other people of color are fully engaged in shaping the global scientific community.

Our mission is to build an eminent cadre of successful diverse global leaders in STEM and advance their professional endeavors by adding value to their academic, development, leadership, and philanthropic endeavors throughout the life cycle of their careers."
summary: NOBCChE is an organization aimed at ensuring that African Americans and other people of color are fully engaged in shaping the global scientific community.

name: Clemson Backpacking Club
description:
The Clemson Backpacking Club is a student run organization that goes on backpacking and camping trips in the various parks and national forests throughout the southeast

Feel free to join our GroupMe to get in touch with us and get information on upcoming trips.
summary: The Clemson Backpacking Club is a student run organization that goes on backpacking and camping trips in the various parks and national forests throughout the southeast.

name: Under the Umbrella
description: "Under the Umbrella" is a club for transgender, non-binary, intersex, and gender nonconforming students to have a space where they can express themselves freely and find community with similar students here at Clemson.
summary: "Under the Umbrella" is a club for transgender, non-binary, intersеx, and gender nonconforming students to have a space where they can express themselves freely and find community with similar students here at Clemson.

name: The Aurantiaco
description:
Mission Statement: To foster a community where Clemson students in the humanities and social sciences fields can share their work and engage in meaningful dialogue with others in similar disciplines.

Vision Statement: To deepen the appreciation for the humanities and social sciences at Clemson, and to highlight their importance in our society through annual journals showcasing students' valuable work in the fields and events that cultivate dialogue among peers.
summary: The Aurantiaco is Clemson's journal for the humanities and social sciences. Authored, edited, and published exclusively by undergraduate students, it aims to create a space for students in these fields to publish their high-level academic work.

name: Rutland Institute for Ethics
description: “The purpose of the Institute for Ethics is to encourage discussion on campus, in businesses and in the community about how ethical decision-making can be the basis of both personal and professional success.” --Robert J. Rutland
summary: Founded in 2001 by Robert J. Rutland, the Rutland Institute for Ethics has been a major partner in helping Clemson University achieve its mission by fostering young, ethical leaders on campus and beyond.

name: English Club
description:
The main goal of EC is to promote a literary presence on campus. The group aims to create and publish creative projects on campus, support other campus art organizations and participate in department events, and provide a comfortable and creative space to share and discuss student work. You don't have to be an English major to join, all disciplines are welcome and encouraged!  

 

If you have any questions or would like to get connected, please contact us at englishclub@g.clemson.edu or join the Discord @ https://discord.gg/TK392JnKNM
summary: The English Club is dedicated to increasing the literary presence on campus through creative projects, career resources, and community.

name: Clemson Pickleball
description: We aim to be an organization that focuses on staying active and having fun by playing one of the fastest-growing sports in America, Pickleball. Pickleball is a sport that is similar to tennis, but is played on a smaller court with a whiffle ball and different types of paddles. We are looking to meet with a group of people that would be interested in gathering to play pickleball, or learning how to play if they never have before. All skill levels are accepted and no previous experience is required.
summary: We aim to be an organization that focuses on staying active and having fun by playing one of the fastest-growing sports in America, Pickleball.

name: Clemson Rodeo Team
description: The goal of the Clemson Rodeo Team is simply to connect fellow rodeo competitors, or even those that enjoy rodeo-associated activities and events, such as rough stock, roping, and barrel racing. The team will hold various fundraisers to help competitors pay for entry fees, organize clinics, and community outreach events to further bridge a relationship between the Upstate’s rich history of agriculture and Clemson University students. We are hoping to provide an opportunity of fellowship and sense of community for Western-related competitors and participants.
summary: Clemson Rodeo Team is to connect students who competes in or simply appreciate Rodeo Events, such as Barrel Racing, Team Roping, Breakaway Roping, or any variation of roughstock. You do not have to own a horse or ride, we welcome all!

name: Lyceum Fellows Program
summary: At the Clemson Institute for the Study of Capitalism (CISC), our mission is to explore the moral foundations of capitalism.

name: African Student Association
description: The African Student Association is a student organization which aims to foster greater interaction and unity among the African students here at Clemson. We do so by creating educational and interactive spaces that promote awareness of the African diaspora. We seek to build bridges with others from various backgrounds and encourage social discourse and engagement amongst ourselves and the Clemson community. ASA is open to all students, faculty, and staff as Clemson University interested in African culture and history.
summary: The African Student Association aims to foster greater interaction and unity among the students here at Clemson.

name: Delta Gamma
description:
Membership in Delta Gamma brings with it countless memories and experiences which will shape its members for years to come.

Consistent with our Founders' thoughts in 1873, our primary purpose is to create an environment for our members in which lasting friendships and our vows of sisterhood emanate all aspects of our Fraternity. We encourage an atmosphere which will foster high ideals of friendship among college women, promote their educational and cultural interests, create in them a true sense of social responsibility and develop in them high qualities of character.

Delta Gamma is more than just a collegiate experience; it is a lifetime of commitment, support and opportunity awaiting you. It marks the beginning of a relationship that will last for a lifetime.

The Pursuit of Doing Good is Never Done!
summary: Panhellenic women's organization. We are the Do Good Sisterhood!

name: FSL Inclusion and Belonging Ambassadors
summary: We are students from all four fraternity and sorority councils, including CPA, IFC, MGC, and NPHC who are working towards creating a more inclusive and diverse Greek community through opportunities for education and programming.

name: The Women's Network
description: The Women's Network is a community of high-achieving, motivated women who are “unapologetically ambitious.” TWN provides opportunities to network with female leaders in a wide array of professions in order to expose members to different industries and career paths.
summary: Revolutionizing networking for collegiate and recently graduated women. Creating a community of women that celebrates the desire to achieve, cultivating ambition to prepare the next generation of leaders.

name: Summer Start
description: Summer Start staff design and implement the Summer Start program, services, and transitional support. The Office of Admissions selects a group of incoming first-year students to complete two to three academic courses during the Summer II term before their first Fall semester through the Summer Start Program.
summary: Summer Start provides a select group of first-year students with an early opportunity to begin their Clemson academic journey.

name: Tigers In The Word
summary: The mission of Tigers in the Word Bible Study Ministry on Clemson University’s campus is to provide an exemplary example of what it means to be a Christian through biblical training that promotes a standard of living pleasing to God.

name: Clemson's Club of Neurodiversity
description: The central goal of Clemson's Club of Neurodiversity (CCN) is to raise awareness and grow a sturdy support system for those who are neurodivergent within the Clemson Community.
summary: Clemson's Club of Neurodiversity is an organization dedicated to the enrichment of those who are neurodivergent within the Clemson Community. We are also a huge refugee from who those who suffer from anxiety, depression, etc.

name: Women's Lifting Club
description: The Clemson Women's Weightlifting Club focuses on empowering women in and outside of the gym. We are a strong community who believe that a healthy mind and body will lead to success. Join us to meet other women who are passionate about pushing themselves physically and mentally through weightlifting.
summary: The Clemson Women's Weightlifting Club focuses on empowering women in and outside of the gym. Come join us to learn more! No experience required.

name: Clemson Battle Buddies
description: Battle Buddies is a nonprofit organization that raises, trains, and places service dogs with veterans suffering from PTSD. Battle Buddies also has a college puppy raiser program, where students at Clemson have the opportunity to raise one of the puppies in training for up to 14 months. Members of this organization will help with fundraising and events, and they may decide to apply to be a volunteer puppy raiser or puppy sitter.
summary: Battle Buddies is a nonprofit organization that raises, trains, and places service dogs with veterans suffering with PTSD. Members of this organization will help with fundraising and events. They may also decide to apply to be a volunteer Puppy Raiser!

name: Clemson D&D Club
description:
A centralized location for everyone to either run their own D&D game or join one.

For more information on meetings, resources, and to contact your DM, join our club discord with link below!
summary: A location for people who play D&D to find dungeon masters and other players to play along side of. We also have resources available for dungeon masters and new players alike.

name: Clemson NAACP
description:
The Clemson NAACP was established to achieve the objective of a more unified and equitable world. For more updates from the Clemson NAACP Leadership Team, please follow us on Instagram @ClemsonUNAACP.

The Clemson NAACP branch is divided into five distinct sectors, with an Executive Board and five committees: Political Action, Public Relations, Civic Action, Financial Services, Campus Relations. We must confront this time with intense activism and leadership if we are to move the needle on many existent concerns in the world we live in. It's time to get down to business, and it all starts with you. Tomorrow is counting on the actions of the present, so join us now.
summary: The NAACP's mission is to ensure the political, educational, equality of minority group citizens of States and eliminate race prejudice. The NAACP works to remove all barriers to racial discrimination through democratic processes.

name: SCAEYC Chapter at Clemson University
summary: The South Carolina Association for the Education of Young Children (SCAEYC) chapter at Clemson University seeks to promote developmentally appropriate practices in the field of early education to best meet the needs of young children.

name: Clemson University Teaching Fellows
description: • A partner of the SC Teaching Fellows program, Clemson’s program is a multi-year fellowship designed to recruit highly talented students to the teaching profession and help them to build leadership capacities through professional development, service, and enrichment opportunities. Students apply for the fellowship through CERRA when they are seniors in high school, and participating students receive financial tuition assistance for each year that they commit to the fellowship. As part of their engagement in Teaching Fellows, students commit to teaching in a SC public school upon graduation. While at Clemson, students participate in a variety of events that mostly take place on Tuesday evenings with some weekend activity. Each year, the new cohort of Teaching Fellows resides in the Teaching Fellows Living-Learning Community (LLC).
summary: Teaching Fellows is a recruitment and development program in partnership between Clemson College of Education and CERRA (The Center for Educator Recruitment, Retention, and Advancement).

name: Bake Back America
description: Bake Back America inspires individuals and communities to come together to perform acts of kindness. We aspire to empower, include, and give back. Our club will serve five sectors:  1. Shelters, foster care, and boys/girls clubs 2. Clemson Students 3. First generation students/financial challenges 4. Hospitals, Ronald McDonald houses 5. Recipient of our choosing. Bake Back America will contribute to these sectors with five programs including health/wellness, education, creativity, give back, and wishlist. We will hold bimonthly meetings to plan and fulfill our acts of kindness for our school and our community. Feel free to join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/86758891/LhXt3nF7
summary: Bake Back America inspires individuals and communities to come together to perform acts of kindness. We aspire to empower, include, and give back.

name: Clemson Motorcycle Club
description: Clemson Motorcycle Club (CMC) is a social organization built around a shared passion for motorcycle riding. Our mission is to promote motorcycle safety and rider awareness among both new and experienced riders. We foster a welcoming community by hosting regular meetups, group rides, events, and safety workshops that bring students together through a love of motorcycles.
summary: Clemson Motorcycle Club (CMC) is a social organization dedicated to motorcycles. Our mission is to facilitate safety and awareness to new riders as well as experienced riders. We give students the opportunity to be involved by hosting meet ups and rides.

name: DreamCatchers
description: Dream Catchers creates powerful connections for younger and older generations by empowering students and their local communities to fulfill the end-of-life dreams of hospice patients. Our vision is a world where age segregation is a thing of the past, barriers in connection across age groups are eliminated, and stigmas around aging and dying are wiped out. Many people faced with the end of their lives are alone and ignored. Our society tends to view aging negatively and elderly and terminally ill people as “unproductive” members of society. By connecting with those in hospice or palliative care, we are giving people at the end of their lives a newfound sense of joy and appreciation at a time when many are ignored and misunderstood. Our biggest mission is fulfilling the dreams of hospice patients, but we also organize intergenerational activities such as a bingo nights and raise awareness for conditions faced by hospice patients.
summary: We fulfill last Dreams of hospice patients. We receive a dream request from a hospice patient through our hospice partners, and we make that dream come true! We also do activities with our nursing home partners to create intergenerational connections.

name: Tigers Against Cancer
description: Tigers Against Cancer is a campus and community-based organization dedicated to creating hope and change in the fight against cancer. Each year, we host a cancer walk, a collaborative Relay for Life event that brings students, faculty, and local residents together for an evening of remembrance, celebration, and fundraising for the American Cancer Society. Beyond the event, Tigers Against Cancer continues its mission throughout the year—organizing fundraisers and outreach efforts to directly assist local patients facing the financial and emotional challenges of cancer. By bridging campus energy with community compassion, Tigers Against Cancer works to ensure that no one in the Clemson area faces cancer alone.
summary: Tigers Against Cancer is a nonprofit organziation that is directly associated with the American Cancer Society. Our goal is to spread awareness about Cancer prevention and screening and to raise money for cancer research through fundraising events.

name: Clemson Aspiring Educators
summary: We strive to support, prepare, and empower diverse, aspiring educators with numerous resources, networks, and opportunities. Aspiring educators will be equipped to make a difference in their schools, communities, and in all phases of their careers!

name: National Alzheimer's Buddies
description: By pairing student volunteers with dementia residents at a local facility, National Alzheimer's Buddies not only offers volunteers the opportunity to make a meaningful difference in the lives of Alzheimer’s residents and their families but also to develop a more robust understanding of their personal identity, build new social skills, and make new friends.
summary: Intergenerational response against the social isolation imposed by Alzheimer’s Disease.

name: Office of Human Resources
summary: Clemson HR

name: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air-Conditioning Engineers Clemson University Student Chapter
summary: The Clemson ASHRAE student chapter seeks to better inform our members about the diverse opportunities in the HVAC industry and to connect with and collaborate with industry professionals to encourage future growth in this field.

name: Clemson Cyber
description: Student organization for anyone with an interest in cybersecurity. Learn about cybersecurity at out weekly meetings along with participating in various cybersecurity competitions.
summary: Clemson's Cybersecurity student organization

name: Clemson Fashion Club
description: Clemson Fashion Club (CFC’s) hopes to encourage students to express their individuality and interests through style and fashion. A central community which promotes self expression will encourage members and non members to embrace creativity and diversity. CFC hopes to hold meetings and events in which students will be actively involved and learning about one of the largest and most profitable industries in the world.
summary: Clemson Fashion Club hopes to encourage students to express their individuality and interests through style and fashion. We will have activities, guest speakers, and events that enable students to explore their style.

name: K-Clemson
description:
Founded in 2022, K-Clemson is a dance and social club that focuses around K-Pop. Our organization is open to all cultural and performance backgrounds who would like to celebrate Korean Pop Music!

Every fall & spring semester, club members apart of our dance team perform K-Pop dances they have learned onstage. In addition to showcase, we host a variety of socials throughout the year to provide anyone who enjoys K-Pop a space to appreciate K-Pop with others, enjoy themed activities together, and build a network of long-lasting connections and friendships!
summary: K-POP Dance and Social Club at Clemson University! Open to all undergraduate, graduate, and Bridge students as well as all cultural and performance backgrounds to come together and enjoy K-POP. Monthly socials, collaborations, dance showcases, and more!

name: Nigerian Students in Clemson
description: The Nigerian Students in Clemson (NSC) organization provides a community for Nigerian students at Clemson University and conducts activities that support the academic, social, mental and emotional well-being of its members. The association also provides necessary information to prospective students.
summary: The Nigerian Students in Clemson (NSC) organization provides a community for Nigerian students at Clemson University and conducts activities that support the academic, social, mental and emotional well-being of its members.

name: Gift of Life Marrow Registry
description: Gift of Life is a marrow registry organization that aims to expand the registry in order to facilitate successful transplants to save those with blood cancer. At Gift of Life we believe every person battling blood cancer deserves a second chance at life — and we are determined to make it happen. We are singularly passionate about engaging the public to help us get everyone involved in curing blood cancer, whether as a donor, a volunteer or a financial supporter. It all begins with one remarkable person, one life-changing swab and one huge win — finding a match and a cure. As a Gift of Life campus ambassadors, it is important for us to grow the marrow registry as much as possible, and one of the most successful ways of doing this is tabling and presentations! Any volunteers are welcome to join!
summary: Headed by campus ambassadors for the national organization Gift of Life, the purpose of this group is to add members of the Clemson campus to the bone marrow and stem cell donation registry, to help patients find their perfect match.

name: Agribusiness Association
description: An organization for the students of agribusiness to gain internship and work opportunity, listen to guest speakers about the career field, and to volunteer in the Clemson community to show what really goes in to agriculture.
summary: An organization for the students of agribusiness to gain internship and work opportunity, listen to guest speakers about the career field, and to volunteer in the Clemson community to show what really goes in to agriculture.

name: Common Justice Initiative
description: We strive to foster common humanity through service, education, and outreach. Our organization seeks to create opportunities for incarcerated people. We raise funds to purchase books for our class at Perry Correctional Institute in Pelzer, SC. We also host various awareness events surrounding re-entry challenges alongside Soteria Community Development Corporation in Greenville, South Carolina.
summary: A group of students investigating mass incarceration in the U.S. in order to educate the Clemson community.

name: Clemson Waterski Team
description:
The ski team members have access to the 2025 Mater Craft Prostar, and represents Clemson University at NCWSA collegiate tournaments. There is no experience required to join the team and has all the equipment you will need! If you would like to join please email clemsonwatersports@g.clemson.edu or fill out this form https://forms.gle/gkJXC2BfPy7htQ78A

 

The Clemson Waterski team is a part of Clemson Watersports, when filling out the form listed above, please select "team" to join the competitive portion of the club.
summary: The ski team members have access to the 2019 Malibu TXi as well as represent Clemson University at NCWSA collegiate tournaments. There is no experience required to join the team and has all the equipment you will need!

name: Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society
description: The Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society, Clemson Chapter (SAACS) is a long-standing student chapter of the American Chemical Society, one of the largest scientific non-profits in the world. Our focus is on supporting our members and the student body at large to flourish as professionals in the world of chemistry. We host a large variety of events, whether that's our biweekly member meeting with fun crafts and activities, attending regional ACS meetings and conferences, hosting professional development events and guest speakers on campus, doing outreach to local schools, or managing fundraising events (such as our annual CH 1010 Lab Notebook Sale). All undergraduate students are welcome to join, whether your interests are in chemistry or not!
summary: The Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society is a professional development and social organization affiliated with the nationally recognized American Chemical Society. We focus on hosting outreach and social events, and attending conferences.

name: IEEE Intelligent Transportation Systems Society (ITSS) Student Chapter, Clemson University
description:
The IEEE Intelligent Transportation Systems Society (ITSS) Student Chapter at Clemson University is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering innovation, collaboration, and professional growth in the field of intelligent transportation systems (ITS). As part of the global IEEE ITSS community, the chapter provides a platform for students interested in ITS technologies including connected and autonomous vehicles (CAV), smart mobility solutions, and cybersecurity of transportation cyber physical systems (TCPS). Through technical workshops, guest lectures, research discussions, career talks, industry visits, technology demonstrations, voluntary activities, and networking events, the chapter connects students with industry professionals, researchers, and fellow TCPS enthusiasts. This chapter also encourages interdisciplinary collaborations, bringing students from diverse academic disciplines to contribute to real-world transportation challenges. By engaging in cutting-edge projects, competitions, conferences, and community outreach initiatives, the student chapter at Clemson University plays a pivotal role in preparing the future leaders in ITS.

The student chapter operates under the guidance of Dr. Mashrur “Ronnie” Chowdhury, the Eugene Douglas Mays Chair of Transportation in the Glenn Department of Civil Engineering with a joint appointment with the School of Computing at Clemson University.
summary: The IEEE Intelligent Transportation Systems Society (ITSS) Student Chapter at Clemson University is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering innovation, collaboration, and professional growth in the field of intelligent transportation systems.

name: Clemson University Police Department
description:
MISSION

Our mission is to enhance safety, reduce risk and prevent harm from affecting the Clemson University community.

VISION

Clemson University Police Department (CUPD) is an organization setting the standard of excellence in law enforcement through integrity, compassion, service before self and honor above all.

CORE VALUES

Honor, Integrity, Courage, Inclusion, Respect

GUIDING PRINCIPLES

We will collaborate with university partners to create and foster a safe, secure campus through education, community service and strategic enforcement. We will utilize innovative technology, specialized training, community policing strategies and strong partnerships to enhance the university’s living and learning environment.

We will create an environment that champions the success and well-being of our students, faculty, staff and visitors through professionalism and a commitment to service. We will foster a climate of trust and respect through a commitment to diversity, equity and procedural justice principles.
summary: CUPD works closely with students, faculty, and staff to create a safe living, learning, and research environment on the Clemson campuses. We work to provide professional, courteous, and effective law enforcement service to our community.

name: Clemson Forestry Club
description:
First and foremost, the Forestry Club serves as the student chapter of the Society of American Foresters. With this, we create an environment of growth and education for pre-professional foresters at Clemson University. This involves hosting prominent figures in the industry for educational meetings, workshops on professionalism and forestry skills, and networking opportunities through club meetings and meetings with the Appalachian Chapter of SAF.

Another facet of the club is creating connections to other forestry clubs throughout the Southeast through friendly competition. The Clemson Forestry Club competes in annual timber sports competitions throughout the year, including Conclave and Timberbeast. We also strive to make the club fun and build community among foresters with social events such as bonfires and cookouts.
summary: The Clemson Forestry Club is an organization primary focusing on timbersports and professional development. We have students from all majors that compete in log chopping, sawing, ax throw, archery, technical events, and many more.

name: Pee Dee Graduate Student Association
description: The Pee Dee Graduate Student Association was established especially targeting the off-campus students  staying at the Pee Dee Research and Education Center. With this organization, the students will get better opportunities to get connected and updated with the main campus activities & policies. This will help them to develop leadership and professional qualities on them.
summary: The Pee Dee Graduate Student Association was established to provide students located at the Pee Dee Research and Education Center with a strong sense of community while also allowing students opportunities for professional development and volunteering.

name: Clemson Undergraduate Real Estate Club
description: The Clemson Real Estate Club serves to facilitate events to expose students to different aspects of the real estate industry. The club’s mission is to provide students of any major interested in the topic of real estate with access to business leaders and professionals in order to expose students to varying knowledge of the real estate industry and networking opportunities. The Clemson Real Estate Club will work to accomplish this mission by hosting guest speakers, workshops, networking events, career information sessions, and training opportunities to prepare students to enter into the real estate industry.
summary: Clemson Undergraduate Real Estate Club serves to facilitate events to expose students to different aspects of the real estate industry. The club's mission is to provide members with access to knowledge about the industry from experienced speakers.

name: Research Troubleshooting Club
description: An open forum for research students to discuss the technical and procedural obstacles that occur in academic research. Students bring forward their specific technical issues, and the group as a whole provides ideas and solutions. Improving research speed and strengthening the community of research students; tackling two of the most difficult elements of academia.
summary: A student lead forum that discusses technical problems in members' research with the aim to solve those problems and producing a community that faces imposter syndrome head-on.

name: Clemson Game Development
description:
The Clemson Game Development Club is dedicated to fostering a supportive community for game development at Clemson University that provides tutorials, presentations, and interviews from developers in the industry to anyone interested in game development. No experience or previous education is necessary to join.

Each semester, we hold three game jams that are roughly a month-long each. These game jams each have one topic, such as "frost" that people are encouraged to work into their games or projects for the game jam. Throughout the time of a game jam, two ordinary club meetings will be held with either tutorials, presentations, or interviews with industry developers, and one meeting will be dedicated solely to giving people a chance to work together on the game jam. On the final meeting of the jam, we meet to showcase what we have accomplished to each other. Even if you only have ideas to show for a game, you are welcome to show whatever you have accomplished throughout the last month.

Our Discord
summary: A game development club that focuses on teaching all aspects including: game engines, music, art, and storytelling. We host monthly game jams to practice skills and team collaboration.

name: Clemson Friday Morning Swim
description:
Friday Morning Swim has something for everyone!! Come hang out with us, meet some new people, and start your morning right with a jump in the lake. Floaties and mugs are encouraged!

For updates, join our GroupMe here: https://groupme.com/join_group/88858692/cgKDU9VR or follow us on instagram @clemsonfridaymorningswim!
summary: Grab a donut and join us Friday mornings for a jump in the lake, to meet new friends, and start the day off great!

name: Office of Student Support and Empowerment
summary: Coming soon!

name: Clemson Guitar Club
description: The Clemson Guitar Club provides the opportunity for guitarists of all skill levels to learn, teach, and share their passion for the instrument. Guitarists looking for beginner or intermediate instruction are welcome to learn from our more experienced members at weekly beginner sessions, while advanced players looking to participate in ensembles may attend our advanced sessions right after. All members MUST bring their own guitar each week.
summary: Clemson Guitar Club offers free weekly guitar lessons open to all Clemson students. Additionally, the club holds a meeting once a week designed for advanced guitarists looking to jam in various styles.

name: German Professional Society
description: • The German Professionals Society (GPS) at Clemson University is an initiative aimed at promoting interest in German language, culture, and professional development. This club seeks to create a vibrant community for students who are passionate about Germany’s global influence across various industries, including business, engineering, arts, and technology. The GPS will offer networking opportunities, workshops, and talks with professionals experienced in working in Germany or German-speaking companies.
summary: The German Professionals Society (GPS) at Clemson University is an initiative aimed at promoting interest in German language, culture, and professional development. This club seeks to create a vibrant community for students who are passionate about Germa

name: Clemson Book Club
description: The purpose of this club is to build community through a shared love of reading/literature and create a space for those who want to discuss different ideas and themes of books they read. This club reads one book per month and meets every other week to discuss its contents in a socratic seminar setting. We are a relatively new club and so excited to continue reading and growing!
summary: The purpose of this club is to build community through a shared love of reading/literature.

name: Graduate Student Advisory Council of School of Mathematical and Statistical Sciences
description: We take complaints and\or valid issues from any graduate student in the School of Mathematical and Statistical Sciences, investigate them among GSAC members and then bring it up to the Assistant Director of Graduate Studies to get a solution if necessary. Otherwise, we try to give a solution by ourselves. We help the department by facilitating activities like picnics, recruitment week, or welcoming bridge/new students and conferences. We also have established a mentorship program for 1st and 2nd-year graduate students to get help in academics or anything they want from senior graduate students in the department. We meet once a week and once in two weeks with the ADGS.
summary: GSAC is an organization that collaborates with the department to enhance the experience of graduate life, solves valid issues with the consultation of the ADGS, and increases communication between the graduate body and Clemson University.

name: PERIOD. @ Clemson
description: PERIOD's goal is to end period poverty and the stigma around menstruation. PERIOD accomplishes this through education, service, and advocacy. To learn more about our national organization, visit this link. To join our Groupme, click here.
summary: Chapter of the national orginization PERIOD. that fights to end period poverty and stigma through education, service, and advocacy.

name: Transfer 2 Tiger
description:
The Transfer2Tiger Peer Mentor Program (T2T) is a peer mentoring program where mentees are matched with an upper-class transfer student (a mentor) based on reported academic, career, and personal interests to help navigate Clemson's academic and social environments and ease the transition to a new campus. 

We have monthly mentor meetings where we focus on leadership work, professional development, bonding, and more! There are always fun events through out the semester that are held for all transfer students to engage with one another, network, and to simply have fun!
summary: The Transfer2Tiger Peer Mentor Program (T2T) is a peer mentoring program where mentees are matched with an upper-class transfer student (a mentor)  to help navigate their transition to Clemson.

name: Association for Supply Chain Management
description:
An organization for supply chain and operations majors to gain internship/work opportunities, network with people in the field, listen to guest speakers, and learn more about the supply chain as a whole.

Stay in the Loop - Our Linktree
summary: An organization for supply chain and operations majors to gain internship/work opportunities, network with people in the field, listen to guest speakers and learn more about the supply chain industry as a whole.

name: Clemson Snow and Ski Club
description:
The purpose of Clemson Snow is to provide Clemson University students an outlet to share their passion for skiing and snowboarding. We organize day trips and weekend trips to nearby ski slopes in Tennessee, North Carolina, and Virginia. We also plan an annual trip to larger ski areas such as Colorado, France, Canada, Utah, and Vermont.

GroupMe:https://groupme.com/join_group/109262433/CgspWrtO
summary: A club at Clemson University to get people skiing and snowboarding!

name: Clemson Yachtbuilding Club
summary: This is an organization for people interested in learning about the yacht and boat-building industry.  We aim to teach members about the industry and process of building a boat by hand.

name: General Engineering
summary: General Engineering offers introductory courses that teach basic skills necessary for success in upper-level engineering curricula and in the engineering professions.

name: Clemson Bodybuilding Club
summary: We work to build a community likeminded students who work to be in the best physical & mental shape they can be! We hold social & training events to educate members on nutrition/training/posing & to give you opportunities to have fun and meet new people!

name: Clemson Heart Association
description: Clemson Heart Association is a non-profit club which raises funds along side of the American Heart Association to support the fight against heart disease and strokes.
summary: Clemson Heart Association is a non-profit club which raises funds along side of the American Heart Association to support the fight against heart disease and strokes.

name: Letters of Love
description:
We meet once a month to write letters to send to children who are spending time in hospitals due to chronic or terminal illnesses. The meetings usually last about an hour and its just a group of students who are taking a little bit of time out of their days to create a colorful and encouraging card to give a little extra hope to kids who are suffering. We also plan to hold fundraisers throughout the year in which the proceeds will be donated to the overall Letters of Love organization. Follow us on instagram to stay up to date about meetings and other activities!

instagram: lettersoflove_clemson
summary: We meet once a month to write letters to send to children who are spending time in hospitals due to chronic or terminal illnesses.

name: Think Strong
description:
Think Strong is created to bridge the gap between academics and mental health awareness through advocating for burnout prevention in students. We provide opportunities for students to improve their mental health by prioritizing life outside of acedemics, eliminating burnout, and creating a community of like minded adults.

Our mission is to 

  1. advocate for burnout prevention in students
  2. emphasize mental health awareness in education
  3. create a positive environment for students

We are partnered with The Jed Foundation, a nonprofit that protects emotional health and prevents suicide for our nation's teens and young adults.
summary: Think Strong is created to bridge the gap between academics and mental health awareness through advocating for burnout prevention in students.

name: Clemson Puppies with a Purpose
description: Clemson Puppies With A Purpose is a student-led organization that exists to support the Guide Dog Foundation for the Blind through volunteer puppy raising and assistance animal advocacy and education. Students can get involved as puppy raisers, puppy sitters/campers, or general members in order to help raise future guide dog puppies for GDF and provide assistance animal awareness to the community.
summary: Clemon Puppies with a Purpose is a student-led organization that exists to support the Guide Dog Foundation for the Blind through volunteer puppy raising and assistance animal advocacy and education.

name: Clemson University Design, Build, Fly
description:
Design, Build, Fly is an engineering design team that competes in AIAA Design, Build, Fly competition. The competition is to design, build, and fly an RC airplane that best meets the challenge rules of the current year.

There are many sub-teams, including Manufacturing, Aerodynamics, Structure, Electronics, Business, and Testing. Students from all majors and backgrounds are encouraged to join! Whether you have been building model airplanes for your whole life or have never seen one before, we have a spot for you and will teach you what you need to know to be successful!

If you're interested in joining, request to join on TigerQuest and we will reach out to you! You can also email us at flydbf@clemson.edu or DM us on Instagram @clemson_dbf.
summary: Design, Build, Fly is an engineering design team that competes in AIAA Design, Build, Fly competition. The competition is to design, build, and fly an RC airplane that best meets the challenge rules of the current year.

name: Clemson Crochets
description:
This is a very relaxed club for students who want to crochet together! Do you love to crochet and want to hang out with like-minded people? Are you a beginner seeking guidance from more experienced crocheters? Then this is the club for you! With no dues or attendance, this club is a great way to destress! Our meetings are every Wednesday from 5:30 pm to 7:00 pm in Powers 208 (check our Instagram @clemsoncrochets for more information)

Knitting welcome!
summary: A club for Clemson students who want to crochet/knit together!

name: Clemson Scholars of Finance
description: Scholars of Finance is a non-profit organization that aims to inspire character and integrity in the next generation of financial leaders. Through values-based leadership development, mentorship, and educational programming, it equips students pursuing careers in finance with the tools to lead with purpose, ethics, and long-term thinking. By fostering a community grounded in principles such as stewardship, humility, and integrity, Scholars of Finance seeks to transform the finance industry from the inside out, promoting a culture where principled leadership drives both personal success and societal good. We host speaker engagements, Weeky meetings for new members, and consistent meetings for veteran members
summary: Scholars of Finance is a non-profit organization that aims to inspire character and integrity in the next generation of financial leaders.

name: Kappa Delta Rho
