description: For more than a century, Kappa Delta Rho has promoted fellowship among its members and the advancement of truth, justice, and virtue, as exemplified by our Latin motto, Honor Super Omnia (Honor Above All Things).  We strive to develop educated gentlemen of character who promote human dignity, positive relationships among men, and moral excellence of the highest ideals.
summary: Founded in 1905, Kappa Delta Rho (KDR) is a social fraternity promoting leadership and character development. Emphasizing brotherhood, academic excellence, and community service, KDR provides a positive collegiate experience for its members.

name: The Redeemed Christian Church of God Campus Fellowship
summary: RCF is geared towards understanding and following the teachings of Jesus Christ as revealed in the bible. We believe that the bible is particularly relevant to navigating life as a student and strive to be good representatives of Christ on campus.

name: Phi Tau Sigma, The Honor Society of Food Science and Technology, Clemson Chapter
description: The mission of Phi Tau Sigma Clemson Chapter is to raise the stature and recognize scholarly achievements of the Food Science and Technology students, to promote the exchange of scientific information among food scientists and related disciplines through meetings, seminars, publications and other relevant activities. This mission is achieved by encouraging outstanding achievement by Food Science students and professionals, and by engaging professionals among Food Science and related disciplines professionals through the Phi Tau Sigma member network.
summary: The mission of Phi Tau Sigma Clemson Chapter is to raise the stature and recognize scholarly achievements of the Food Science and Technology students, to promote the exchange of scientific information among food scientists and related disciplines.

name: Women in Economics
description: Created in 2023, Women in Economics (WiE) is designed to support women majoring or interested in the economics field at Clemson University, specifically by providing tools on how to network and empowering all women to reach their personal goals despite the large gender gap the economic field faces. WiE hopes to provide adequate awareness about the lack of women in economic fields, and hopes to prove to women (and men!) that they are capable of reaching their desired goals. Study sessions with peers, a speakers panel of graduate economic majors at Clemson, and informative presentations are all things this club will strive for in order to empower the women in economics around us!
summary: Women in Economics is designed to support women majoring or interested in the economics field, specifically by providing tools on how to network and empowering all women to reach their personal goals despite the large gender gap the economic field faces.

name: Clemson EMS Club
description:
Clemson EMS club is a club for those who want to join a community of Clemson students involved in EMS or who are interested in learning more about the field. We share a common passion of helping others by providing the first line of care. No certifications are required and all are welcome.

 

Our Mission:

To get and keep people involved in EMS while at Clemson

To provide education to member and non members on basic life saving skills

To facilitate friendships among members
summary: Clemson EMS club is a club for those who want to join a community of Clemson students involved in EMS or who are interested in learning more about the field.

name: Society of Doctoral Students in Automotive Engineering
description:
The Society of Doctoral Students in Automotive Engineering aims to promote a supportive and inclusive community for doctoral students in the Department of Automotive Engineering to bolster their personal and professional growth.

We aspire to create opportunities to celebrate the diverse doctoral roster at CU-ICAR, instill a sense of camaraderie amongst the Ph.D. students in Automotive Engineering, and foster a safe support space for symbiotic collaborations.
summary: This organization aims to promote a supportive and inclusive community for doctoral students in Automotive Engineering that fosters camaraderie, helps build relationships, and bolsters professional growth through symbiotic collaborations.

name: Young Americans for Freedom
description: YAF seeks to built a community of students who are inspired by the ideas of individual freedom, a strong national defense, free enterprise, and traditional values. Through hosting speakers, we want to bring conservative speakers to campus to foster respectful debate and conversation. YAF wants to build a network of likeminded students together on campus.
summary: YAF seeks to built a community of students who are inspired by the ideas of individual freedom, a strong national defense, free enterprise, and traditional values.

name: Asian Business Student Association
description: The Asian Business Student Association (ABSA) is a multicultural and Asian-inclusive business organization that fosters the professional growth of business leaders in the Wilbur O. Ann Powers College of Business. In addition to celebrating and acknowledging people of diverse backgrounds, we aim to provide leadership development, cross-cultural networking, and career opportunities to help our members gain the skills and connections they need to succeed in the professional environment.
summary: The Asian Business Student Association (ABSA) is a multicultural business organization that fosters the professional growth of business leaders in the Wilbur O. Ann Powers College of Business.

name: Clemson Tiger Racing
description: Clemson Tiger Racing is a club dedicated to teaching students the art of motorsport racing. In part with the education of proper racing technique, etiquette, and car control, we participate in track competitions with other universities. Dues are required to be paid in order to become a full member! Please join the discord for all club information - https://discord.gg/NrPDK4CMwg
summary: Clemson Tiger Racing is a club dedicated to teaching students the sport of motorsport racing. In part with the education of proper racing technique, etiquette, and car control, we participate in track days in competition with other universities.

name: Clemson Build
description:
Intent: To build a collective of high-performance individuals dedicated to pushing boundaries, expanding perspectives, and creating value for others through entrepreneurship. We exist to ensure no one bears the pressure of ambition alone, replacing isolation with a peer group committed to active support, serendipitous growth, and genuine emotional connection. Don't burn alone.

Membership is open to all young entrepreneurs. Aspiring, active, or retired.
Join us everywhere through our linktree  https://linktr.ee/clemsonignite

As part of our dedication to transparency, we have made our finances public, which are available at https://hcb.hackclub.com/clemson-build.
summary: Stop action faking. Start building. Build is an ecosystem for doers. We trade generic networking for "Vibe Code" sessions, pitch roasts, and cross-club collabs. We provide the pressure to perform and the community to support. Don't burn alone.

name: College of Engineering, Computing, and Applied Sciences Graduate Student Advisory Board
description:
The CECAS Graduate Student Advisory Board exists to encourage and advocate for graduate students and their well-being while being a bridge of communication to administration at Clemson. GSAB promotes diversity and inclusivity as they help incorporate students into the Clemson Family. CECAS GSAB provides social events and townhalls in order to create networks between college of engineering and applied science departments. 

If you would like to stay connected and be up to date on events happening for our CECAS Grad students- please feel free to join us on Tiger Quest. If you want to see about openings to actually obtain a position on the board- please contact us at cecasgsab@clemson.edu!
summary: The CECAS Graduate Student Advisory Board exists to encourage and advocate for graduate students and their well-being while being a bridge of communication to administration at Clemson.

name: Women in Mechanical and Automotive Engineering
summary: A group focused on retention and recruitment of women in engineering, achieved through building a network of community members, providing critical mental and academic support, preparing for future success and establishing life-long relationships.

name: Clemson Kappa Alpha Pi - Pre Law & Government Fraternity
description:
**Please do not request to join on Tiger Quest unless you have been offered an acceptance bid.**

Kappa Alpha Pi is a gender-inclusive pre-law and government fraternity. The fraternity is open to all undergraduate students interested in law school and/or government service, regardless of major. The chapter will participate in social events and bonding, mentorship programs, leadership and professional development, LSAT preparation through intercampus hybrid LSAT courses, and law school admissions officer visits.

Recruitment will be held in the Fall and Spring semesters. Please follow us on Instagram @clemsonkapi to keep up to date with our recruitment schedules.
summary: Clemson KAPi, Kappa Alpha Pi, is a gender-inclusive pre-law and government professional fraternity. Within the Sigma Chapter, we welcome undergraduates passionate about law school and/or government service, irrespective of their field of study.

name: Clemson Alumni Association
summary: We are the conduit that brings together alumni of all ages and from all geographies to keep their flame for Clemson alive and glowing.

name: Interpersonal Violence Resource Alliance
description:
Mission

The Interpersonal Violence Resource Alliance (IVRA) aims to educate, advise, and provide inclusive advocacy to the Clemson University community on matters related to interpersonal violence by offering training opportunities and streamlined resource information. The alliance aims to collaborate to implement consistent resources to support and educate Clemson University students, faculty, and staff.

Purpose

The Interpersonal Violence Resource Alliance is a group of trauma-informed resources available to the Clemson University community, consisting of representatives from offices across campus who provide interpersonal violence-related services. These services range from prevention education and response training to direct services supporting students who have been victimized. As part of Clemson’s commitment to be an outstanding place to live, learn, work, and grow, IVRA will work with members of the University who are survivors or allies to use the alliance as a resource.

  • Request a training- Request form linked here.

IVRA, as a whole, is a non-emergency group dedicated to serving those affected by interpersonal violence. If you or someone you are supporting is in immediate danger or feel unsafe, call 9-1-1 or the Clemson University Police Department at 864-656-2222.

 

Current Members

  • SPARC Stalking Victim Resource

  • First Light
  • Safe Harbor
  • Julie Valentine Center
  • SCCADVASA
summary: The Interpersonal Violence Resource Alliance aims to educate, advise, and provide inclusive advocacy to the Clemson University community on matters related to interpersonal violence.

name: CUSG Campus Advancement Committee
description: We are a committee within CUSG dedicated to making Clemson a better place for everyone! We are typically described as the CUSG "catch-all" committee. Our subcommittees consist of Environmental Advocacy, Student Wellness, and Community Outreach. The majority of our work is accomplished through both on-campus events, off-campus events and distribution of the Sustainability Initiative Fund.
summary: Campus Advancement is a committee dedicated to improving Clemson for the betterment of the students, environment, and community. We do this by creating events that focus on sustainability, student wellness, and community service.

name: Clemson Undergraduate Consulting Association
description:
The Clemson Undergraduate Consulting Association (CUCA) is Clemson’s premier hub for aspiring consultants, open to all students regardless of major or experience. Focused on management consulting, our organization fosters collaboration across colleges, providing essential resources such as application timelines, case study prep, guest speakers, and interactive workshops. Our goal is to equip students with the knowledge and skills to secure internships and full-time roles in the consulting industry.

As a key part of Clemson’s consulting ecosystem, CUCA works closely with the Clemson Consulting Consortium and 180 Degrees Consulting, featuring their members and projects in our meetings. With hands-on training, mentorship, and a strong network, we aim to build Clemson’s reputation in the consulting world and create a direct pipeline into top firms.
summary: CUCA serves to facilitate events to expose students to opportunities within the consulting industry, connect students with business leaders and other professionals within consulting, and train them in case interviews in order to break into consulting.

name: Annual Giving Office
description: Annual gifts to Clemson help us support top students and faculty, the heart of the University’s purpose. By establishing a personal tradition of annual giving, donors help the University rise into the ranks of the nation’s finest institutions. This rank keeps the brightest students in the state and attracts industry and higher paying jobs.
summary: Annual gifts to Clemson help us support top students and faculty, the heart of the University’s purpose. By establishing a personal tradition of annual giving, donors help the University rise into the ranks of the nation’s finest institutions.

name: Tigers 4 Accessibility
description: Tigers 4 Accessibility is committed to creating a more inclusive and accessible environment, both on campus and within the greater Clemson area. We collaborate as a team to organize an annual Accessibility Awareness Week in March, discuss with the administration to identify ways to enhance campus accessibility, and raise funds to provide mobility aids to medically underserved members of the Clemson community.
summary: Tigers 4 Accessibility is a student organization dedicated to fostering accessibility, awareness, and acceptance for people with disabilities through both physical and social initiatives.

name: Clemson Pokemon GO Club
description: The Clemson Pokemon GO Club provides a medium for Clemson students to meet new people, work together, and have fun playing Pokemon GO. We welcome all players, regardless of level, to join us for in-game activities like spotlight hours, 5-star raids, community days, and more! The Club will formally meet once a week on Wednesdays at 5:30PM, then participate in Raid Hours from 6:00-7:00PM. There will also be optional, informal opportunities to come and play with us. Our GroupMe is linked via the "globe" icon on this page, as well as our Instagram page. Check them out for more information! Let's GO!
summary: We seek to bring all players of Pokemon GO at Clemson together to level up, raid, and have fun!

name: Planning Student Organization at Clemson
description: Planning Student Organization at Clemson is a student-led organization which promotes networking and professional growth in the city, urban, and regional planning profession.
summary: Planning Student Organization at Clemson is a student-led organization which promotes networking and professional growth in the city, urban, and regional planning profession.

name: Vaw Clemson Dental Team
description:
develops community health systems while providing interns the opportunity to work alongside experienced local healthcare professionals. VAW Global Dental Interns work closely with community partners to implement dental clinics across low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). In the Dental Outreach program, interns learn about healthcare in a global context. Through shadowing local dentists, interns will also learn about dental procedures, treatment courses, clinic operations, and disinfection and sanitation techniques. 

nterns will prepare for their trip throughout the year by attending team training sessions, participating in local community service projects, and developing team building activities, led by their Team Leader or Faculty Team Leader. Interns are required to attend trainings and workshops prior to their trip, so that they are well prepared upon arrival in country.

This is an intensive internship — valuable to future dentists, dental hygienists, dental assistants, as well as other future healthcare providers, global practitioners, and anyone interested in gaining skills in public health while supporting healthcare systems around the world.
summary: VAW Global Dental Interns work closely with community partners to implement dental clinics across low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). In the Dental Outreach program, interns learn about healthcare in a global context.Through shadowing local dentist

name: Clemson Remote Area Medical
summary: Clemson Remote Area Medical aims to provide Clemson students the chance to participate in Remote Area Medical (RAM) clinics held across the nation providing free medical, dental, and vision services to those who are unable to afford or access healthcare.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha: Eta Lambda
description: Pi Sigma Alpha is the national honor society for undergraduate and graduate students of Political Science. Clemson’s Eta Lambda Chapter provides undergraduates with opportunities to get involved in the community and obtain information about Clemson’s Political Science Department, internships at the local, state, and national level, discuss current political issues, and network with other students, professors, and local business, political, and educational leaders.
summary: An invite-only national Political Science Honors Society, whose local chapter values giving back to the Clemson Community through scholarship and service.

name: Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student Organization
description:
Purpose of PandA GSO shall be to:

  1. Build solidarity among the Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student Body, 
  2. Promote better communication among graduate students, faculty, and Clemson University Administration,
  3. Organize professional, social, recreational, cultural, and intellectual enrichment for Graduate Student Body, and
  4. Promote leadership and professional development among the Graduate Student Body.
summary: PandA GSO aims to build solidarity among the Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student body by planning social and professional development events and by working with faculty to better the department.

name: American Water Works Association - Water Environment Federation Student Chapter
description:
This organization connects students to professionals in the water/wastewater/environmental industries. This is done through events such as career panels, guest speakers, and local outreach (lake cleanup).

Join our GroupMe to stay up to date! https://groupme.com/join_group/96190093/Cqy5JN4D
summary: This organization connects students to professionals in the water/wastewater/environmental industries. This is done through events such as career panels, guest speakers, and local outreach (lake cleanup).

name: Nintendo Club
description:
We host free to attend casual gaming tournaments, in person meetings and hang outs to discuss our shared interest, watch parties for Nintendo Directs and media, and even more!

To stay updated on meetings and events, join the club for weekly emails and to gain access to our discord server via the link in our documents.
summary: We host free to attend casual gaming tournaments, in person meetings and hang outs to discuss our shared interest, Nintendo Direct watch parties, and even more fun events!

name: As You Are
description: As You Are is an all girl bible study that focuses on welcoming girls with open arms. So that they feel comfortable to learn about the Bible and they don't have to fit a certain mold, but can come as you are.
summary: As You Are is an organization that creates community amongst girls on campus as they learn to have an everyday relationship with God. Worship nights are once a semester and bible studies are held once a week.

name: Clemson Campus Recreation Fitness and Wellness
description: Clemson Group Fitness strives to offer the most safe, effective, and innovative programs to students and members of all abilities. Our classes are taught by certified instructors, all of which are current students. We offer over 80 weekly classes during the Fall and Spring semesters. We offer a wide variety of classes that will help you reach your fitness and wellness goals whether they focus on cardiovascular training, strength training, or flexibility. Join our group to stay up to date with all of our special fitness events throughout the semester! Learn more about our program by visiting our website at https://www.clemson.edu/studentaffairs/get-involved/cr/fitness/index.html
summary: Clemson Group Fitness strives to offer the most safe, effective, and innovative programs to students and members of all abilities. Our classes are taught by certified instructors, all of which are current students.

name: Clemson Libraries
description: Clemson Libraries has six locations to serve you — Cooper Library, the Gunnin Architecture Library (Lee Hall), the Education Media Center (Tillman Hall), Special Collections and Archives (Strom Thurmond Institute), the CU-ICAR Library (Greenville), and the Charleston Design Center Library. Our collections contain more than 1.2 million print materials and more than 1.9 million digital and electronic resources, such as e-books, journals, and streaming media titles. We can also provide access to research materials at other South Carolina libraries through PASCAL Delivers and around the world through interlibrary loan. Our technology check-out collection and our Adobe Studio and Makerspace can provide assistance for just about any kind of creative project, and our dedication team of librarians and information experts can help you access the resources you need to succeed. Come visit one of our locations and let us help you take your next research or creative project to the next level.
summary: Clemson Libraries has six locations, more than 3 million print and digital collections items, and a dedicated team of library professionals who can help you with your research assignments and other projects.

name: Social Media Listening Center
description: The Social Media Listening Center is an interdisciplinary lab that utilizes cutting edge technology to listen, measure, and engage in online conversations across various digital and social media platforms. Its mission is to empower students and faculty from across the university to leverage the power of analytics and artificial intelligence to better understand how information spreads, resonates, and performs.
summary: An interdisciplinary lab built to harvest, analyze, and interpret social and digital media.

name: Clemson Stargazing Club
description: Clemson Stargazing Club promotes mental wellbeing in the form of stargazing. Each month, members are invited to join us in a night of relaxation where we will look at the stars. Stargazing is a great way to let yourself unwind amidst the hectic college environment. We believe in having time to step away from academic requirements and value your personal mental health. We hope to see you under the stars soon!
summary: Clemson Stargazing Club offers a relaxing activity for students looking to destress by offering monthly stargazing meetings.

name: Clemson Religious Studies Club
summary: An academic club focusing on the interdisciplinary study of religion. The primary goal of this student-led organization is to further students' knowledge and strengthen relationships among peers through discussions, movie nights, and study groups.

name: Clemson Pre-Anesthesiologist Assistant Club
description: The Pre-Anesthesiologist Assistant club is aimed at assisting students that are interested in pursuing a career as an anesthesiologist assistant. It provides students with numerous opportunities to expand their knowledge about the field and expectations associated with acceptance into an anesthesiologist assistant program. The Pre-Anesthesiologist Assistant club meets with several programs, anesthesiologist assistants, and has community service opportunities. To join the Pre-AA GroupMe, please use this link: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/98106191/gsqXewPJ
summary: This club will provide students who are pursuing a career as an anesthesiologist assistant to become part of a community of like-minded individuals, network with AA programs and anesthesia providers, and learn more about the profession

name: College of Education Graduate Student Advisory Board
description:
The mission of the College of Education Graduate Student Advisory Board (CoE GSAB) is to promote the welfare of the graduate student body, to enhance relationships between students and faculty, and to serve as ambassadors for the College of Education.

We have many ways for you to get involved! If you're looking for a leadership role and can offer a greater time commitment, consider running for an Executive Board Role and serving as either a Chair or Co-Chair of a committee. Joining a committee is a great way to get started and learn more about our mission and activities. Available committees include the Graduate Student Conference Committee, Peer Mentoring Committee, Outreach Committee, Advocacy Committee, and Academic Writing Committee. 

Members must be enthusiastic about the College and willing to share their commitment with others. Members should be interested in governance. The GSAB will consist of a diverse group of education majors, each bringing a unique perspective to the Executive Board. All CoE GSAB members must be currently enrolled.

For meeting agendas, recordings, and reports, you can access our Box here.

GSAB members, provide your feedback here.
summary: The mission of the College of Education Graduate Student Advisory Board (GSAB) is to promote the welfare of the graduate student body, to enhance relationships between students and faculty, and to serve as ambassadors for the College of Education.

name: Financial Management Association
description: To get involved, join this year's GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/109518801/joAIVgXH
summary: Our mission is to provide a fully integrated program that combines education, career preparation, industry intellectual capital, and alumni engagement to prepare students for careers in finance.

name: Clemson FinTech
description: Dedicated to fostering innovation in the ever-evolving realm of financial technology, the Clemson Fintech Club serves as a platform for students to explore, learn, and engage with cutting-edge trends in finance and technology. Through industry workshops, networking events, and startup projects, we will provide our members with the knowledge and skills necessary to thrive in the rapidly changing landscape of FinTech.
summary: At Clemson FinTech, we aim to build real-world experience in the ever-evolving industry of financial technology.

name: Clemson Flag Football
description: Clemson Flag Football is open to any woman eager to explore the world of flag football at the next level. Women's Flag Football has been a growing sport around the nation, and with our club, we want to provide a supportive environment where players can learn, grow, and excel in the game we all love. We are open to anyone at any skill level. Please reach out if you have any questions or are interested in joining!!
summary: Clemson Flag Football is a student-run organization committed to providing a supportive environment for women on our campus while allowing these female athletes to take flag football to the next level.

name: IfYoureReadingThis Clemson
description: The mission of IfYoureReadingThis.org is to serve as an open-ended university-wide support network to help students struggling with anxiety and depression, as well as help students with mental illness feel comfortable talking to somebody, by identifying the people who want to listen, and by showing an outpouring of love and understanding
summary: IfYoureReadingThis is a mental health community that empowers students to build and tap into their support networks by amplifying the faces and stories of our communities.

name: American Society of Biomechanics Student Chapter at Clemson University
description: The American Society of Biomechanics (ASB) Student Chapter at Clemson University will contribute to the professional development of biomechanists by engaging industry experts who routinely use biomechanics and those in allied fields, such as robotics, to help our chapter members learn to communicate their findings to a broader audience, help keep biomechanics broadly relevant to society, and form new professional networks.
summary: It is a Clemson University's American Society of Biomechanics (ASB) student chapter.

name: Clemson Yoga Club
description: Clemson Yoga Club is dedicated to fostering wellness, mindfulness, and community amongst Clemson students with or without yoga experience. Our sessions aim to help you deal with the mental and physical challenges of being a student. We will teach various yoga styles and promote self-growth.
summary: Clemson Yoga Club is dedicated to fostering wellness, mindfulness, and community amongst Clemson students with or without yoga experience.

name: Themed Entertainment Association at Clemson
description: The Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) at Clemson brings together students interested in theme parks, roller coasters, and more. We attend networking events, plan park trips, and learn more about the amusement industry.
summary: The Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) at Clemson brings together students interested in theme parks, roller coasters, and more. We attend networking events, plan park trips, and learn more about the amusement industry.

name: STEM Start
summary: STEM Start is for students that have been admitted to the Summer II STEM Start Program.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) is a community-based, nonpartisan, and nonprofit with the mission of uniting Clemson's Vietnamese students. The purpose of our organization is to foster communal cohesion while endowing the next generation with the leadership skills, cultural awareness, and discernment necessary to fortify and progress our community.
summary: By connecting students through the wonders of the Vietnamese culture, the Vietnamese Student Association at Clemson aims to strengthen the community amongst students.

name: Ghanaian Students in Clemson
summary: The Ghanaian Students in Clemson (GSC) organization would provide a community for Ghanaian students at Clemson University. GSC would create an amicable academic atmosphere and provide necessary information for prospective students.

name: Clemson Writing Lab
description:
Unlock your writing potential at the Clemson Writing Lab! We're dedicated to helping students excel in their writing endeavors. Whether drafting an essay, refining a research paper, or polishing a presentation, our expert consultants offer personalized, one-on-one guidance. We assist with essays, research papers, resumes, creative writing, and more, helping you express your ideas clearly and confidently.

Our consultants provide constructive feedback and writing strategies in a collaborative environment. We also offer workshops on grammar, punctuation, and research techniques, with 24/7 online resources. Join our community of writers committed to excellence. Write your success story with us!
summary: Unlock your writing potential at the Writing Lab! Our consultants offer personalized support for all stages of writing, from brainstorming to final edits. Elevate your writing in a collaborative and supportive environment. Write your story with us!

name: Phi Kappa Psi
description: For the past 170 years, Phi Kappa Psi has played a critical role in shaping the lives of its members. Each member has taken an oath and committed themselves to upholding the values of our Fraternity as committed scholars and upstanding citizens who recognize that our role on this earth is to serve something or someone much greater than ourselves. Phi Kappa Psi maintains the values that are the core of the Fraternity while integrating what we stand for into the ever-changing world in which we live. Providing value-driven experiences for our members is what we strive for.
summary: Phi Kappa Psi develops members of character by providing them with the proper environment and tools to pursue productive, honorable, and purposeful lives.

name: Biomedical Applications Club
description: As a club, we work on various projects throughout the semester in order to develop and strengthen skills that are applicable to Biology and Bioengineering. Our most recent projects include a toy car powered by muscle impulses, rat adipose derived stem cell culture, porcine heart suturing, and more! We strive to explore BIOE and biology in a hands-on way through projects that are interesting, useful, and most importantly, fun.
summary: Previously known as the Bionics club, our goal is to develop and progress skills that may be applicable to the fields of Biomedical Engineering and Biology.

name: Parkinson's Together
summary: We are a national 501(c)3 nonprofit focused on bringing awareness of Parkinson's and how it impacts the individual and their families to college campuses. Our main goal is to get reach individuals with Parkinson's in the Clemson community.

name: Clemson Women's Wrestling Club
summary: Clemson Women's Wrestling Club is a group of women that love wrestling or want to learn something new! A space to grow your wrestling skills and leadership as well as a place to meet new people and have new experiences! Anyone is welcomed to join.

name: People Advancing Women's Empowerment
description:
We are a group of students who acknowledge the differences and unique experiences each person holds, especially on Clemson's campus. We want to create a space for people of all genders to come together while supporting women in leadership roles and create unique opportunities to grow in our community, grow in our academics, and grow ourselves. We hold regular meetings, each falling under one of our designated four categories:

Our Women in Leadership Speaker Series! - We value outside viewpoints, personal and professional experiences, and passion. We host speakers to allow our members to gain insight into real-world experiences, specifically highlighting women and minorities in their field of expertise.

Professional Development - We emphasize improving our members' awareness within their own lives and careers while on and off campus and would love to encourage them through training and self-awareness.

Networking - We want our club members to create connections and build relationships with fellow students and organizations here at Clemson and in the professional world beyond Tigertown.

Service Opportunities - We see our campus and community here at Clemson and want to give back just as it has given to us! We want to designate time for our members to serve while bettering themselves and the community.
summary: PAWE is a new club on campus, which stands for People Advancing Women's Empowerment! It is a gender-inclusive club that will be hosting our ongoing speaker series, networking, and professional development events.

name: HEARTFIRE
description: HEARTFIRE club here at Clemson invites and includes anyone who is inspired to change the lives of others. Heartfire Missions is a non-profit organization dedicated to spreading the love of Christ through various outreach and serving people in need. Our mission includes trips to support local villages and provide medical and humanitarian aid. Partnering with churches, hospitals, and other organizations makes a tangible impact on the lives of those we serve. Heartfire Missions focuses on ministry to address spiritual and physical needs. We provide medical, financial, educational, and humanitarian relief for the people we serve. We invite individuals, believers and non-believers, to participate through volunteering, donating, and engaging in mission trips. Our vision is to empower a new generation of world-changers by taking young adults out of their comfort zones and into impoverished villages and hospitals, where we can witness miracles and undergo life-long transformations. Since its launch in 2017, HeartFire has impacted countless people in several countries worldwide. With 245 surgeries performed each year, improving and saving lives, providing critical medical supplies for surgeries, and fostering hope above all. We emphasize diversity, community service, and global impact. By fostering a community of dedicated supporters overall and as a club here at Clemson, we seek to create a ripple effect of positive change, spreading Christ's love far and wide through serving others.
summary: HEARTFIRE trains future healthcare and non-healthcare leaders to sharpen their medical skills and advance their humanitarian aptitude; then provides unique opportunities to travel both locally and globally on life-changing mission trips.

name: Jack A. McKenzie ’76 Leadership Endowment
description:
The Alpha Phi Omega Jack A. McKenzie '76 Leadership Endowment is designed to support the professional development of students by providing travel grants for those in need of financial assistance. This endowment aims to empower both undergraduate and graduate students at Clemson University who are seeking to broaden their horizons through non-academic experiences, such as attending conferences, workshops, regional career fairs, and other professional development events. These opportunities allow students to build leadership skills, network with professionals, and enhance their future career prospects.

Inspired by the extraordinary legacy of Jack A. McKenzie, this endowment reflects his deep commitment to leadership, service, and community engagement. A proud Clemson alumnus from the class of 1976, Jack A. McKenzie exemplified the core values of Alpha Phi Omega through his decades-long service to both the organization and his community. He served as the National President of Alpha Phi Omega, where he made significant contributions to the mission of fostering leadership, friendship, and service.

In addition to his leadership within Alpha Phi Omega, McKenzie was a dedicated employee of Clemson University, where he worked tirelessly to support student success. His remarkable dedication to service reached beyond the university, impacting local, state, regional, and national communities. This endowment is a lasting tribute to his passion for helping others reach their full potential through service and professional development.

By providing crucial financial assistance to students, the Alpha Phi Omega Jack A. McKenzie '76 Leadership Endowment continues his legacy of leadership and service, ensuring that future generations of Clemson Tigers are equipped to lead and make a meaningful impact in their fields.
summary: The Alpha Phi Omega Jack A. McKenzie ’76 Leadership Endowment is a travel grant-in-aid scholarship for students seeking financial assistance for professional development events. Open to undergraduate and graduate students.

name: Women in Architecture
summary: We aim to create a safe place for women to collaborate, network and support one another in the field of architecture.

name: Catan Club
description:
Join us in exploring the exciting world of Settlers of Catan! Whether you’re a seasoned Catan strategist or a curious newbie, our club offers a welcoming space to engage in friendly competition, master new strategies, and share your passion for board games.

 

Join our Groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/102447295/cYy0pc8b
summary: Join us in exploring the exciting world of Settlers of Catan! Whether you’re a seasoned Catan strategist or a curious newbie, our club offers a welcoming space to engage in friendly competition, master new strategies, and share your passion!

name: Students for Stewardship
description:
Are you passionate about the environment and eager to make a tangible impact on our local ecosystems? Join Students for Stewardship, Clemson University's premier club dedicated to fostering a culture of sustainability, stewardship, and community engagement. Our mission is to empower students to actively participate in the care and restoration of natural spaces, starting with the Hunnicutt Food Forest—a unique project right here on campus.

As a member, you'll have the opportunity to get hands-on experience in regenerative agriculture, ecological restoration, and sustainable land management. Whether you're planting fruit trees, cultivating mushrooms, or building biochar systems, every activity is designed to enhance the health of our environment while providing valuable learning opportunities. Our club is also a hub for collaboration, where students from diverse disciplines come together to innovate and implement solutions that benefit both our campus and the broader community.

Beyond the Hunnicutt Food Forest, we engage in projects that support the local water cycle, improve stream and riparian health, and promote biodiversity. We also host workshops, volunteer events, and social activities that connect like-minded individuals who are passionate about making a difference.

Join us in regenerating upstate South Carolina and become part of a movement that’s not only about protecting the environment but also about creating a thriving, sustainable future. Together, we can make Clemson greener, healthier, and more resilient.
summary: Join us in restoring Clemson’s environment! We focus on the Hunnicutt Food Forest, where you'll gain hands-on experience in sustainability. Connect with others and help make our campus greener. Let's Regenerate together!

name: Clemson Federalist Society
description: The Federalist Society is a group of conservatives and libertarians interested in the current state of the legal order. It is founded on the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our Constitution, and that it is emphatically the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is, not what it should be. The Clemson chapter seeks both to promote an awareness of these principles and to further their application through lectures with guest speakers and chapter meetings.
summary: The Federalist Society provides a forum for high quality debate, discussion, and discourse on various legal issues.

name: Stay Here
description:
Stay Here is an organization with the mission to help put an end to suicide. We provide yearly suicide prevention training, as well as a safe space on campus to be able to discuss mental health worries without worrying about any negative stigma that may surround the topic otherwise. 

Join our GroupMe! GroupMe Link
summary: Stay Here dedicated to helping Gen Z be suicide free. We want to spread awareness about mental health and help students be able to take the next steps to preventing suicide on campus, as well as provide a safe space to talk.

name: Clemson Cars and Coffee
summary: Clemson Cars and Coffee is designed to bring together car enthusiasts from within the university and the local community to host car gatherings and meets.

name: Clemson University Constructors Guild
description: An umbrella organization for all the clubs with the Construction Science Department. The Guild serves as a liaison between the faculty/industry and the students.
summary: An umbrella organization for all the clubs with the Construction Science Department. The Guild serves as a liaison between the faculty/industry and the students.

name: Clemson Survivor Fans
description: The Clemson Survivor Fans club, is an organization where Clemson students get together to watch and discuss the hit CBS reality TV show, Survivor. In addition to hosting watch parties for weekly episodes of the show, the organization will host a variety of events that are Survivor related, including a shortened Survivor competition that club members and students can compete to earn the title of Clemson Sole Survivor!
summary: A club where students at Clemson get together to watch and discuss the hit CBS reality show, Survivor. We also host a shortened Survivor Competition where club members and Clemson students can compete for the title of Clemson Sole Survivor!

name: Preventing Child Abuse Club
description: Bringing awareness to the dangers of child abuse while teaching others about how to spot signs of abuse. Our club members strive to advocate everyday for child abuse victims and helping the world become a better place for the children that grow up to be our future.
summary: Bringing awareness to the dangers of child abuse while teaching others about how to spot signs of abuse.

name: Clemson Fusion
description:
The first all-inclusive multicultural club founded at Clemson University, bringing together students from diverse backgrounds to create a vibrant, welcoming community.

Our mission is to celebrate and share the rich tapestry of global cultures through the exploration of traditions, holidays, foods, music, and more at a personal level.

At Clemson Fusion, we believe in the power of cultural exchange and understanding. We host events and workshops where members can learn about and experience different cultures firsthand, from cooking traditional dishes and celebrating global holidays to exploring the music and arts of various regions. Our community is a place where everyone is encouraged to share their heritage and learn from others, fostering mutual respect and a deeper appreciation for the world’s cultural diversity.

Whether you're looking to connect with others, learn something new, or share your own culture, Clemson Fusion is the perfect place.
summary: Clemson Fusion's mission aims to foster cultural awareness and inclusivity within the Clemson University community by celebrating diverse traditions, holidays, and customs from around the world.

name: Clemson Strikers
summary: Clemson Strikers is a fun club for students that like to bowl! We meet once a week and bowl 3 games at Boulevard Lanes. If you like to bowl and want to make some friends, this club is for you!

name: Women Who Worship at Clemson
description: This organization is open to all women. While its culture is inspired by the Southern African American Christian Church.
summary: Focuses on fostering a community with women to help them incorporate God into their daily lives. We aim to create an environment inspired by the African American Christian Church for those students struggling to find that environment here at Clemson.

name: Clemson Para Soccer
description:
Clemson Para Soccer is a competitive adaptive soccer program for student athletes with cerebral palsy, stroke, and traumatic brain injury. 

Players without these disabilities are invited to join the program to play as volunteers.
summary: Competitive adaptive soccer for student athletes with cerebral palsy, stroke, and traumatic brain injury.

name: Clemson Hip- Hop Club: Lifes Looking Good:
description:
The Clemson Hip Hop Club is a vibrant social community dedicated to fostering creativity and innovation, encouraging individuals to break boundaries and unleash their full potential—regardless of their background or circumstances. Inspired by the pioneers of hip-hop, we aim to influence and empower people to take bold leaps of faith and embrace their unique voices.

Our club hosts meetings to delve into historical moments in hip-hop, celebrate diverse histories, and explore the vital role diverse communities played in shaping this influential culture. We are committed to supporting other clubs, communities, and the Divine Nine, fostering a spirit of unity and collaboration.

Through the teachings of hip-hop, we aim to show that being different is not only cool but also a powerful force for change. The HHC will explore the art, history, and culture of hip-hop, and learn how to make a lasting impact in our own way.
summary: The Clemson Hip Hop Club is a vibrant social community dedicated to fostering creativity and innovation, encouraging individuals to break boundaries and unleash their full potential—regardless of their background or circumstances.

name: Clemson Lego Club
summary: A club for any and all LEGO fans and builders.

name: Clemson Society for Health Psychology
description: The CSHP Student Chapter is dedicated to promoting and advancing the profession of health psychology. Our mission is to explore how biological, social, and psychological factors interact to influence health and illness. Through education, research, and community engagement, we aim to foster a deeper awareness of these factors' critical role in overall well-being. The Society serves as a platform for members to share knowledge, enhance skills for future psychologists, and promote well-being through community-based activities.
summary: The CSHP Student Chapter is dedicated to promoting and advancing the profession of health psychology. Our mission is to explore how biological, social, and psychological factors interact to influence health and illness.

name: Clemson Line Dancing Club
description:
The Clemson Line Dancing Club is a social organization that welcomes line dancers of all skill levels. Members meet regularly to learn and practice various line dances, from classic country steps to more modern and creative routines. The club provides a great way to stay active, meet new people, and enjoy the spirit of community at Clemson. Whether you are an experienced dancer or a complete beginner, the club offers a supportive environment for all to improve their dancing skills while having a great time.

 

In addition to regular practice sessions, the Clemson Line Dancing Club hosts various social events throughout the year. These include themed dance nights, collaborations with other student organizations, and participation in other events around campus. The club also organizes fundraising events and supports students on campus through community engagement. Overall, the Clemson Line Dancing Club offers a dynamic range of activities that allow students to stay engaged and immersed in the Clemson experience!
summary: Howdy! Welcome to Clemson Line Dancing Club, we are a social club for dancers of all skill levels! We teach and learn songs from all artist and genres from Pitbull to Luke Combs. So grab your boots and dancing partner and come check us out!

name: Clemson University Literacy Leaders Student Association
description: The Literacy Leadership Student Association is dedicated to empowering undergraduate and graduate students across all programs by providing access to essential literacy resources and information. We aim to meet the diverse needs and interests of our members by facilitating access to conferences, webinars, and workshops on current literacy issues. Through mentorship programs, we foster personal and professional growth, ensuring that our members are well-equipped to contribute to the evolving field of literacy. Our commitment is to be a supportive and dynamic resource, cultivating a community of informed and passionate literacy advocates.
summary: The Literacy Leadership Student Association is dedicated to empowering undergraduate and graduate students across all programs by providing access to essential literacy resources and information.

name: Student Organ Donation Advocates
description:
SODA is a national nonprofit organization, founded in 2014 at Washington University in order to make a difference in organ donation education and registration. As an organization, SODA national is heavily involved in the activities and success of its chapters, which creates a unique and exciting opportunity of students on campus who want to play and active role in saving lives on campus!

The club encourages students across a wide variety of fields and interests and majors to come together and make a difference using their strengths and passion- students will have the opportunity to engage in a variety of events geared towards education and advocacy during the semester, as well as ample opportunities for leadership.

Whether you are a pre-med student looking to make a real difference in patients lives, a political science major excited to educate and inform the public about the truth of organ donation, or just someone who is just as passionate about organ donation as we are, this club will give you the resources and support to do just that!
summary: SODA is a national nonprofit with chapters across the nation, dedicated to the education of students on the facts of organ donation and registration of new donors!

name: Clemson Archery Club
description: Clemson Archery Club is designed to increase interest in archery and create a space where archers can have a community of other people who also enjoy archery. All skill levels are welcomed, as there will be instruction for those who are just starting, as well as local or even national competitions for those who are more advanced. Bring your own equipment, or borrow club equipment.
summary: Clemson Archery Club is an organization for any student interested in archery regardless of skill level. Its goal is to increase interest in archery and create a community where Clemson archers can come together and do what they love.

name: Clemson Brazilian Student Association
description:
BRASA is a student organization focused on further integrating the Brazilian community at Clemson University. Not a Brazilian? No problem! We are focused on creating a warm environment for all on campus!

Join our GroupMe to stay updated! https://groupme.com/join_group/103883678/3sYfJxnN
summary: BRASA is a student organization focused on further integrating the Brazilian community at Clemson University. Not a Brazilian? No problem! We are focused on creating a warm environment for all on campus!

name: Clemson Students for Mental Health
summary: Clemson students come together as we do fun activities to release mental stress, as well as outreach programs and education to end mental health stigma. "Keep looking where the light pours in" -Morgan Harper Nicoles.

name: Leukemia and Lymphoma Society On Campus at Clemson University
description: LLS On Campus at Clemson is an organization focused on fundraising and outreach efforts for patients with blood cancers.
summary: This organization brings the ideals of the Leukemia and Lymphoma Society (now Blood Cancer U) to Clemson's campus! Many schools have already seen the great impact of fundraising for patients, new treatments, and outreach through their university's branch

name: Dream Now
description:
Dream Now is a STEAM outreach organization in partnership with the Dream Center of Easley, South Carolina, dedicated to expanding educational access and opportunity for children and families in the community. Through this collaboration, Clemson University students lead engaging, hands-on classes that introduce foundational principles of science, technology, engineering, art, and mathematics. These interactive learning experiences are designed to spark curiosity, encourage creativity, and foster critical thinking in an inclusive and supportive environment.

Dream Now’s programming extends beyond academic enrichment by intentionally supporting families as a whole. Community members are able to directly benefit from Dream Now events simply by attending and participating in the activities offered. Families earn Dream Dollars through their involvement, which can then be used to purchase essential items for themselves and their children at the Dream Center’s retail stores. This model reinforces the value of education while simultaneously addressing practical needs.

By connecting university students with local families, Dream Now creates a meaningful exchange of knowledge, service, and empowerment. Children gain exposure to STEAM concepts in an accessible and engaging way, while Clemson students develop leadership, communication, and teaching skills through real-world service. Ultimately, Dream Now serves as a bridge between education and community support, promoting both learning and well-being while strengthening connections within Easley and beyond.
summary: Dream Now is a STEAM outreach organization partnered with the Dream Center of Easley, SC. Clemson University students provide engaging, hands-on instruction in science, technology, engineering, art, and math for children and families.

name: Doctors Without Borders
description: Our chapter is committed to: -Educating peers and community members about MSF’s work and its foundational humanitarian principles: neutrality, impartiality, independence, medical ethics, bearing witness, transparency, accountability, and inclusion. -Fundraising and supporting MSF’s field operations to ensure that aid reaches those most in need, irrespective of race, religion, gender, or political affiliation. -Advocating and bearing witness, speaking out on neglected crises or systemic barriers to healthcare access, while upholding MSF’s commitment to “do no harm.” -Acting with accountability and transparency, regularly evaluating our efforts, respecting the dignity of all communities, and adhering to MSF’s standards of diversity and inclusion. -Partner with local organizations aligned with MSF’s mission (e.g. refugee health groups, disaster preparedness networks). -Send at least one representative to two virtual MSF events yearly (e.g. fall leadership summit, spring closing session).
summary: Our chapter is committed to: -Educating peers and community members about MSF’s work and its foundational humanitarian principles: neutrality, impartiality, independence, medical ethics, bearing witness, transparency, accountability, and inclusion.

name: Italian Conversation and Cultural Club
summary: A group dedicated to talking about Italian culture in Italian, and giving students confidence to speak in the language!

name: Every Campus a Refuge with Global Refuge
description: As a student Org., ECAR will focus on creating community engagement and raising awareness about our mission. Students will plan campus events and volunteer with our families through ESL classes and other projects.
summary: ECAR's mission is to create new experiential learning opportunities to work with refugee newcomers in the Clemson Community and aid the local resettlement agency responsible for providing core services to refugee clients.

name: Student Organization Resource Library
description:
This page will serve as a hub for student organization resources! Please feel free to reach out to us at csle@clemson.edu if you have any questions, or if you believe there is a resource that our organization community could benefit from. 

 

Watch our current (Jan. 2026) RSO Officer Training Video!
summary: This page will serve as the Resource Library for our Student Organizations and Advisors at Clemson University!

name: 180 Degrees Consulting at Clemson University
description:
180 Degrees Consulting at Clemson University is a student-led organization that provides pro bono consulting services to nonprofits, social enterprises, and socially-minded businesses. As part of the world’s largest university-based consultancy, our branch enables students to apply critical thinking, research, and problem-solving skills to real-world challenges that create positive social impact.


Through interdisciplinary teams, members are split into small teams and work directly with clients over the course of a semester to deliver strategic recommendations in areas such as revenue generation, operations, organizational growth, and business strategy. Our model emphasizes professional development, client engagement, and collaborative teamwork.

Open to students of all majors and backgrounds, 180DC Clemson is designed to offer practical experience in consulting while fostering a commitment to meaningful, community-driven change.
summary: A student-led non-profit consulting group working on real projects with clients in the upstate

name: TigerFITT
description:
TigerFITT is a women-focused group fitness and wellness club that aims to empower women at Clemson University to embrace fitness through a supportive, student-run community that fosters strength, connection, and growth. We plan to host weekly workouts.

In addition, we intend to host social events and workshops, volunteer locally, and host local fundraisers and proceeds nights. Our goal is to make Clemson students more aware of health, fitness, and wellness and lower the stigma of it being difficult to start.

Want more information? Reach out to us through email at tigerfittcu@gmail.com or Instagram @tigerfittcu!
summary: TigerFITT is a women-focused group fitness and wellness club that aims to empower women at Clemson University to embrace fitness through a supportive, student-run community that fosters strength, connection, and growth. We plan to host weekly workouts.

name: Kappa Delta Pi
description: Through Kappa Delta Pi, we would like to offer education majors opportunities on a local and national level, build a community within the College of Education, provide guest speakers and professional development, and serve Clemson and surrounding communities. Our organization has three main philanthropies. Room to Read, Adopt a Classroom, and Children's Mental Health. Throughout the school year, we would hold events to support our fiscal philanthropic goals and raise awareness about different topics. Beyond the professional and service-based levels, we want to offer a welcoming community for like-minded people who are going to inspire future generations. Making these connections and feeling supported by your peers is important. We hope that this organization can become a major strength to the College of Education at Clemson University.
summary: Kappa Delta Pi's national mission is to "inspire and equip teachers to thrive." This organization is focused on those who plan to work in the education field and how to support them in college and beyond and give back to the community.

name: Love Fellowship Clemson
description: We are an inter-denominational fellowship, embracing Christians who share the same doctrinal belief irrespective of their denominations and affiliations. We have a divine mandate and mission to meet the needs of our neighbors by being the mouth, ears, hands, and feet for Jesus. These needs include (but not limited to) spiritual, academic, professional, financial, physical, mental, and social needs. We will regularly organize professional development seminars and attend regional conferences for spiritual, academic and professional advancement.
summary: We are an inter-denominational fellowship, embracing Christians who share the same doctrinal belief irrespective of their denominations and affiliations. We have a divine mandate and mission to meet the needs of our neighbors by being the mouth, ears, ha

name: Tigers Be Well
summary: Tigers Be Well is a club that focuses on improving the holistic wellness of Clemson students by creating a balance between academic success, physical wellbeing, and mental health.

name: Death Valley Steppas
description:
We're a diverse and vibrant community of trailriders/line dancers who come together to celebrate culture, connection, and build community. Whether you're veteran to the saddle or just learning your first steps, we welcome everyone with open arms. Our club is about more than just boots on the ground—it’s about sharing tradition, creating memories, and riding (dancing) together as one.

Please join the GroupMe 

Practices: 

Sunday 6pm in Sirrine 302

Tuesday 6pm in Humanities 366

Thursday 6pm in Humanities 366

All semester dues are DUE BY TBA
summary: We are a vibrant student organization committed to fostering a welcoming environment where students can immerse themselves in the rich traditions of Southern TrailRiding culture.

name: Plant Futures
description:
Plant Futures is a global movement at the intersection of climate justice, public health, social justice, and animal welfare.

Our work is proactively facilitating a time critical transformation for planetary sustainability, and ultimately, the survival of humanity.

Our program is creating a diverse, multidisciplinary talent pipeline for the global plant-rich food & ag sector by equipping students with the necessary education, tools, and networks to make an impact.
summary: Plant Futures, a project of the nonprofit Ideagarden, is a social venture committed to accelerating the transition to a plant-centric world for planetary, human and animal well being by equipping and empowering the next generation of food system leaders

name: Clemson Pre-Law Litigation Club
summary: The objectives of the Clemson Pre-Law Litigation Club are to develop a foundational understanding of litigation, business, and economics through hands-on experiences, educational workshops, and engagement with industry professionals.

name: Clemson Ruck Club
description: Clemson Ruck Club is a club dedicated to the military activity of rucking for the purpose of fitness and military heritage awareness. Rucking is the military related practice of packing a rucksack with weight and hiking for long distances, often in increments of 6-12-20 miles as preparation for field and combat readiness under strenuous conditions. It is an effective means of mental and physical conditioning. Membership is all-inclusive to current ROTC cadets, veterans, military enthusiasts, and normal civilians who enjoy a good challenge.
summary: Clemson Ruck Club is a club dedicated to the military activity of rucking for the purposes of fitness and military/veteran awareness.

name: Clemson EDM
description: We want to help promote people's passions. This is a great opportunity for marketing, sales, videography, photography, DJing, set production, audio engineering, as well as visual effects to showcase their skills to the Clemson community. With new festivals and events going on within and around Clemson, we want to hear about it all!
summary: A group that shares an interest in a wide EDM genre and create an EDM community. People who want to pursue DJing, lighting effects, marketing, visuals, and music production are highly encouraged! We want to be able to share our love for the music!

name: Clemson Mycology Club
description:
The Clemson Mycology Club is dedicated to exploring the diversity, ecology, and significance of fungi through engaging, hands-on experiences. Throughout the spring and fall semesters, the club hosts activities such as fungal inoculation workshops, guided trail walks, and geocaching/documentation efforts to discover and record local fungal species, including seasonal highlights such as bioluminescent fungi in the fall.

Members also have opportunities to connect with faculty and professionals in the field, including Dr. Julia Kerrigan, and to hear from guest speakers from across the country through the South Carolina Upstate Mycological Society (SCUMS), providing broader perspectives on fungal research, identification, and conservation.

Our mission is to encourage meaningful engagement with the natural world, promote learning beyond the classroom, and foster an appreciation for and stewardship of the environment. As the club grows, additional events, collaborations, and outreach opportunities will continue to be developed.
summary: The Clemson Mycology Club explores fungal diversity through hands-on activities, outdoor discovery, and educational events. Members learn from faculty and national speakers, promote environmental stewardship, and engage with fungi beyond the classroom.

name: Clemson Women's Club Ice Hockey
description: Our plan is to roster a team to compete in College Hockey South for the 2025-26 season and beyond.
summary: We are a student organization creating a safe, fun, and competitive environment for female athletes to enjoy the sport of women's ice hockey.

name: Clemson Cure Club
description: Cure Childhood Cancer is a non-profit organization that funds pediatric cancer patients and their families. CURE proudly funds and supports pediatric cancer research all throughout the country, now expanding to a college demographic. Through fundraisers such as 5K runs, raffles/silent auctions, and providing meals or goody bags for patients, CURE works to be interactive and hands on with local patients while benefiting research that strives to one day find a cure for cancer. The significance of bringing light to pediatric cancer patients in particular is the focal point of CURE's mission, and Clemson University is a beautiful platform to expand this cause. Those who are members of Clemson Cure Club will attend general body meetings to help effectively plan for fundraisers and brainstorm ideas for upcoming events as a group, with the goal of creating the best possible outcomes for patients and their families. Overall, Clemson Cure Club is an organization striving to benefit pediatric cancer patients and their families, and it is my goal to bring opportunities for involvement in organizations such as this to students at Clemson University.
summary: Clemson Cure Club is a non-profit organization funding pediatric cancer patients and their families. Through fundraising and various events, we provide college students with the opportunity to take part in CURE and make an impact on local patients.

name: Clemson MedTech Society
description:
Clemson MedTech Society is an independent student organization dedicated to bridging healthcare, business and engineering through hands on innovation and collaboration. We cultivate a community of matriculated Clemson and Bridge students who share a passion for medical technologies whether you’re a bioengineering major, nursing student, business analyst or simply curious about the next breakthrough device.

Each semester members engage in case study nights, prototyping workshops, regulatory deep dives and site visits to local hospitals and MedTech startups. We host pitch competitions, journal club discussions and speed networking sessions with industry professionals alumni and regulatory experts.
summary: To cultivate a collaborative environment where students innovate and develop medical technologies, bridging the gap between healthcare, business, and engineering.

name: Clemson Sports Networking
description: The Clemson Sports & Networking Club is a dynamic space where business-minded students, athletes, and sports enthusiasts come together to build lasting connections, gain industry insight, and bond over a shared love for sports. Our mission is to bridge the gap between passion and profession—whether you're aiming to work in the sports industry, expand your professional network, or just meet others who live for the game.
summary: The Clemson Sports & Networking Club unites business-driven students, athletes, and sports fans to network, explore careers in the sports industry, and connect over a shared passion for the game—bridging the gap between interest and opportunity.

name: Clemson Finance Club
summary: Assisting in the recruitment of Clemson’s financial management majors into full-time jobs after graduation. Our club invites employers to visit Clemson for informational sessions about various positions at their companies.

name: Athletes in Action
description:
Athletes in Action seeks to serve the Clemson athletic community by supporting through
character and life-skills coaching, mentoring, providing spaces for growth and encouragement,
and a presence in the lives of coaches and athletes. Our mission is to build spiritual
movements everywhere through the platform of sport so that everyone knows someone who
truly follows Jesus.
summary:
Athletes in Action seeks to serve the Clemson athletic community by supporting through
character and life-skills coaching, mentoring, providing spaces for growth and encouragement,
and a presence in the lives of coaches and athletes.

name: Spanish Language and Culture Club
description: Si estás trabajando para dominar el español o te interesa aprender más sobre él, ¡nos encantaría conocerte! Nos reunimos para compartir meriendas y una conversación agradable. A menudo nos reímos y también sacamos Word Reference, pero nuestro objetivo es crear una cultura de estímulo y camaradería. Por favor, pasa a hablar un poco de español.
summary: We are a community that meets together to speak entirely in Spanish. From beginners to near fluent speakers, our love of learning the Spanish language drives us to discuss serious and comedic topics, play games, and encourage each other as we learn.

name: Division of Research
summary: The Division of Research supports researchers at Clemson. We assist faculty in pursuing competitive research funding, managing awards, navigating complex regulation and compliance matters, collaborating with industry, and commercializing technologies.

name: The Orange and Regalia Fund
summary: The Orange and Regalia Fund is a student-managed investment fund focused on providing students with hands-on opportunities to improve their investment analysis skills. We are focused on long-term growth in both monetary and human capital.

name: Clemson Adventist Christian Fellowship
description: A bible based Christian fellowship whose purpose is to provide a holistic bible based Christian experience for Clemson University students. Furthermore we seek to provide a family experience where you can feel you have a family away from home. We will have weekly meetings, where we will have fellowship, share together, and then study the bible. We will also have monthly socials for our group to be able to share together even more and have fun.
summary: A bible based Christian fellowship whose purpose is to provide a holistic bible based Christian experience for Clemson University students. Furthermore we seek to provide a family experience where you can feel you have a family away from home.

name: Purpose Project
summary: Purpose Project is dedicated to inspiring students to find their purpose by building meaningful relationships while serving the Clemson community.

name: Clemson Global Dental Brigades
description: Clemson University is a chapter of Global Dental Brigades, an international movement of students and medical professionals working alongside local communities and staff to implement sustainable health systems. We work in remote, rural, and under resourced communities in Honduras, Panama, Nicaragua and Ghana who would otherwise have limited to no access to health care. Each community receives a brigade every 3 to 4 months where hundreds of patients are provided access to dental & medical care and volunteers deliver public health workshops. Electronic patient records are collected for future visitations and to monitor overall community health trends. In conjunction with our Medical & Dental Programs, Global Brigades also supports communities with economic development, sanitation and clean water projects, and uniquely implements these programs in a holistic model to meet a community’s health and economic goals. Our model systematically builds community ownership and collaboratively executes programs with the end goal of sustainably evolving to a relationship of impact monitoring.
summary: Clemson Global Dental Brigades is a student-led chapter of the international Global Brigades organization. This group brings together students and dental professionals to provide free dental care and education in underserved communities abroad.

name: Tigers Tillers and Keepers
summary: To create a community of Christian fellowship for students who desire to grow in the understanding and application of stewarding God’s creation as professionals in agricultural industries while embracing the mission of sowing seeds of the Gospel of Jesus

name: Clemson Mahjong Club
summary: Clemson Mahjong Club is a student-run organization that aims to share, play, and teach the game of mahjong. The club plays American Mahjong and uses sets donated by the community or brought in by members.

name: Clemson Cooking Club
description:
The CCC's mission is to make cooking approachable and exciting for everyone, whether you're just starting out or looking to master more advanced recipes, hoping to help break the stereotype of a college student sitting on their bed eating ramen noodles for the 4th night in a row. 

The CCC is committed to being flexible and student-centered. At the start of each month, we'll set a meeting schedule and adjust it as needed based on member feedback, especially around busy times like exams or for scheduled breaks.

Members will pay club dues each semester to help cover the cost of ingredients, materials, and special events. Dues will be calculated during the first two weeks of the semester, depending on the size of the club and a finalized recipe/ingredient list. With this in mind, recipe lists will be prepared in the Spring semester for fund requests through the Student Funding Board for the following semesters. These dues ensure we can have a one-time fee to supplement activities for the rest of the semester and keep the proportions a reasonable size.

Ultimately, CCC is about learning practical skills, trying new foods, and building a supportive community around the shared love of food.
summary: The Clemson Cooking Club teaches recipes from the basics to advanced dishes, covers budget-friendly and healthy shopping, and highlights global cuisines monthly, bringing students together through food, culture, and skills.

name: Undergraduate Advancement into Postgraduate Education
summary: Our mission is to empower underrepresented individuals and provide the necessary resources, information, and guidance to support their pathway into postgraduate education.

name: Critical Research Initiative
summary: The Critical Research Initiative focuses on in service learning for incarcerated individuals residing in correctional facilities nearby the Clemson campus.  This organization also investigates and presents research on the overall topic of incarceration.

name: Tension Release Engineered Ballistics
description:
Tension Release Engineered Ballistics is a club team built specifically for Engineering and Physics students who are looking for hands on experience with projectile motion machines and want to get familiar with working in teams to complete projects and execute designs. We also compete typically once a year against teams from other schools, so if you find that interesting we have that too! the club meets once a week on campus for design days, then once a design is settled we switch to build days.

If you're interested, join the groupme and/or follow the instagram:

https://groupme.com/join_group/105219350/JpOA99SK

https://www.instagram.com/clemson_treb/
summary: Tension Release Engineered Ballistics is a club team built specifically for Engineering and Physics students who are looking for hands on experience with projectile motion machines and want to get familiar with working in teams to complete projects.

name: Paw Packs
